96	## Module map
97	
98	CLI layer:
99	
100	- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge:
101	  - `pub struct Cli` — clap `Parser`.
102	  - `pub enum Commands` — clap `Subcommand`; matched in the
103	    dispatch arms.
104	  - `cli_with_banner()` — walks the command tree adding the
105	    `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as `before_help` on every subcommand.
106	  - `CompleteEnv` wiring for dynamic completion.
107	  - `bm_track` — a permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
108	    diagnostic printed on entry/exit of every command.
109	
110	- `src/options_flags/` — options/flags shared across
111	  subcommands, so a flag is defined once (parser, help,
112	  completer); Migration B tracks what's been moved here so
113	  far. See
114	  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
115	  - reusable **leaves**: `account`, `config`, `dry_run`,
116	    `private`, `push_retry`, `repo`, `scope`, `squash`,
117	    `use_template`.
118	  - reusable **bundles**: `provision_bundle`.
119	- `src/common.rs` — `CommonArgs` (the shared positional-rev /
120	  `-R` repo-list / `-n` / `--limit` / `-L` flag set flattened
121	  by `chid`, `desc`, `list`, `show`); `CommonParams`, its
122	  clap-free counterpart (resolved `DotSpec` + `Header` + repos,
123	  built `From<&CommonArgs>`); plus the repo-iteration and
124	  revision-resolution helpers they share. The 0.49.0 CommonArgs
125	  sweep relocates `CommonArgs` to `src/options_flags/` (-5) and
126	  swaps `-R` for `-s`/`--scope` (-6); the helpers stay here.
127	
128	Subcommand layer scaffolding:
129	
130	- `src/context.rs` — `Context`: the shared platform handle
131	  every subcommand runs against. Today it carries the loaded
132	  `UserConfig` and the `--log` path; built once at startup
133	  via `Context::load(log)`.
134	- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
135	- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
136	  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for
137	  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.
138	
139	Subcommand modules — each holds an `XxxArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
140	struct and a `pub fn x(...)` entrypoint:
141	
142	| Module | Subcommand | Notes |
143	| --- | --- | --- |
144	| `chid.rs` | `chid` | migrated (0.49.0-1) — `chid(&Context, &ChidParams)`, `From<&ChidArgs>`; flattens `CommonArgs` (→ `CommonParams`) |
145	| `desc.rs` (+ `desc_helpers.rs`) | `desc` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
146	| `list.rs` | `list` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
147	| `show.rs` | `show` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
148	| `validate_desc.rs` | `validate-desc` | migrated (0.48.0-4) — `validate_desc(&Context, &ValidateDescParams)`, `From<&ValidateDescArgs>` |
149	| `fix_desc.rs` | `fix-desc` | migrated (0.48.0-5) — `fix_desc(&Context, &FixDescParams)`, `From<&FixDescArgs>` |
150	| `clone.rs` | `clone` | migrated (0.48.0-2) — `clone_repo(&Context, &CloneParams)`, `From<&CloneArgs>` |
151	| `init.rs` (+ `init/params.rs`) | `init` | subcommand-layer worked example (0.44.0) |
152	| `symlink.rs` | `symlink` | migrated (0.48.0-1) — `symlink(&Context, &SymlinkParams)`, `From<&SymlinkArgs>` |
153	| `sync.rs` | `sync` | migrated (0.48.0-3) — `sync(&Context, &SyncParams)`, `From<&SyncArgs>` |
154	| `finalize.rs` | `finalize` | migrated (0.46.0) — `finalize(&Context, &FinalizeParams)`, `TryFrom<&FinalizeArgs>` |
155	| `push.rs` | `push` | resumable state machine; migrated (0.48.0-6) — `push(&Context, &PushParams)`, `From<&PushArgs>` |
156	
157	Support:
158	
159	- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
160	  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
161	  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
162	- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
163	  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures (`Fixture` / `FixturePor`) and
164	  tempdir resolution. Subcommand modules also carry their
165	  own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and `integration_tests` for
166	  `sync` / `push`).
167	
168	## Subcommand model
169	
170	Adding a subcommand `x`:
171	
172	1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XxxArgs`
173	   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
174	   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
175	2. Add a variant `X(x::XxxArgs)` to `Commands` in
176	   `src/main.rs` with a `///` summary (and
177	   `#[command(long_about = …)]` for the long form).
178	3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
179	   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
180	4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
181	   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
182	   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
183	   prose).
184	5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
185	   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
186	   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
187	   and stays at this layer.
188	
189	## Migration A — args → Context + Params
190	
191	Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` to
192	`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`, adding an
193	`XxxParams` flat struct + a `From` (or `TryFrom`, if the
194	conversion is fallible) at the binary edge. `init` (0.44.0) is
195	the worked example; `finalize` (0.46.0) added the `TryFrom` /
196	`Context.log` variant; the `0.48.0` cycle swept the standalone
197	subcommands; the `0.49.0` cycle is the "CommonArgs sweep" for
198	the four `CommonArgs`-flattening ones — Migration A (`chid` -1,
199	`desc` -2, `list` -3, `show` -4; these embed a shared clap-free
200	`CommonParams`), then Migration B (`CommonArgs` → `options_flags/`
201	leaf-bundle, -5) and the `--scope` rollout (`-R`/`--repo` →
202	`-s`/`--scope`, -6) — see the Migration B section below and
203	`notes/chores-09.md`. The live checklist is the "Subcommand
204	layer / CLI decoupling" item in [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).
205	
206	| Subcommand | Status |
207	| --- | --- |
208	| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)`; `From<&InitArgs>` |
209	| `finalize` | done (0.46.0) — `finalize(&Context, &FinalizeParams)`; `TryFrom<&FinalizeArgs>` (fallible boundary); `--log` moved onto `Context` |
210	| `symlink` | done (0.48.0-1) — `symlink(&Context, &SymlinkParams)`; `From<&SymlinkArgs>` |
211	| `clone` | done (0.48.0-2) — `clone_repo(&Context, &CloneParams)`; `From<&CloneArgs>` |
212	| `sync` | done (0.48.0-3) — `sync(&Context, &SyncParams)`; `From<&SyncArgs>` |
213	| `validate-desc` | done (0.48.0-4) — `validate_desc(&Context, &ValidateDescParams)`; `From<&ValidateDescArgs>` |
214	| `fix-desc` | done (0.48.0-5) — `fix_desc(&Context, &FixDescParams)`; `From<&FixDescArgs>` |
215	| `push` | done (0.48.0-6) — `push(&Context, &PushParams)`; `From<&PushArgs>` (collapses the two bookmark spellings) |
216	| `chid` | done (0.49.0-1) — `chid(&Context, &ChidParams)`; `From<&ChidArgs>` (wraps `CommonParams`). Migration B + `--scope` for `CommonArgs` land in 0.49.0-5/-6. |
217	| `desc` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-2) — the 0.49.0 CommonArgs sweep |
218	| `list` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-3) — the 0.49.0 CommonArgs sweep |
219	| `show` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-4) — the 0.49.0 CommonArgs sweep (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse) |
220	
221	Out of scope for these ports (deferred until a real consumer
222	surfaces): typed errors, returned outcomes vs `println!`, the
223	`ProgressSink`, and `Context` fields beyond `UserConfig` + the
224	`--log` path (`finalize` surfaced `log`).
225	
226	## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`
227	
228	Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
229	fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
230	`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
231	completer) exactly once. See
232	[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
233	for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
234	Flag-vs-Option classification.
235	
236	State today:
237	
238	- **`init`** — fully composed from leaves/bundles
239	  (`account`, `repo`, `scope`, the `provision` bundle,
240	  `use_template`, `config`).
241	- **`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`** — share
242	  `common::CommonArgs`, a per-domain shared struct rather
243	  than an `options_flags/` leaf. The 0.49.0 "CommonArgs
244	  sweep" cycle (in flight) folds these into the leaf model:
245	  0.49.0-5 decomposes `CommonArgs` into `options_flags/`
246	  leaves (`revision`, the `-n` commit limit, the `-l`/`-L`
247	  label pair, the two positionals) and relocates the bundle
248	  to `src/options_flags/common_bundle.rs`; 0.49.0-6 replaces
249	  the repeatable `-R`/`--repo` with `-s`/`--scope`
250	  (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>` — wiring up `scope.rs`'s
251	  `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single`, today `#[allow(dead_code)]`).
252	- **`finalize`** — `--squash` lifted to the `squash` leaf
253	  (which carries the `value`-field naming convention — see
254	  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
255	  "Adding a new leaf"). `--delay` / `--detach` / `--exec` /

file.content:
## Module map

CLI layer:

- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge:
  - `pub struct Cli` — clap `Parser`.
  - `pub enum Commands` — clap `Subcommand`; matched in the
    dispatch arms.
  - `cli_with_banner()` — walks the command tree adding the
    `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as `before_help` on every subcommand.
  - `CompleteEnv` wiring for dynamic completion.
  - `bm_track` — a permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
    diagnostic printed on entry/exit of every command.

- `src/options_flags/` — options/flags shared across
  subcommands, so a flag is defined once (parser, help,
  completer); Migration B tracks what's been moved here so
  far. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
  - reusable **leaves**: `account`, `config`, `dry_run`,
    `private`, `push_retry`, `repo`, `scope`, `squash`,
    `use_template`.
  - reusable **bundles**: `provision_bundle`.
- `src/common.rs` — `CommonArgs` (the shared positional-rev /
  `-R` repo-list / `-n` / `--limit` / `-L` flag set flattened
  by `chid`, `desc`, `list`, `show`); `CommonParams`, its
  clap-free counterpart (resolved `DotSpec` + `Header` + repos,
  built `From<&CommonArgs>`); plus the repo-iteration and
  revision-resolution helpers they share. The 0.49.0 CommonArgs
  sweep relocates `CommonArgs` to `src/options_flags/` (-5) and
  swaps `-R` for `-s`/`--scope` (-6); the helpers stay here.

Subcommand layer scaffolding:

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`: the shared platform handle
  every subcommand runs against. Today it carries the loaded
  `UserConfig` and the `--log` path; built once at startup
  via `Context::load(log)`.
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

Subcommand modules — each holds an `XxxArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
struct and a `pub fn x(...)` entrypoint:

| Module | Subcommand | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `chid.rs` | `chid` | migrated (0.49.0-1) — `chid(&Context, &ChidParams)`, `From<&ChidArgs>`; flattens `CommonArgs` (→ `CommonParams`) |
| `desc.rs` (+ `desc_helpers.rs`) | `desc` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `list.rs` | `list` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `show.rs` | `show` | flattens `CommonArgs` |
| `validate_desc.rs` | `validate-desc` | migrated (0.48.0-4) — `validate_desc(&Context, &ValidateDescParams)`, `From<&ValidateDescArgs>` |
| `fix_desc.rs` | `fix-desc` | migrated (0.48.0-5) — `fix_desc(&Context, &FixDescParams)`, `From<&FixDescArgs>` |
| `clone.rs` | `clone` | migrated (0.48.0-2) — `clone_repo(&Context, &CloneParams)`, `From<&CloneArgs>` |
| `init.rs` (+ `init/params.rs`) | `init` | subcommand-layer worked example (0.44.0) |
| `symlink.rs` | `symlink` | migrated (0.48.0-1) — `symlink(&Context, &SymlinkParams)`, `From<&SymlinkArgs>` |
| `sync.rs` | `sync` | migrated (0.48.0-3) — `sync(&Context, &SyncParams)`, `From<&SyncArgs>` |
| `finalize.rs` | `finalize` | migrated (0.46.0) — `finalize(&Context, &FinalizeParams)`, `TryFrom<&FinalizeArgs>` |
| `push.rs` | `push` | resumable state machine; migrated (0.48.0-6) — `push(&Context, &PushParams)`, `From<&PushArgs>` |

Support:

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures (`Fixture` / `FixturePor`) and
  tempdir resolution. Subcommand modules also carry their
  own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and `integration_tests` for
  `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XxxArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XxxArgs)` to `Commands` in
   `src/main.rs` with a `///` summary (and
   `#[command(long_about = …)]` for the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.

## Migration A — args → Context + Params

Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` to
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`, adding an
`XxxParams` flat struct + a `From` (or `TryFrom`, if the
conversion is fallible) at the binary edge. `init` (0.44.0) is
the worked example; `finalize` (0.46.0) added the `TryFrom` /
`Context.log` variant; the `0.48.0` cycle swept the standalone
subcommands; the `0.49.0` cycle is the "CommonArgs sweep" for
the four `CommonArgs`-flattening ones — Migration A (`chid` -1,
`desc` -2, `list` -3, `show` -4; these embed a shared clap-free
`CommonParams`), then Migration B (`CommonArgs` → `options_flags/`
leaf-bundle, -5) and the `--scope` rollout (`-R`/`--repo` →
`-s`/`--scope`, -6) — see the Migration B section below and
`notes/chores-09.md`. The live checklist is the "Subcommand
layer / CLI decoupling" item in [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).

| Subcommand | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)`; `From<&InitArgs>` |
| `finalize` | done (0.46.0) — `finalize(&Context, &FinalizeParams)`; `TryFrom<&FinalizeArgs>` (fallible boundary); `--log` moved onto `Context` |
| `symlink` | done (0.48.0-1) — `symlink(&Context, &SymlinkParams)`; `From<&SymlinkArgs>` |

## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification.

State today:

- **`init`** — fully composed from leaves/bundles
  (`account`, `repo`, `scope`, the `provision` bundle,
  `use_template`, `config`).
- **`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`** — share
  `common::CommonArgs`, a per-domain shared struct rather
  than an `options_flags/` leaf. The 0.49.0 "CommonArgs
  sweep" cycle (in flight) folds these into the leaf model:
  0.49.0-5 decomposes `CommonArgs` into `options_flags/`
  leaves (`revision`, the `-n` commit limit, the `-l`/`-L`
  label pair, the two positionals) and relocates the bundle
  to `src/options_flags/common_bundle.rs`; 0.49.0-6 replaces
  the repeatable `-R`/`--repo` with `-s`/`--scope`
  (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>` — wiring up `scope.rs`'s
  `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single`, today `#[allow(dead_code)]`).
- **`finalize`** — `--squash` lifted to the `squash` leaf
  (which carries the `value`-field naming convention — see
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  "Adding a new leaf"). `--delay` / `--detach` / `--exec` /

content.content:
947	## refactor: chid → Context + ChidParams (0.49.0-1)
948	
949	Step 1 of the cycle, and the point where the cycle is
950	re-scoped (see below). `chid` is the smallest of the four:
951	`#[command(flatten)]` of `CommonArgs`, body is `for_each_repo`
952	+ `collect_ids` + print. The port also introduces the shared
953	`CommonParams` the other three will reuse.
954	
955	- `common::CommonParams` (flat, clap-free): the resolved
956	  `DotSpec` + `Header` + raw `repos: Vec<PathBuf>`, with
957	  `impl From<&CommonArgs>` running `resolve_spec` (default rev
958	  `@`) and `resolve_header` at the binary edge — the `..`
959	  parsing and `-l`/`-L` resolution leave the op bodies.
960	- `ChidParams { common: CommonParams }` + `impl From<&ChidArgs>`;
961	  `chid(args)` → `chid(_ctx: &Context, params: &ChidParams)`,
962	  `ctx` the uniform-signature placeholder (as in symlink /
963	  validate-desc / fix-desc). `chid.rs` also gains the `//!`
964	  module docstring it was missing.
965	- `main.rs` `Chid` arm builds `Context::load(cli.log)` +
966	  `ChidParams::from`; `suppress_banner` still reads
967	  `a.common.no_label` off the args (clap edge).
968	
969	### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)
970	
971	The 0.49.0-0 plan opened a "finish Migration A" cycle for the
972	four `CommonArgs`-flattening subcommands. During 0.49.0-1
973	review the cycle was expanded to the full **"CommonArgs sweep"**
974	long planned in `chores-06` / `chores-07` — Migration A is
975	joined by Migration B and the `--scope` rollout for these four.
976	(The 0.49.0-0 commit and its chores section keep the original
977	narrow framing; this subsection is the record of the change of
978	plan.)
979	
980	- **Migration B** — decompose `CommonArgs`'s fields into
981	  `options_flags/` leaves (`revision`, the `-n` commit limit,
982	  the `-l`/`-L` label pair, the two positionals) and relocate
983	  the bundle to `src/options_flags/common_bundle.rs`. Pure
984	  refactor; the `From<&CommonArgs> for CommonParams` boundary

## refactor: chid → Context + ChidParams (0.49.0-1)

Step 1 of the cycle, and the point where the cycle is
re-scoped (see below). `chid` is the smallest of the four:
`#[command(flatten)]` of `CommonArgs`, body is `for_each_repo`
+ `collect_ids` + print. The port also introduces the shared
`CommonParams` the other three will reuse.

- `common::CommonParams` (flat, clap-free): the resolved
  `DotSpec` + `Header` + raw `repos: Vec<PathBuf>`, with
  `impl From<&CommonArgs>` running `resolve_spec` (default rev
  `@`) and `resolve_header` at the binary edge — the `..`
  parsing and `-l`/`-L` resolution leave the op bodies.
- `ChidParams { common: CommonParams }` + `impl From<&ChidArgs>`;
  `chid(args)` → `chid(_ctx: &Context, params: &ChidParams)`,
  `ctx` the uniform-signature placeholder (as in symlink /
  validate-desc / fix-desc). `chid.rs` also gains the `//!`
  module docstring it was missing.
- `main.rs` `Chid` arm builds `Context::load(cli.log)` +
  `ChidParams::from`; `suppress_banner` still reads
  `a.common.no_label` off the args (clap edge).

### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)

The 0.49.0-0 plan opened a "finish Migration A" cycle for the
four `CommonArgs`-flattening subcommands. During 0.49.0-1
review the cycle was expanded to the full **"CommonArgs sweep"**
long planned in `chores-06` / `chores-07` — Migration A is
joined by Migration B and the `--scope` rollout for these four.
(The 0.49.0-0 commit and its chores section keep the original
narrow framing; this subsection is the record of the change of
plan.)

- **Migration B** — decompose `CommonArgs`'s fields into
  `options_flags/` leaves (`revision`, the `-n` commit limit,
  the `-l`/`-L` label pair, the two positionals) and relocate
  the bundle to `src/options_flags/common_bundle.rs`. Pure
  refactor; the `From<&CommonArgs> for CommonParams` boundary
  is the only consumer that changes shape (reads `.value`).
- **`--scope`** — replace the repeatable `-R`/`--repo` with
  `-s`/`--scope` (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>`), wiring up
  `scope.rs`'s `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single` (both currently
  `#[allow(dead_code)]` — built in 0.42.0 for exactly this) and
  `common.rs`'s `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`. Per the
  0.42.0 capture (`chores-06` 0.41.0-4): `-R` is dropped, not
  aliased; arbitrary multi-repo (`-R . -R .claude`) is not
  preserved — `--scope=<path>` covers single-repo, the default
  is `default_scope()`. `for_each_repo` then takes an
  already-resolved `Vec<PathBuf>` (comma-expansion + `["."]`
  default move to the boundary), and all four `From`s become
  `TryFrom` (scope→repo resolution is fallible). `init`'s
  `--scope` (the separate `ScopeKind` `code,bot|por`) is left
  alone — reconciling the two vocabularies is the "por/dual
  parity" todo.

Revised ladder (supersedes the 0.49.0-0 one):

- 0.49.0-1 chid Migration A + `CommonParams` + this re-scope
- 0.49.0-2 desc Migration A
- 0.49.0-3 list Migration A
- 0.49.0-4 show Migration A (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse)
- 0.49.0-5 Migration B — `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/`
  leaf-bundle + relocate
- 0.49.0-6 `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope` (may split into
  `-6.N` sub-steps)
- 0.49.0 close-out — drop suffix, todo→Done (Migration A
  12/12 + CommonArgs sweep), README + ARCHITECTURE.md

input.new_string:
## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1)

Step 1 of the cycle (Migration B), and the point where the
cycle is re-scoped (see below). Decompose `common::CommonArgs`
into `options_flags/` leaves and relocate the bundle to
`src/options_flags/common_bundle.rs`, so the four read-only
commit-query subcommands flatten a proper leaf-bundle the way
`init` flattens `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle`. Pure refactor —
no behavior change; `vc-x1 chid -h` etc. are byte-identical.

- New leaves (modeled on `squash.rs`): `revision`
  (`RevisionOption`, `-r`/`--revision`, default `@`),
  `commit_limit` (`CommitLimitOption`, `-n`/`--commits`),
  `repo_label` (`RepoLabelOptions`, the coupled `-l`/`-L`
  pair). The two positionals (`REVISION` / `COMMITS`) and the
  `-R`/`--repo` repo list stay inline in the bundle — no second
  consumer would compose them independently, and `-R` is on the
  chopping block in 0.49.0-2.
- `CommonOptionFlagBundle` (`common_bundle.rs`,
  `OptionFlagBundle` marker) flattens the three leaves +
  inline `pos_rev` / `pos_count` / `repos`. `CommonArgs`
  removed from `common.rs` (which keeps the
  `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` / `resolve_*` / `format_*`
  helpers — those didn't move).
- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` flatten the bundle; their
  bodies read `c.revision.value` / `c.limit.value` /
  `c.repo_label.{label,no_label}`. `main.rs`'s `suppress_banner`
  match reads `a.common.repo_label.no_label`. All four `.rs`
  files also gain the `//!` module docstring they were missing.

### `value`-field clash on flattened leaves

`RevisionOption` and `CommitLimitOption` both follow the
single-field-named-`value` convention
([`options_flags/README.md`](../src/options_flags/README.md)
"Adding a new leaf"). clap derives an arg's *id* from the field
name, so two `value` fields flattened into the same struct
collide — `clap_builder` panics at parse time ("Argument names
must be unique, but 'value' is in use by more than one
argument"). Fix: an explicit `#[arg(id = "revision")]` /
`#[arg(id = "commits")]` on each. The convention's worked
example (`finalize`'s `--squash`) never hit this because no
command had flattened two `value` leaves before — this is the
first; the README is updated to note the `id` requirement.

### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)

The 0.49.0-0 plan opened a "finish Migration A" cycle for the
four `CommonArgs`-flattening subcommands. During 0.49.0-1
review the cycle was expanded to the full **"CommonArgs sweep"**
long planned in `chores-06` / `chores-07` — Migration A is
joined by Migration B and the `--scope` rollout for these four,
and re-ordered B-first so the Migration A ports land once,
against the final `CommonArgs` shape. (The 0.49.0-0 commit and
its chores section keep the original narrow framing; this
subsection is the record of the change of plan.)

- **Migration B** (this step, 0.49.0-1) — decompose
  `CommonArgs` into `options_flags/` leaves + relocate the
  bundle. Done; see above.
- **`--scope`** (0.49.0-2) — replace the repeatable
  `-R`/`--repo` with `-s`/`--scope` (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>`),
  wiring up `scope.rs`'s `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single` (both
  `#[allow(dead_code)]` — built in 0.42.0 for exactly this) and
  `common.rs`'s `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`. Per the
  0.42.0 capture (`chores-06` 0.41.0-4): `-R` is dropped, not
  aliased; arbitrary multi-repo (`-R . -R .claude`) is not
  preserved — `--scope=<path>` covers single-repo, the default
  is `default_scope()`. `for_each_repo` then takes an
  already-resolved `Vec<PathBuf>` (comma-expansion + `["."]`
  default move to the boundary). `init`'s `--scope` (the
  separate `ScopeKind` `code,bot|por`) is left alone —
  reconciling the two vocabularies is the "por/dual parity"
  todo.
- **Migration A** (0.49.0-3..-6) — `XxxParams` +
  `fn x(&Context, &XxxParams)` ports against the post-B/-scope
  `CommonArgs`; introduces the shared clap-free `CommonParams`
  (resolved `DotSpec` + `Header` + repos). All four take a
  fallible `TryFrom<&XxxArgs>` (scope→repo resolution can
  error); `show` also parses `--files` into `FileLimit` at the
  boundary.

Revised ladder (supersedes the 0.49.0-0 one):

- 0.49.0-1 Migration B — `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/`
  leaf-bundle + relocate; this re-scope
- 0.49.0-2 `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope` (wire `parse_scope` /
  `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`; slim `for_each_repo`) —
  may split into `-2.N`
- 0.49.0-3 chid Migration A + introduce `CommonParams`
- 0.49.0-4 desc Migration A
- 0.49.0-5 list Migration A
- 0.49.0-6 show Migration A (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse)
- 0.49.0 close-out — drop suffix, todo→Done (Migration A
  12/12 + CommonArgs sweep), README + ARCHITECTURE.md

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# options_flags — Reusable CLI Options and Flags

Per-flag CLI surfaces shared across multiple subcommands. Each
shared option or flag (OF) lives in its own submodule so its
type, parser, and tests stay together.

This module is the CLI-layer leaf store for "Migration B" in
[`../../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../../ARCHITECTURE.md); see there for
how the leaves fit the wider clap-args / `Context`+`Params`
split.

## Architecture

Three composition patterns:

- **Leaf** — a `#[derive(Args)]` struct holding the flag(s),
  `value_parser`, default, and help text (via the field
  doc-comment). One `#[derive(Args)]` per logical OF — single
  flag or a small fixed pair (e.g. `push_retry`'s two fields).
  Help text aims to be generic enough for any reasonable
  consumer.
- **Bundle** — a `#[derive(Args)]` that flattens N leaves into a
  named role (e.g. `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle`). One
  `#[command(flatten)]` line at the consumer picks up the whole
  bundle.
- **Pattern A escape hatch** — when a consumer needs unique help
  text (or different defaults), it skips the leaf's flatten and
  inlines its own `#[arg(value_parser = …)]` field, reusing the
  leaf's typed value and parser.

## Flag vs Option — classify by domain, not wire syntax

Every leaf falls into one of two categories, picked by the value
domain it carries:

- **Flag** — boolean domain. Wire form may be presence/absence
  (`--dry-run`), explicit negation (`--no-dry-run`), or value
  (`--dry-run=true|false`). All three express a `bool`. Type
  name suffix: `*Flag`.
- **Option** — non-boolean domain (string, integer, enum, path,
  …). Wire form is always `--name=<value>` (or `--name <value>`).
  Type name suffix: `*Option` (or `*Options` for a multi-field
  leaf like `PushRetryOptions`).

Wire form is presentation; the category is the underlying domain.

## Adding a new leaf

1. Create `options_flags/<name>.rs` with a
   `#[derive(Args, Debug, Clone, Default)]` struct.
2. Pick the suffix per domain: `*Flag` (boolean) or `*Option(s)`
   (non-boolean).
3. Name the field(s):
   - **Single-field leaf** — call the field `value` (it holds the
     parsed value-side of the option) and declare the flag
     explicitly with `#[arg(long = "<flag>", …)]`. The consumer
     then reads `args.<leaf>.value` rather than doubling the flag
     name (`args.squash.value`, not `args.squash.squash`).
   - **Multi-field leaf** — descriptive per-field names
     (`push_retries`, `push_retry_delay`); clap derives each
     flag from its field name as usual.
   - The pre-existing single-field leaves (`scope`, `repo`,
     `dry_run`, `private`, `account`, `config`, `use_template`)
     predate this and still derive the flag from the field name;
     migrating them to `value` is tracked in
     [`../../notes/todo.md`](../../notes/todo.md).
4. Add `pub mod <name>;` to `mod.rs`.
5. If the leaf has explicit parsing logic, declare a unit-struct
   implementor of `FlagParser` (boolean domain) or `OptionParser`
   (non-boolean domain). Bare `Option<String>` leaves and
   presence/absence boolean leaves need no parser impl — clap
   handles them.
6. Add tests for any non-trivial parser/resolver logic.
7. Leaves do **not** implement a Bundle marker — bundles do.

## Consuming an OF

Default — flatten the leaf into your subcommand's `Args`:

```rust
#[derive(Args)]
pub struct MyArgs {
    #[command(flatten)]
    pub config: ConfigOption,
    // ...
}
```

Pattern A — when generic help doesn't fit:

```rust
#[derive(Args)]
pub struct MyArgs {
    /// My subcommand-specific help for --config.
    #[arg(long = "config", value_name = "none|PATH",
          verbatim_doc_comment)]
    pub config: Option<String>,
    // ...
}
```

A Pattern A consumer reuses the leaf's types (e.g. `ConfigKind`)
and parsers (e.g. `parse_config_kind`) but owns its own clap
attributes.

## Consumer function shape

Helper functions called by a subcommand body should accept the
relevant leaf type by reference rather than unpacking individual
fields at the call site:

```rust
// Multi-field leaf — pass the whole leaf
fn run_retry(cmd: &str, args: &[&str], cwd: &Path,
             retry: &PushRetryOptions) -> Result<…> { … }

run_retry("git", &["push", …], cwd, &args.provision.push_retry)?;
```

This wins on:
- Readability — `&args.provision.push_retry` reads as a single
  unit; the function body works with `retry.push_retries` (no
  leaf-name doubling because the parameter name is the consumer's
  choice).
- Argument count — every multi-field leaf collapses N args into
  one ref, so chained helpers don't accumulate
  `clippy::too_many_arguments` warnings.
- Future-proofing — extending a leaf with another field is a
  zero-touch change at every call site.

For **single-field leaves** (e.g. `SquashOption`, `DryRunFlag`,
`PrivateFlag`), direct read at the consumer site
(`args.squash.value`, or `args.provision.dry_run.dry_run` for the
pre-`value` leaves) is fine — wrapping the inner value in a
`&LeafType` parameter doesn't earn the indirection.

## Marker traits

Three Bundle markers — one per content classification. Bundles
implement exactly one. Leaves do **not** implement these markers
(their category lives in the type-name suffix).

- `FlagBundle: clap::Args` — pure-boolean bundle (every
  constituent leaf is a Flag). Rare in practice.
- `OptionBundle: clap::Args` — pure-non-boolean bundle (every
  constituent leaf is an Option).
- `OptionFlagBundle: clap::Args` — mixed bundle (constituents
  include both Flag and Option leaves). Most common in practice.

Two Parser traits — conditional contracts implemented only when
a leaf has explicit parsing logic (custom `value_parser`):

- `FlagParser` — boolean-domain parser. Implemented only for
  boolean leaves that take a value form (`--flag=true|false`).
  Presence/absence flags need no impl; clap parses directly.
- `OptionParser` — non-boolean-domain parser. Implemented only
  for leaves with custom parsing logic (e.g. `ScopeParser`,
  `RepoParser`); bare `Option<String>` leaves need no impl.

`FlagParser` and `OptionParser` share an identical method shape;
the difference is documentation (which value-domain the parser
targets).

All five traits are documentation-level — clap derive doesn't
see them; the discipline is that every leaf and bundle declares
the appropriate ones explicitly.

## Bundle marker discipline

Bundle markers aren't compiler-checked against contents. If a
bundle's leaf set shifts category — e.g. a pure-Flag bundle gains
an Option leaf — you must update the marker by hand
(`FlagBundle` → `OptionFlagBundle`). No lint, no failure; silent
drift if forgotten. Worth a glance at the marker line whenever a
bundle's `#[command(flatten)]` set changes.

## Pattern A worked example

`init`'s `--config` field originally carried init-specific help
("Only valid with `--scope=por`..."). It currently flattens
`ConfigOption` for the generic help; the `--scope=por` constraint
surfaces in `init`'s preflight error. If the help-text generality
becomes a usability problem, init will switch to Pattern A as the
worked example.

## Layout note

OFs currently sit as flat `<name>.rs` files alongside this
README. Bundle modules carry the `_bundle` suffix
(e.g. `provision_bundle.rs`) so the leaf/bundle split is visible
in the directory listing without opening files. If an individual
OF accumulates enough rationale, edge-case detail, or examples
to outgrow doc-comments, it will graduate to a `<name>/mod.rs` +
`<name>/README.md` subdirectory layout. Mechanical when needed;
not done preemptively.

+     collide (`clap_builder` panics at parse time). When that's
+     possible, add an explicit `#[arg(id = "<unique>")]` to each
+     — e.g. `revision`/`commit_limit` both carry one because the
flag from its field name as usual.

content.text:
`0.49.0-1` is staged — **Migration B: decompose `CommonArgs` into `options_flags/` leaves + relocate the bundle**, plus the cycle re-scope docs (B-first). Pure refactor; `vc-x1 chid/desc/list/show -h` are byte-identical.

**Code:**
- **New `options_flags/` files** — `revision.rs` (`RevisionOption`, `-r`/`--revision`, default `@`), `commit_limit.rs` (`CommitLimitOption`, `-n`/`--commits`), `repo_label.rs` (`RepoLabelOptions`, the coupled `-l`/`-L` pair), `common_bundle.rs` (`CommonOptionFlagBundle` — `OptionFlagBundle` marker — flattens the three leaves + inline `pos_rev`/`pos_count`/`repos`). `mod.rs` updated.
- **`src/common.rs`** — `CommonArgs` removed (moved to the bundle); the `for_each_repo`/`collect_ids`/`resolve_*`/`format_*` helpers stay. Dropped the now-unused `use clap::Args;`.
- **`src/chid.rs` / `desc.rs` / `list.rs` / `show.rs`** — flatten `CommonOptionFlagBundle`; bodies read `c.revision.value` / `c.limit.value` / `c.repo_label.{label,no_label}`; tests updated to match; all four gained the `//!` module docstring they were missing.
- **`src/main.rs`** — `suppress_banner` match reads `a.common.repo_label.no_label`.
- **One snag fixed:** `RevisionOption` and `CommitLimitOption` both use the `value` field-name convention, and clap derives the arg *id* from the field name → two `value`s flattened into one struct panic at parse time. Fix: explicit `#[arg(id = "revision")]` / `#[arg(id = "commits")]`. (First time two `value` leaves got flattened together.)

**Docs:**
- **`src/options_flags/README.md`** — "Adding a new leaf" notes the `#[arg(id = …)]` caveat for `value`-field leaves flattened together.
- **`ARCHITECTURE.md`** — module map: new leaves/bundle listed; `common.rs` entry rewritten (helpers only, `CommonArgs` moved); `scope.rs` entry notes `parse_scope`/`Single` are dead code until `0.49.0-2`; the four subcommand rows say "flattens `CommonOptionFlagBundle` (Migration A → 0.49.0-N)"; Migration A intro + table reflect the B-first reorder (B `-1`/`-2`, A `-3`..`-6`, all four `TryFrom`); Migration B "State today" bullet rewritten.
- **`notes/chores-09.md`** — the `0.49.0-1` section is now `## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1)`: the Migration B work, a `### value-field clash on flattened leaves` subsection (the `id` snag), and the `### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)` subsection updated to the B-first ladder.
- **`notes/todo.md`** — In Progress ladder reordered B-first; `0.49.0-1` = Migration B `(current)`.

Checks: `cargo fmt --check` / `clippy --all-targets -D warnings` clean / `cargo test` 360+5+4+5 pass / `cargo install --path . --locked` / `vc-x1 -V` → `0.49.0-1` / smoke `vc-x1 list -L @.. 2`, `vc-x1 show -h` look right.

(Note: the `params_from_args` test I'd added to chid.rs in the now-reverted Migration-A attempt is gone — it returns with the chid port at `0.49.0-3`. Lib test count is 360, was 361 pre-cycle... actually it was 360 before `0.49.0-0` too — net zero.)

Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title/body.

content: Goal: 0.49.0 "CommonArgs sweep" for chid/desc/list/show. Step 0.49.0-1 (Migration B — `CommonArgs` decomposed into options_flags leaves + relocated, plus cycle re-scope docs) is staged, all checks green, awaiting your review. Next: review, then I prep the commit title/body.

content.text:
Fair — `0.49.0-1` doing all four subcommands + removing `CommonArgs` + the full docs reframe in one commit is too much. Let me break it into sub-steps. Proposal — `0.49.0-1` decomposes into:

- **`0.49.0-1.1`** — create `options_flags/{revision, commit_limit, repo_label, common_bundle}.rs` + `mod.rs` + the README `#[arg(id=…)]` caveat; migrate **`chid`** to flatten `CommonOptionFlagBundle` (with the `id` fix). `desc`/`list`/`show` keep flattening `common::CommonArgs`; `CommonArgs` stays in `common.rs` (the two coexist). The cycle re-scope record + the sub-step ladder land here.
- **`0.49.0-1.2`** — migrate **`desc`**.
- **`0.49.0-1.3`** — migrate **`list`**.
- **`0.49.0-1.4`** — migrate **`show`**; nothing uses `common::CommonArgs` anymore → remove it from `common.rs`; finish the ARCHITECTURE.md / chores-09 docs for Migration B.
- **`0.49.0-1`** — close-out: squash the four into one `0.49.0-1` commit (or keep separate — decided then), then `vc-x1 push main` (two gates).

Per the sub-step protocol these `-1.N` are local jj commits committed as I finish each (no per-sub-step approval gate; you review the committed revision in your editor), and the push happens once, at the `0.49.0-1` close-out.

On your "no one uses it / just chid" question: I lean **chid uses it in `-1.1`** — otherwise the new leaves are unused and `clippy -D warnings` flags `dead_code`, forcing `#[allow(dead_code)]` placeholders that come right back off. Having chid consume it makes `-1.1` a clean working state.

What I'll do to the currently-staged work: roll `desc.rs`/`list.rs`/`show.rs`/`common.rs` back to original (keeping `chid.rs` + the new `options_flags/` files), fix `main.rs`'s `suppress_banner` to the mixed state, dial the ARCHITECTURE.md/chores-09 docs back to "chid migrated, rest pending", bump `Cargo.toml` to `0.49.0-1.1`, and commit that as `0.49.0-1.1`.

OK to proceed this way?

lastPrompt: You, took a gaint step, I'd like some smaller steps, I'm thinking create the option_flags/ changes and have no one use it or maybe just one subcommand use it, chid?

947	## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1)
948	
949	Step 1 of the cycle (Migration B), and the point where the
950	cycle is re-scoped (see below). Decompose `common::CommonArgs`
951	into `options_flags/` leaves and relocate the bundle to
952	`src/options_flags/common_bundle.rs`, so the four read-only
953	commit-query subcommands flatten a proper leaf-bundle the way
954	`init` flattens `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle`. Pure refactor —
955	no behavior change; `vc-x1 chid -h` etc. are byte-identical.
956	
957	- New leaves (modeled on `squash.rs`): `revision`
958	  (`RevisionOption`, `-r`/`--revision`, default `@`),
959	  `commit_limit` (`CommitLimitOption`, `-n`/`--commits`),
960	  `repo_label` (`RepoLabelOptions`, the coupled `-l`/`-L`
961	  pair). The two positionals (`REVISION` / `COMMITS`) and the
962	  `-R`/`--repo` repo list stay inline in the bundle — no second
963	  consumer would compose them independently, and `-R` is on the
964	  chopping block in 0.49.0-2.
965	- `CommonOptionFlagBundle` (`common_bundle.rs`,
966	  `OptionFlagBundle` marker) flattens the three leaves +
967	  inline `pos_rev` / `pos_count` / `repos`. `CommonArgs`
968	  removed from `common.rs` (which keeps the
969	  `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` / `resolve_*` / `format_*`
970	  helpers — those didn't move).
971	- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` flatten the bundle; their
972	  bodies read `c.revision.value` / `c.limit.value` /
973	  `c.repo_label.{label,no_label}`. `main.rs`'s `suppress_banner`
974	  match reads `a.common.repo_label.no_label`. All four `.rs`
975	  files also gain the `//!` module docstring they were missing.
976	
977	### `value`-field clash on flattened leaves
978	
979	`RevisionOption` and `CommitLimitOption` both follow the
980	single-field-named-`value` convention
981	([`options_flags/README.md`](../src/options_flags/README.md)
982	"Adding a new leaf"). clap derives an arg's *id* from the field
983	name, so two `value` fields flattened into the same struct
984	collide — `clap_builder` panics at parse time ("Argument names
985	must be unique, but 'value' is in use by more than one
986	argument"). Fix: an explicit `#[arg(id = "revision")]` /
987	`#[arg(id = "commits")]` on each. The convention's worked
988	example (`finalize`'s `--squash`) never hit this because no
989	command had flattened two `value` leaves before — this is the
990	first; the README is updated to note the `id` requirement.
991	
992	### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)
993	
994	The 0.49.0-0 plan opened a "finish Migration A" cycle for the
995	four `CommonArgs`-flattening subcommands. During 0.49.0-1
996	review the cycle was expanded to the full **"CommonArgs sweep"**
997	long planned in `chores-06` / `chores-07` — Migration A is
998	joined by Migration B and the `--scope` rollout for these four,
999	and re-ordered B-first so the Migration A ports land once,
1000	against the final `CommonArgs` shape. (The 0.49.0-0 commit and
1001	its chores section keep the original narrow framing; this
1002	subsection is the record of the change of plan.)
1003	
1004	- **Migration B** (this step, 0.49.0-1) — decompose
1005	  `CommonArgs` into `options_flags/` leaves + relocate the
1006	  bundle. Done; see above.
1007	- **`--scope`** (0.49.0-2) — replace the repeatable
1008	  `-R`/`--repo` with `-s`/`--scope` (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>`),
1009	  wiring up `scope.rs`'s `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single` (both
1010	  `#[allow(dead_code)]` — built in 0.42.0 for exactly this) and
1011	  `common.rs`'s `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`. Per the
1012	  0.42.0 capture (`chores-06` 0.41.0-4): `-R` is dropped, not
1013	  aliased; arbitrary multi-repo (`-R . -R .claude`) is not
1014	  preserved — `--scope=<path>` covers single-repo, the default
1015	  is `default_scope()`. `for_each_repo` then takes an
1016	  already-resolved `Vec<PathBuf>` (comma-expansion + `["."]`
1017	  default move to the boundary). `init`'s `--scope` (the
1018	  separate `ScopeKind` `code,bot|por`) is left alone —
1019	  reconciling the two vocabularies is the "por/dual parity"
1020	  todo.
1021	- **Migration A** (0.49.0-3..-6) — `XxxParams` +
1022	  `fn x(&Context, &XxxParams)` ports against the post-B/-scope
1023	  `CommonArgs`; introduces the shared clap-free `CommonParams`
1024	  (resolved `DotSpec` + `Header` + repos). All four take a
1025	  fallible `TryFrom<&XxxArgs>` (scope→repo resolution can
1026	  error); `show` also parses `--files` into `FileLimit` at the
1027	  boundary.
1028	
1029	Revised ladder (supersedes the 0.49.0-0 one):
1030	
1031	- 0.49.0-1 Migration B — `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/`
1032	  leaf-bundle + relocate; this re-scope
1033	- 0.49.0-2 `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope` (wire `parse_scope` /
1034	  `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`; slim `for_each_repo`) —
1035	  may split into `-2.N`
1036	- 0.49.0-3 chid Migration A + introduce `CommonParams`
1037	- 0.49.0-4 desc Migration A
1038	- 0.49.0-5 list Migration A
1039	- 0.49.0-6 show Migration A (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse)
1040	- 0.49.0 close-out — drop suffix, todo→Done (Migration A
1041	  12/12 + CommonArgs sweep), README + ARCHITECTURE.md
1042

## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1)

Step 1 of the cycle (Migration B), and the point where the
cycle is re-scoped (see below). Decompose `common::CommonArgs`
into `options_flags/` leaves and relocate the bundle to
`src/options_flags/common_bundle.rs`, so the four read-only
commit-query subcommands flatten a proper leaf-bundle the way
`init` flattens `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle`. Pure refactor —
no behavior change; `vc-x1 chid -h` etc. are byte-identical.

- New leaves (modeled on `squash.rs`): `revision`
  (`RevisionOption`, `-r`/`--revision`, default `@`),
  `commit_limit` (`CommitLimitOption`, `-n`/`--commits`),
  `repo_label` (`RepoLabelOptions`, the coupled `-l`/`-L`
  pair). The two positionals (`REVISION` / `COMMITS`) and the
  `-R`/`--repo` repo list stay inline in the bundle — no second
  consumer would compose them independently, and `-R` is on the
  chopping block in 0.49.0-2.
- `CommonOptionFlagBundle` (`common_bundle.rs`,
  `OptionFlagBundle` marker) flattens the three leaves +
  inline `pos_rev` / `pos_count` / `repos`. `CommonArgs`
  removed from `common.rs` (which keeps the
  `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` / `resolve_*` / `format_*`
  helpers — those didn't move).
- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` flatten the bundle; their
  bodies read `c.revision.value` / `c.limit.value` /
  `c.repo_label.{label,no_label}`. `main.rs`'s `suppress_banner`
  match reads `a.common.repo_label.no_label`. All four `.rs`
  files also gain the `//!` module docstring they were missing.

### `value`-field clash on flattened leaves

`RevisionOption` and `CommitLimitOption` both follow the
single-field-named-`value` convention
([`options_flags/README.md`](../src/options_flags/README.md)
"Adding a new leaf"). clap derives an arg's *id* from the field
name, so two `value` fields flattened into the same struct
collide — `clap_builder` panics at parse time ("Argument names
must be unique, but 'value' is in use by more than one
argument"). Fix: an explicit `#[arg(id = "revision")]` /
`#[arg(id = "commits")]` on each. The convention's worked
example (`finalize`'s `--squash`) never hit this because no
command had flattened two `value` leaves before — this is the
first; the README is updated to note the `id` requirement.

### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)

The 0.49.0-0 plan opened a "finish Migration A" cycle for the
four `CommonArgs`-flattening subcommands. During 0.49.0-1
review the cycle was expanded to the full **"CommonArgs sweep"**
long planned in `chores-06` / `chores-07` — Migration A is
joined by Migration B and the `--scope` rollout for these four,
and re-ordered B-first so the Migration A ports land once,
against the final `CommonArgs` shape. (The 0.49.0-0 commit and
its chores section keep the original narrow framing; this
subsection is the record of the change of plan.)

- **Migration B** (this step, 0.49.0-1) — decompose
  `CommonArgs` into `options_flags/` leaves + relocate the
  bundle. Done; see above.
- **`--scope`** (0.49.0-2) — replace the repeatable
  `-R`/`--repo` with `-s`/`--scope` (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>`),
  wiring up `scope.rs`'s `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single` (both
  `#[allow(dead_code)]` — built in 0.42.0 for exactly this) and
  `common.rs`'s `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`. Per the
  0.42.0 capture (`chores-06` 0.41.0-4): `-R` is dropped, not
  aliased; arbitrary multi-repo (`-R . -R .claude`) is not
  preserved — `--scope=<path>` covers single-repo, the default
  is `default_scope()`. `for_each_repo` then takes an
  already-resolved `Vec<PathBuf>` (comma-expansion + `["."]`
  default move to the boundary). `init`'s `--scope` (the
  separate `ScopeKind` `code,bot|por`) is left alone —
  reconciling the two vocabularies is the "por/dual parity"
  todo.
- **Migration A** (0.49.0-3..-6) — `XxxParams` +
  `fn x(&Context, &XxxParams)` ports against the post-B/-scope
  `CommonArgs`; introduces the shared clap-free `CommonParams`
  (resolved `DotSpec` + `Header` + repos). All four take a
  fallible `TryFrom<&XxxArgs>` (scope→repo resolution can
  error); `show` also parses `--files` into `FileLimit` at the
  boundary.

Revised ladder (supersedes the 0.49.0-0 one):

- 0.49.0-1 Migration B — `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/`
  leaf-bundle + relocate; this re-scope
- 0.49.0-2 `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope` (wire `parse_scope` /
  `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`; slim `for_each_repo`) —
  may split into `-2.N`
- 0.49.0-3 chid Migration A + introduce `CommonParams`
- 0.49.0-4 desc Migration A
- 0.49.0-5 list Migration A
- 0.49.0-6 show Migration A (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse)
- 0.49.0 close-out — drop suffix, todo→Done (Migration A
  12/12 + CommonArgs sweep), README + ARCHITECTURE.md

# References

[1]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/commit/bdec8579c28b "bdec8579c28b76989e52807a9e6bba93ba301c96"
[2]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/commit/bb27daa86a07 "bb27daa86a078e2e06ebc56e0159e89829fb6356"
[3]: /notes/chores-07.md#--scope-enum-refactor-0420

## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1)

Step 1 of the cycle (Migration B), and the point where the
cycle is re-scoped (see below). Decompose `common::CommonArgs`
into `options_flags/` leaves and relocate the bundle to
`src/options_flags/common_bundle.rs`, so the four read-only
commit-query subcommands flatten a proper leaf-bundle the way
`init` flattens `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle`. Pure refactor —
no behavior change; `vc-x1 chid -h` etc. are byte-identical.

- New leaves (modeled on `squash.rs`): `revision`
  (`RevisionOption`, `-r`/`--revision`, default `@`),
  `commit_limit` (`CommitLimitOption`, `-n`/`--commits`),
  `repo_label` (`RepoLabelOptions`, the coupled `-l`/`-L`
  pair). The two positionals (`REVISION` / `COMMITS`) and the
  `-R`/`--repo` repo list stay inline in the bundle — no second
  consumer would compose them independently, and `-R` is on the
  chopping block in 0.49.0-2.
- `CommonOptionFlagBundle` (`common_bundle.rs`,
  `OptionFlagBundle` marker) flattens the three leaves +
  inline `pos_rev` / `pos_count` / `repos`. `CommonArgs`
  removed from `common.rs` (which keeps the
  `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` / `resolve_*` / `format_*`
  helpers — those didn't move).
- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` flatten the bundle; their
  bodies read `c.revision.value` / `c.limit.value` /
  `c.repo_label.{label,no_label}`. `main.rs`'s `suppress_banner`
  match reads `a.common.repo_label.no_label`. All four `.rs`
  files also gain the `//!` module docstring they were missing.
input.new_string:
## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1)

Step 1 of the cycle (Migration B), and the point where the
cycle is re-scoped (see below). Decompose `common::CommonArgs`
into `options_flags/` leaves + a `common_bundle` bundle, so the
four read-only commit-query subcommands flatten a proper
leaf-bundle the way `init` flattens `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle`.
Pure refactor — no behavior change; `vc-x1 chid -h` etc. stay
byte-identical.

Landed across sub-steps so each commit is one migration, not a
giant move:

- **0.49.0-1.1** — create the leaves + bundle; migrate `chid`.
  New leaves (modeled on `squash.rs`): `revision`
  (`RevisionOption`, `-r`/`--revision`, default `@`),
  `commit_limit` (`CommitLimitOption`, `-n`/`--commits`),
  `repo_label` (`RepoLabelOptions`, the coupled `-l`/`-L`
  pair). `CommonOptionFlagBundle` (`common_bundle.rs`,
  `OptionFlagBundle` marker) flattens the three leaves +
  inline `pos_rev` / `pos_count` / `repos` (the two positionals
  and the `-R` list stay inline — no second consumer composes
  them, and `-R` is dropped in 0.49.0-2). `chid` flattens the
  bundle; its body reads `c.revision.value` / `c.limit.value` /
  `c.repo_label.{label,no_label}`. `main.rs`'s `suppress_banner`
  match reads `a.common.repo_label.no_label` for `Chid`.
  `chid.rs` gains the `//!` docstring it was missing.
  `desc` / `list` / `show` keep flattening `common::CommonArgs`
  for now (the two coexist).
- **0.49.0-1.2 / -1.3 / -1.4** — migrate `desc`, `list`, `show`
  onto the bundle in turn; -1.4 removes `CommonArgs` from
  `common.rs` (which keeps the `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` /
  `resolve_*` / `format_*` helpers — those don't move) once
  nothing references it, and finishes the ARCHITECTURE.md
  reframe.

(Each `.rs` file also gains the `//!` module docstring it was
missing, as it's migrated.)

`0.49.0-1.1` committed in both repos (commit-first per the sub-step protocol — no approval gate at this granularity).

**What landed** — first sub-step of Migration B: the `options_flags/` leaf-bundle infrastructure + `chid` migrated to it. `desc`/`list`/`show` still flatten `common::CommonArgs` (the two coexist); they migrate in `-1.2`/`-1.3`/`-1.4`.

- New `options_flags/` files: `revision.rs` (`RevisionOption`), `commit_limit.rs` (`CommitLimitOption`), `repo_label.rs` (`RepoLabelOptions` — the `-l`/`-L` pair), `common_bundle.rs` (`CommonOptionFlagBundle`, flattens the three leaves + inline `pos_rev`/`pos_count`/`repos`); `mod.rs` registers them. `revision`/`commit_limit` both use the `value` field-name convention → each carries an explicit `#[arg(id=…)]` so the ids don't collide when flattened together; `README.md` notes that caveat.
- `src/chid.rs` — flattens `CommonOptionFlagBundle`; body reads `.value` / `.repo_label.*`; tests updated; gained the `//!` docstring it was missing.
- `src/main.rs` — `suppress_banner` reads `a.common.repo_label.no_label` for `Chid` (others unchanged).
- Docs: `ARCHITECTURE.md` + `notes/chores-09.md` re-scope the `0.49.0` cycle to the full "CommonArgs sweep" (B-first, sub-stepped) — chores section `## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1)` (provisional close-out title) with `### value-field clash` and `### Cycle re-scope` subsections; `notes/todo.md` In Progress ladder shows the `-1.N` sub-steps.
- `Cargo.toml`/`Cargo.lock` → `0.49.0-1.1`.

Checks: `cargo fmt --check` / `clippy --all-targets -D warnings` clean / `cargo test` 360+5+4+5 pass / `cargo install --path . --locked` / `vc-x1 -V` → `0.49.0-1.1` / smoke: `vc-x1 chid -L @.. 2` (bundle path), `vc-x1 desc -L @` (still-CommonArgs path) both correct. Ochid trailers paired: app→`/.claude/msvqwkrrukxv`, `.claude`→`/zrnkqqxzrvon`.

Pure refactor — `vc-x1 chid -h` is byte-identical. Review `@-` in your editor; say the word for `0.49.0-1.2` (`desc`).

message.content: It feels like you've conflated transient information like what we are doing now, which should be in a chores-xx file, into Architecture.md. IMO, Architecture.md is what and some why, but shouldn't contian how or even details of every subcommand but be more generic.

IMO, Architecture.md is what and some why, but shouldn't contian how or even details of every subcommand but be more generic. And related to that we should start chores-10.md.

content.text:
Good catch — that's a real drift. `ARCHITECTURE.md` has accumulated per-subcommand migration status, version-by-version tables, sub-step ladders — all of which is "how"/transient and belongs in a chores file. And `0.49.0-1.1` made it worse. Let me fix that as a focused sub-step before continuing the migrations.

Proposed **`0.49.0-1.2` — docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md, start chores-10.md**:

- **`ARCHITECTURE.md`** — cut to "what + some why, generic":
  - Keep: Overview, the two-layer (CLI args vs `Context`+`Params`) section + rationale, the Naming subsection, the Subcommand-model recipe, See-also.
  - Module map → a generic listing of the *kinds* of module and their roles (`main.rs` the clap edge; `options_flags/` the shared leaf/bundle store; `common.rs` the read-only-subcommand helpers; `context.rs`/`scope.rs`/`config.rs` the subcommand-layer scaffolding; the per-command modules; support) — **no** per-subcommand table with migration status, no version annotations.
  - Migration A / Migration B → keep a short "what it is + why" for each (the layering goal; the leaf/bundle goal), drop the per-subcommand status table, the "Out of scope" enumeration, the "State today" per-subcommand bullets — replace with "live status: `notes/chores-*.md` + `notes/todo.md`".
- **`notes/chores-10.md`** — new file. Move the `0.49.0-0` and `0.49.0-1` sections (+ their `# References`) out of `chores-09.md` into it (chores-09 now ends at `0.48.4` — all done/closed). Add a "Migration A / Migration B — live tracking" section carrying the per-subcommand status tables that came out of ARCHITECTURE.md.
- **`notes/chores-09.md`** — the moved sections gone; prune any now-orphaned `# References` entries.
- **`notes/README.md`** — light touch (it already says `chores-*.md`; note what chores-10 covers).
- **`notes/todo.md`** — repoint the "Design:" link to chores-10; shift the ladder: `desc` → `0.49.0-1.3`, `list` → `-1.4`, `show` → `-1.5` (the last also removes `CommonArgs`).

Net effect: after `-1.2`, ARCHITECTURE.md stays generic and the subsequent desc/list/show sub-steps barely touch it.

One thing to confirm: starting chores-10 by **moving the `0.49.0-*` sections out of chores-09** (so chores-09 = "0.42–0.48, closed", chores-10 = "0.49 onward"), or would you rather chores-09 keeps `0.49.0-0`/`-1.1` and chores-10 starts fresh from `0.49.0-1.2`?

content.content:
869	### When to re-pack a file's references
870	
871	- A renumber is a whole-file rewrite of citations + defs — do it
872	  opportunistically (when the namespace has drifted enough to
873	  annoy) rather than under a standing "keep it dense" rule that
874	  would force a rewrite on every prune.
875	- `todo.md` is the live churn surface (entries land and get
876	  pruned every cycle) so it fragments fastest and is the usual
877	  candidate. `chores-NN.md` / `done.md` are append-mostly; their
878	  numbering only drifts out of reading order after an unusual
879	  event like the 0.48.2/0.48.3 retrofit — which is exactly why
880	  `chores-09` got re-packed here and the other chores files
881	  don't need it.
882	
883	## chore: open 0.49.0 — finish Migration A (0.49.0-0)
884	
885	Multi-step. Finish Migration A: port the last four subcommands
886	`pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` → `pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params:
887	&XxxParams)`, same shape as the `0.48.0` sweep. `chid` / `desc`
888	/ `list` / `show` all `#[command(flatten)]` `common::CommonArgs`,
889	so the cycle also adds a shared clap-free `CommonParams` they
890	reuse. Mechanical; no behavior change.
891	
892	### Per-step shape
893	
894	- `common::CommonParams` (flat, clap-free): the resolved
895	  `DotSpec` + `Header` + raw `repos: Vec<PathBuf>`, with
896	  `impl From<&CommonArgs>` doing the `resolve_spec` /
897	  `resolve_header` work at the binary edge. The `..` parsing and
898	  `-l`/`-L` resolution move out of the subcommand bodies.
899	- `XxxParams`: flat struct embedding `CommonParams`, plus the
900	  subcommand's own fields — `list` adds `width: usize`, `show`
901	  adds `files: FileLimit` (the `--files` string is parsed at the
902	  boundary). `impl From<&XxxArgs>` (total) for `chid` / `desc` /
903	  `list`; `impl TryFrom<&ShowArgs>` for `show` (fallible —
904	  `FileLimit::parse`), mirroring `finalize`.
905	- `pub fn x(args)` → `pub fn x(_ctx: &Context, params:
906	  &XxxParams)`; `ctx` unused (uniform-signature placeholder, as
907	  in `symlink` / `validate-desc` / `fix-desc`).
908	- `main.rs` dispatch arm builds `Context::load(cli.log)` + the
909	  params; the `suppress_banner` match keeps reading
910	  `a.common.no_label` off the args (clap edge, unchanged).
911	- Tests: existing `XxxArgs` parse tests untouched; add a small
912	  "construct `XxxParams` directly" test per the worked-example
913	  precedent.
914	
915	### Ladder
916	
917	Smallest first; `show` (the `TryFrom` + `FileLimit` parse) last.
918	
919	- 0.49.0-0 plan + version bump + this section + todo ladder
920	  (current)
921	- 0.49.0-1 chid + introduce `CommonParams` in `common.rs`
922	- 0.49.0-2 desc
923	- 0.49.0-3 list
924	- 0.49.0-4 show
925	- 0.49.0 close-out — drop suffix, todo→Done, ARCHITECTURE.md
926	  Migration A table all-done (12/12)
927	
928	This cycle is Migration A only. Migration B for these four
929	(folding `CommonArgs` into the `options_flags/` leaf model,
930	dropping the repeatable `-R`/`--repo` for the `--scope` path
931	form) stays the separate "CommonArgs sweep" todo.
932	
933	### Out of scope
934	
935	Typed errors, returned-outcomes-vs-`println!`, `ProgressSink`,
936	`Context` fields beyond `UserConfig` + `--log` — deferred until a
937	real consumer surfaces. Migration B (see above).
938	
939	### Per-substep contract
940	
941	Per `notes/substep-protocol.md`: `cargo fmt` / `clippy
942	--all-targets -- -D warnings` / `test` / `install --path .
943	--locked` + retest before each commit; bump `Cargo.toml` at
944	sub-step start; flip todo ladder markers; pair commits across
945	both repos with ochid trailers.
946	
947	## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1)
948	
949	Step 1 of the cycle (Migration B), and the point where the
950	cycle is re-scoped (see below). Decompose `common::CommonArgs`
951	into `options_flags/` leaves + a `common_bundle` bundle, so the
952	four read-only commit-query subcommands flatten a proper
953	leaf-bundle the way `init` flattens `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle`.
954	Pure refactor — no behavior change; `vc-x1 chid -h` etc. stay
955	byte-identical.
956	
957	Landed across sub-steps so each commit is one migration, not a
958	giant move:
959	
960	- **0.49.0-1.1** — create the leaves + bundle; migrate `chid`.
961	  New leaves (modeled on `squash.rs`): `revision`
962	  (`RevisionOption`, `-r`/`--revision`, default `@`),
963	  `commit_limit` (`CommitLimitOption`, `-n`/`--commits`),
964	  `repo_label` (`RepoLabelOptions`, the coupled `-l`/`-L`
965	  pair). `CommonOptionFlagBundle` (`common_bundle.rs`,
966	  `OptionFlagBundle` marker) flattens the three leaves +
967	  inline `pos_rev` / `pos_count` / `repos` (the two positionals
968	  and the `-R` list stay inline — no second consumer composes
969	  them, and `-R` is dropped in 0.49.0-2). `chid` flattens the
970	  bundle; its body reads `c.revision.value` / `c.limit.value` /
971	  `c.repo_label.{label,no_label}`. `main.rs`'s `suppress_banner`
972	  match reads `a.common.repo_label.no_label` for `Chid`.
973	  `chid.rs` gains the `//!` docstring it was missing.
974	  `desc` / `list` / `show` keep flattening `common::CommonArgs`
975	  for now (the two coexist).
976	- **0.49.0-1.2 / -1.3 / -1.4** — migrate `desc`, `list`, `show`
977	  onto the bundle in turn; -1.4 removes `CommonArgs` from
978	  `common.rs` (which keeps the `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` /
979	  `resolve_*` / `format_*` helpers — those don't move) once
980	  nothing references it, and finishes the ARCHITECTURE.md
981	  reframe.
982	
983	(Each `.rs` file also gains the `//!` module docstring it was
984	missing, as it's migrated.)
985	
986	### `value`-field clash on flattened leaves
987	
988	`RevisionOption` and `CommitLimitOption` both follow the
989	single-field-named-`value` convention
990	([`options_flags/README.md`](../src/options_flags/README.md)
991	"Adding a new leaf"). clap derives an arg's *id* from the field
992	name, so two `value` fields flattened into the same struct
993	collide — `clap_builder` panics at parse time ("Argument names
994	must be unique, but 'value' is in use by more than one
995	argument"). Fix: an explicit `#[arg(id = "revision")]` /
996	`#[arg(id = "commits")]` on each. The convention's worked
997	example (`finalize`'s `--squash`) never hit this because no
998	command had flattened two `value` leaves before — this is the
999	first; the README is updated to note the `id` requirement.
1000	
1001	### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)
1002	
1003	The 0.49.0-0 plan opened a "finish Migration A" cycle for the
1004	four `CommonArgs`-flattening subcommands. During 0.49.0-1
1005	review the cycle was expanded to the full **"CommonArgs sweep"**
1006	long planned in `chores-06` / `chores-07` — Migration A is
1007	joined by Migration B and the `--scope` rollout for these four,
1008	and re-ordered B-first so the Migration A ports land once,
1009	against the final `CommonArgs` shape. (The 0.49.0-0 commit and
1010	its chores section keep the original narrow framing; this
1011	subsection is the record of the change of plan.)
1012	
1013	- **Migration B** (0.49.0-1, sub-steps -1.1..-1.4) — decompose
1014	  `CommonArgs` into `options_flags/` leaves + a bundle, migrate
1015	  the four subcommands one per sub-step, remove `CommonArgs`.
1016	  See above.
1017	- **`--scope`** (0.49.0-2) — replace the repeatable
1018	  `-R`/`--repo` with `-s`/`--scope` (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>`),
1019	  wiring up `scope.rs`'s `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single` (both
1020	  `#[allow(dead_code)]` — built in 0.42.0 for exactly this) and
1021	  `common.rs`'s `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`. Per the
1022	  0.42.0 capture (`chores-06` 0.41.0-4): `-R` is dropped, not
1023	  aliased; arbitrary multi-repo (`-R . -R .claude`) is not
1024	  preserved — `--scope=<path>` covers single-repo, the default
1025	  is `default_scope()`. `for_each_repo` then takes an
1026	  already-resolved `Vec<PathBuf>` (comma-expansion + `["."]`
1027	  default move to the boundary). `init`'s `--scope` (the
1028	  separate `ScopeKind` `code,bot|por`) is left alone —
1029	  reconciling the two vocabularies is the "por/dual parity"
1030	  todo.
1031	- **Migration A** (0.49.0-3..-6) — `XxxParams` +
1032	  `fn x(&Context, &XxxParams)` ports against the post-B/-scope
1033	  `CommonArgs`; introduces the shared clap-free `CommonParams`
1034	  (resolved `DotSpec` + `Header` + repos). All four take a
1035	  fallible `TryFrom<&XxxArgs>` (scope→repo resolution can
1036	  error); `show` also parses `--files` into `FileLimit` at the
1037	  boundary.
1038	
1039	Revised ladder (supersedes the 0.49.0-0 one):
1040	
1041	- 0.49.0-1 Migration B — `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/`
1042	  leaf-bundle + relocate; this re-scope. Sub-steps:
1043	  - 0.49.0-1.1 leaves + bundle + migrate `chid`
1044	  - 0.49.0-1.2 migrate `desc`
1045	  - 0.49.0-1.3 migrate `list`
1046	  - 0.49.0-1.4 migrate `show`; remove `CommonArgs`; ARCH docs
1047	  - 0.49.0-1 close-out (squash to one commit or keep separate —
1048	    decided then)
1049	- 0.49.0-2 `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope` (wire `parse_scope` /
1050	  `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`; slim `for_each_repo`) —
1051	  may split into `-2.N`
1052	- 0.49.0-3 chid Migration A + introduce `CommonParams`
1053	- 0.49.0-4 desc Migration A
1054	- 0.49.0-5 list Migration A
1055	- 0.49.0-6 show Migration A (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse)

file.content:
### When to re-pack a file's references

- A renumber is a whole-file rewrite of citations + defs — do it
  opportunistically (when the namespace has drifted enough to
  annoy) rather than under a standing "keep it dense" rule that
  would force a rewrite on every prune.
- `todo.md` is the live churn surface (entries land and get
  pruned every cycle) so it fragments fastest and is the usual
  candidate. `chores-NN.md` / `done.md` are append-mostly; their
  numbering only drifts out of reading order after an unusual
  event like the 0.48.2/0.48.3 retrofit — which is exactly why
  `chores-09` got re-packed here and the other chores files
  don't need it.

## chore: open 0.49.0 — finish Migration A (0.49.0-0)

Multi-step. Finish Migration A: port the last four subcommands
`pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` → `pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params:
&XxxParams)`, same shape as the `0.48.0` sweep. `chid` / `desc`
/ `list` / `show` all `#[command(flatten)]` `common::CommonArgs`,
so the cycle also adds a shared clap-free `CommonParams` they
reuse. Mechanical; no behavior change.

### Per-step shape

- `common::CommonParams` (flat, clap-free): the resolved
  `DotSpec` + `Header` + raw `repos: Vec<PathBuf>`, with
  `impl From<&CommonArgs>` doing the `resolve_spec` /
  `resolve_header` work at the binary edge. The `..` parsing and
  `-l`/`-L` resolution move out of the subcommand bodies.
- `XxxParams`: flat struct embedding `CommonParams`, plus the
  subcommand's own fields — `list` adds `width: usize`, `show`
  adds `files: FileLimit` (the `--files` string is parsed at the
  boundary). `impl From<&XxxArgs>` (total) for `chid` / `desc` /
  `list`; `impl TryFrom<&ShowArgs>` for `show` (fallible —
  `FileLimit::parse`), mirroring `finalize`.
- `pub fn x(args)` → `pub fn x(_ctx: &Context, params:
  &XxxParams)`; `ctx` unused (uniform-signature placeholder, as
  in `symlink` / `validate-desc` / `fix-desc`).
- `main.rs` dispatch arm builds `Context::load(cli.log)` + the
  params; the `suppress_banner` match keeps reading
  `a.common.no_label` off the args (clap edge, unchanged).
- Tests: existing `XxxArgs` parse tests untouched; add a small
  "construct `XxxParams` directly" test per the worked-example
  precedent.

### Ladder

Smallest first; `show` (the `TryFrom` + `FileLimit` parse) last.

- 0.49.0-0 plan + version bump + this section + todo ladder
  (current)
- 0.49.0-1 chid + introduce `CommonParams` in `common.rs`
- 0.49.0-2 desc
- 0.49.0-3 list
- 0.49.0-4 show
- 0.49.0 close-out — drop suffix, todo→Done, ARCHITECTURE.md
  Migration A table all-done (12/12)

This cycle is Migration A only. Migration B for these four
(folding `CommonArgs` into the `options_flags/` leaf model,
dropping the repeatable `-R`/`--repo` for the `--scope` path
form) stays the separate "CommonArgs sweep" todo.

### Out of scope

Typed errors, returned-outcomes-vs-`println!`, `ProgressSink`,
`Context` fields beyond `UserConfig` + `--log` — deferred until a
real consumer surfaces. Migration B (see above).

### Per-substep contract

Per `notes/substep-protocol.md`: `cargo fmt` / `clippy
--all-targets -- -D warnings` / `test` / `install --path .
--locked` + retest before each commit; bump `Cargo.toml` at
sub-step start; flip todo ladder markers; pair commits across
both repos with ochid trailers.

## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1)

Step 1 of the cycle (Migration B), and the point where the
cycle is re-scoped (see below). Decompose `common::CommonArgs`
into `options_flags/` leaves + a `common_bundle` bundle, so the
four read-only commit-query subcommands flatten a proper
leaf-bundle the way `init` flattens `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle`.
Pure refactor — no behavior change; `vc-x1 chid -h` etc. stay
byte-identical.

Landed across sub-steps so each commit is one migration, not a
giant move:

- **0.49.0-1.1** — create the leaves + bundle; migrate `chid`.
  New leaves (modeled on `squash.rs`): `revision`
  (`RevisionOption`, `-r`/`--revision`, default `@`),
  `commit_limit` (`CommitLimitOption`, `-n`/`--commits`),
  `repo_label` (`RepoLabelOptions`, the coupled `-l`/`-L`
  pair). `CommonOptionFlagBundle` (`common_bundle.rs`,
  `OptionFlagBundle` marker) flattens the three leaves +
  inline `pos_rev` / `pos_count` / `repos` (the two positionals
  and the `-R` list stay inline — no second consumer composes
  them, and `-R` is dropped in 0.49.0-2). `chid` flattens the
  bundle; its body reads `c.revision.value` / `c.limit.value` /
  `c.repo_label.{label,no_label}`. `main.rs`'s `suppress_banner`
  match reads `a.common.repo_label.no_label` for `Chid`.
  `chid.rs` gains the `//!` docstring it was missing.
  `desc` / `list` / `show` keep flattening `common::CommonArgs`
  for now (the two coexist).
- **0.49.0-1.2 / -1.3 / -1.4** — migrate `desc`, `list`, `show`
  onto the bundle in turn; -1.4 removes `CommonArgs` from
  `common.rs` (which keeps the `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` /
  `resolve_*` / `format_*` helpers — those don't move) once
  nothing references it, and finishes the ARCHITECTURE.md
  reframe.

(Each `.rs` file also gains the `//!` module docstring it was
missing, as it's migrated.)

### `value`-field clash on flattened leaves

`RevisionOption` and `CommitLimitOption` both follow the
single-field-named-`value` convention
([`options_flags/README.md`](../src/options_flags/README.md)
"Adding a new leaf"). clap derives an arg's *id* from the field
name, so two `value` fields flattened into the same struct
collide — `clap_builder` panics at parse time ("Argument names
must be unique, but 'value' is in use by more than one
argument"). Fix: an explicit `#[arg(id = "revision")]` /
`#[arg(id = "commits")]` on each. The convention's worked
example (`finalize`'s `--squash`) never hit this because no
command had flattened two `value` leaves before — this is the
first; the README is updated to note the `id` requirement.

### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)

The 0.49.0-0 plan opened a "finish Migration A" cycle for the
four `CommonArgs`-flattening subcommands. During 0.49.0-1
review the cycle was expanded to the full **"CommonArgs sweep"**
long planned in `chores-06` / `chores-07` — Migration A is
joined by Migration B and the `--scope` rollout for these four,
and re-ordered B-first so the Migration A ports land once,
against the final `CommonArgs` shape. (The 0.49.0-0 commit and
its chores section keep the original narrow framing; this
subsection is the record of the change of plan.)

- **Migration B** (0.49.0-1, sub-steps -1.1..-1.4) — decompose
  `CommonArgs` into `options_flags/` leaves + a bundle, migrate
  the four subcommands one per sub-step, remove `CommonArgs`.
  See above.
- **`--scope`** (0.49.0-2) — replace the repeatable
  `-R`/`--repo` with `-s`/`--scope` (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>`),
  wiring up `scope.rs`'s `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single` (both
  `#[allow(dead_code)]` — built in 0.42.0 for exactly this) and
  `common.rs`'s `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`. Per the
  0.42.0 capture (`chores-06` 0.41.0-4): `-R` is dropped, not
  aliased; arbitrary multi-repo (`-R . -R .claude`) is not
  preserved — `--scope=<path>` covers single-repo, the default
  is `default_scope()`. `for_each_repo` then takes an
  already-resolved `Vec<PathBuf>` (comma-expansion + `["."]`
  default move to the boundary). `init`'s `--scope` (the
  separate `ScopeKind` `code,bot|por`) is left alone —
  reconciling the two vocabularies is the "por/dual parity"
  todo.
- **Migration A** (0.49.0-3..-6) — `XxxParams` +
  `fn x(&Context, &XxxParams)` ports against the post-B/-scope
  `CommonArgs`; introduces the shared clap-free `CommonParams`
  (resolved `DotSpec` + `Header` + repos). All four take a
  fallible `TryFrom<&XxxArgs>` (scope→repo resolution can
  error); `show` also parses `--files` into `FileLimit` at the
  boundary.

Revised ladder (supersedes the 0.49.0-0 one):

- 0.49.0-1 Migration B — `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/`
  leaf-bundle + relocate; this re-scope. Sub-steps:
  - 0.49.0-1.1 leaves + bundle + migrate `chid`
  - 0.49.0-1.2 migrate `desc`
  - 0.49.0-1.3 migrate `list`
  - 0.49.0-1.4 migrate `show`; remove `CommonArgs`; ARCH docs
  - 0.49.0-1 close-out (squash to one commit or keep separate —
    decided then)
- 0.49.0-2 `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope` (wire `parse_scope` /
  `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`; slim `for_each_repo`) —
  may split into `-2.N`
- 0.49.0-3 chid Migration A + introduce `CommonParams`
- 0.49.0-4 desc Migration A
- 0.49.0-5 list Migration A
- 0.49.0-6 show Migration A (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse)
- 0.49.0 close-out — drop suffix, todo→Done (Migration A
  12/12 + CommonArgs sweep), README + ARCHITECTURE.md

# References
