input.content:
# Chores-10

Continuation of `chores-09.md` (which is closed at `0.48.4` —
the `0.42.0`–`0.48.x` cycles). This file covers the `0.49.0`
cycle onward, plus a living **Migration A / Migration B
tracking** section (the per-subcommand status that used to sit
in `ARCHITECTURE.md`).

See [`../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ARCHITECTURE.md) for what
Migration A / B *are* and why; this file is the *how* and the
*live status*. Reference numbering is file-local — see
[`README.md`](README.md#reference-numbering); chores-10 starts
at `[1]`.

## Migration A / Migration B — live tracking

Two parallel refactors of the subcommand layer (see
`ARCHITECTURE.md`). Status as of the entries below; update in
place as cycles land.

### Migration A — `pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` → `pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`

| Subcommand | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | done (0.44.0) — worked example; `From<&InitArgs>` |
| `finalize` | done (0.46.0) — `TryFrom<&FinalizeArgs>` (fallible); `--log` onto `Context` |
| `symlink` | done (0.48.0-1) — `From<&SymlinkArgs>` |
| `clone` | done (0.48.0-2) — `From<&CloneArgs>` |
| `sync` | done (0.48.0-3) — `From<&SyncArgs>` |
| `validate-desc` | done (0.48.0-4) — `From<&ValidateDescArgs>` |
| `fix-desc` | done (0.48.0-5) — `From<&FixDescArgs>` |
| `push` | done (0.48.0-6) — `From<&PushArgs>` (collapses the two bookmark spellings) |
| `chid` | pending (0.49.0-3) — introduces the shared `CommonParams`; `TryFrom` |
| `desc` | pending (0.49.0-4) — `TryFrom` |
| `list` | pending (0.49.0-5) — `TryFrom` |
| `show` | pending (0.49.0-6) — `TryFrom`; also parses `--files` → `FileLimit` at the boundary |

Out of scope for the ports (deferred until a real consumer
surfaces): typed errors, returned-outcomes-vs-`println!`,
`ProgressSink`, `Context` fields beyond `UserConfig` + `--log`.

### Migration B — per-subcommand inline `#[arg]` → `src/options_flags/` leaves/bundles

| Subcommand(s) | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | fully composed — `account` / `repo` / `scope` / the `provision` bundle / `use_template` / `config` |
| `finalize` | `--squash` → `squash` leaf; `--delay` / `--detach` / `--exec` / `--repo` / `--push` still inline |
| `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` | being moved onto `options_flags::common_bundle::CommonOptionFlagBundle` — `chid` 0.49.0-1.1, `desc` -1.3, `list` -1.4, `show` -1.5 (`common::CommonArgs` removed after the last); then `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope` leaf in 0.49.0-2 |
| `sync` / `push` / `clone` / `validate-desc` / `fix-desc` / `symlink` | mostly inline; the `--scope` retrofits queued in `todo.md` are the usual entry point |

## chore: open 0.49.0 — finish Migration A (0.49.0-0)

Commits: [[1]]

Multi-step. Finish Migration A: port the last four subcommands
`pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` → `pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params:
&XxxParams)`, same shape as the `0.48.0` sweep. `chid` / `desc`
/ `list` / `show` all `#[command(flatten)]` `common::CommonArgs`,
so the cycle also adds a shared clap-free `CommonParams` they
reuse. Mechanical; no behavior change.

(Superseded at 0.49.0-1 — the cycle was expanded to the full
"CommonArgs sweep", B-first; see the next section's `### Cycle
re-scope`. This section keeps the original narrow framing as the
record of what the 0.49.0-0 commit's plan was.)

### Per-step shape

- `common::CommonParams` (flat, clap-free): the resolved
  `DotSpec` + `Header` + raw `repos: Vec<PathBuf>`, with
  `impl From<&CommonArgs>` doing the `resolve_spec` /
  `resolve_header` work at the binary edge. The `..` parsing and
  `-l`/`-L` resolution move out of the subcommand bodies.
- `XxxParams`: flat struct embedding `CommonParams`, plus the
  subcommand's own fields — `list` adds `width: usize`, `show`
  adds `files: FileLimit` (the `--files` string is parsed at the
  boundary). `impl From<&XxxArgs>` (total) for `chid` / `desc` /
  `list`; `impl TryFrom<&ShowArgs>` for `show` (fallible —
  `FileLimit::parse`), mirroring `finalize`.
- `pub fn x(args)` → `pub fn x(_ctx: &Context, params:
  &XxxParams)`; `ctx` unused (uniform-signature placeholder, as
  in `symlink` / `validate-desc` / `fix-desc`).
- `main.rs` dispatch arm builds `Context::load(cli.log)` + the
  params; the `suppress_banner` match keeps reading
  `a.common.no_label` off the args (clap edge, unchanged).
- Tests: existing `XxxArgs` parse tests untouched; add a small
  "construct `XxxParams` directly" test per the worked-example
  precedent.

### Ladder (original — superseded)

Smallest first; `show` (the `TryFrom` + `FileLimit` parse) last.

- 0.49.0-0 plan + version bump + this section + todo ladder
  (current)
- 0.49.0-1 chid + introduce `CommonParams` in `common.rs`
- 0.49.0-2 desc
- 0.49.0-3 list
- 0.49.0-4 show
- 0.49.0 close-out — drop suffix, todo→Done, ARCHITECTURE.md
  Migration A table all-done (12/12)

This cycle is Migration A only. Migration B for these four
(folding `CommonArgs` into the `options_flags/` leaf model,
dropping the repeatable `-R`/`--repo` for the `--scope` path
form) stays the separate "CommonArgs sweep" todo.

### Per-substep contract

Per `notes/substep-protocol.md`: `cargo fmt` / `clippy
--all-targets -- -D warnings` / `test` / `install --path .
--locked` + retest before each commit; bump `Cargo.toml` at
sub-step start; flip todo ladder markers; pair commits across
both repos with ochid trailers.

## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1)

The Migration B step of the cycle, and the point where the cycle
is re-scoped (see `### Cycle re-scope`). Decompose
`common::CommonArgs` into `options_flags/` leaves + a
`common_bundle` bundle, so the four read-only commit-query
subcommands flatten a proper leaf-bundle the way `init` flattens
`ProvisionOptionFlagBundle`. Pure refactor — no behavior change;
`vc-x1 chid -h` etc. stay byte-identical.

Landed across sub-steps so each commit is one migration, not a
giant move:

- **0.49.0-1.1** — create the leaves + bundle; migrate `chid`.
  New leaves (modeled on `squash.rs`): `revision`
  (`RevisionOption`, `-r`/`--revision`, default `@`),
  `commit_limit` (`CommitLimitOption`, `-n`/`--commits`),
  `repo_label` (`RepoLabelOptions`, the coupled `-l`/`-L`
  pair). `CommonOptionFlagBundle` (`common_bundle.rs`,
  `OptionFlagBundle` marker) flattens the three leaves +
  inline `pos_rev` / `pos_count` / `repos` (the two positionals
  and the `-R` list stay inline — no second consumer composes
  them, and `-R` is dropped in 0.49.0-2). `chid` flattens the
  bundle; its body reads `c.revision.value` / `c.limit.value` /
  `c.repo_label.{label,no_label}`. `main.rs`'s `suppress_banner`
  match reads `a.common.repo_label.no_label` for `Chid`.
  `chid.rs` gains the `//!` docstring it was missing.
  `desc` / `list` / `show` keep flattening `common::CommonArgs`
  for now (the two coexist).
- **0.49.0-1.2** — the ARCHITECTURE.md slim + chores-10 reorg
  (its own section below); slotted into this step's ladder
  because ARCHITECTURE.md had accumulated this cycle's transient
  detail.
- **0.49.0-1.3 / -1.4 / -1.5** — migrate `desc`, `list`, `show`
  onto the bundle in turn; -1.5 removes `CommonArgs` from
  `common.rs` (which keeps the `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` /
  `resolve_*` / `format_*` helpers — those don't move) once
  nothing references it.

(Each `.rs` file also gains the `//!` module docstring it was
missing, as it's migrated.)

### `value`-field clash on flattened leaves

`RevisionOption` and `CommitLimitOption` both follow the
single-field-named-`value` convention
([`options_flags/README.md`](../src/options_flags/README.md)
"Adding a new leaf"). clap derives an arg's *id* from the field
name, so two `value` fields flattened into the same struct
collide — `clap_builder` panics at parse time ("Argument names
must be unique, but 'value' is in use by more than one
argument"). Fix: an explicit `#[arg(id = "revision")]` /
`#[arg(id = "commits")]` on each. The convention's worked
example (`finalize`'s `--squash`) never hit this because no
command had flattened two `value` leaves before — this is the
first; the README is updated to note the `id` requirement.

### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)

The 0.49.0-0 plan opened a "finish Migration A" cycle for the
four `CommonArgs`-flattening subcommands. During 0.49.0-1
review the cycle was expanded to the full **"CommonArgs sweep"**
long planned in `chores-06` / `chores-07` — Migration A is
joined by Migration B and the `--scope` rollout for these four,
and re-ordered B-first so the Migration A ports land once,
against the final `CommonArgs` shape. (The 0.49.0-0 commit and
its chores section keep the original narrow framing; this
subsection is the record of the change of plan.)

- **Migration B** (0.49.0-1, sub-steps -1.1, -1.3, -1.4, -1.5;
  `-1.2` is the docs reorg) — decompose `CommonArgs` into
  `options_flags/` leaves + a bundle, migrate the four
  subcommands one per sub-step, remove `CommonArgs`. See above.
- **`--scope`** (0.49.0-2) — replace the repeatable
  `-R`/`--repo` with `-s`/`--scope` (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>`),
  wiring up `scope.rs`'s `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single` (both
  `#[allow(dead_code)]` — built in 0.42.0 for exactly this) and
  `common.rs`'s `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`. Per the
  0.42.0 capture (`chores-06` 0.41.0-4): `-R` is dropped, not
  aliased; arbitrary multi-repo (`-R . -R .claude`) is not
  preserved — `--scope=<path>` covers single-repo, the default
  is `default_scope()`. `for_each_repo` then takes an
  already-resolved `Vec<PathBuf>` (comma-expansion + `["."]`
  default move to the boundary). `init`'s `--scope` (the
  separate `ScopeKind` `code,bot|por`) is left alone —
  reconciling the two vocabularies is the "por/dual parity"
  todo.
- **Migration A** (0.49.0-3..-6) — `XxxParams` +
  `fn x(&Context, &XxxParams)` ports against the post-B/-scope
  `CommonArgs`; introduces the shared clap-free `CommonParams`
  (resolved `DotSpec` + `Header` + repos). All four take a
  fallible `TryFrom<&XxxArgs>` (scope→repo resolution can
  error); `show` also parses `--files` into `FileLimit` at the
  boundary.

Revised ladder (supersedes the 0.49.0-0 one):

- 0.49.0-1 Migration B — `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/`
  leaf-bundle + relocate; this re-scope. Sub-steps:
  - 0.49.0-1.1 leaves + bundle + migrate `chid`
  - 0.49.0-1.2 docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; start chores-10
  - 0.49.0-1.3 migrate `desc`
  - 0.49.0-1.4 migrate `list`
  - 0.49.0-1.5 migrate `show`; remove `CommonArgs`
  - 0.49.0-1 close-out (squash to one commit or keep separate —
    decided then)
- 0.49.0-2 `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope` (wire `parse_scope` /
  `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`; slim `for_each_repo`) —
  may split into `-2.N`
- 0.49.0-3 chid Migration A + introduce `CommonParams`
- 0.49.0-4 desc Migration A
- 0.49.0-5 list Migration A
- 0.49.0-6 show Migration A (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse)
- 0.49.0 close-out — drop suffix, todo→Done (Migration A
  12/12 + CommonArgs sweep), README + ARCHITECTURE.md

## docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; chores-10 (0.49.0-1.2)

Acting on review feedback that `ARCHITECTURE.md` had drifted
into "how" / transient territory — per-subcommand migration
status, version-by-version tables, sub-step ladders. Pull that
out so `ARCHITECTURE.md` is "what + some why, generic" and the
transient tracking lives here.

- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — keeps the Overview, the two-layer
  (CLI-args vs `Context`+`Params`) section + rationale + the
  `Naming` subsection, a generic module map (the *kinds* of
  module and their roles, no per-subcommand table or version
  annotations), the Subcommand-model recipe, and the See-also.
  The `## Migration A` / `## Migration B` sections become short
  "what it is + why" with a pointer here for live status — the
  per-subcommand status table, the version-by-version "done"
  list, and the "State today" bullets are gone.
- `notes/chores-10.md` — new (this file). Carries the
  `## Migration A / Migration B — live tracking` section (the
  tables that came out of `ARCHITECTURE.md`) plus the `0.49.0-0`
  and `0.49.0-1` sections moved out of `chores-09.md`.
- `notes/chores-09.md` — the `0.49.0-*` sections removed; it now
  ends at `0.48.4` (all done/closed).
- `notes/README.md` — notes that `chores-09` is closed and
  `chores-10` is the active file with the tracking section.
- `notes/todo.md` — "Design:" link repointed to `chores-10.md`;
  the `0.49.0-1` sub-step ladder inserts `-1.2` (this) and
  shifts `desc` → `-1.3`, `list` → `-1.4`, `show` → `-1.5`.

# References

[1]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/commit/10788bd158c4 "10788bd158c4574fe5a10fab41ea32e4becc86d3"

### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)

The 0.49.0-0 plan opened a "finish Migration A" cycle for the
four `CommonArgs`-flattening subcommands. During 0.49.0-1
review the cycle was expanded to the full **"CommonArgs sweep"**
long planned in `chores-06` / `chores-07` — Migration A is
joined by Migration B and the `--scope` rollout for these four,
and re-ordered B-first so the Migration A ports land once,
against the final `CommonArgs` shape. (The 0.49.0-0 commit and
its chores section keep the original narrow framing; this
subsection is the record of the change of plan.)

- **Migration B** (0.49.0-1, sub-steps -1.1, -1.3, -1.4, -1.5;
  `-1.2` is the docs reorg) — decompose `CommonArgs` into
  `options_flags/` leaves + a bundle, migrate the four
  subcommands one per sub-step, remove `CommonArgs`. See above.
- **`--scope`** (0.49.0-2) — replace the repeatable
  `-R`/`--repo` with `-s`/`--scope` (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>`),
  wiring up `scope.rs`'s `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single` (both
  `#[allow(dead_code)]` — built in 0.42.0 for exactly this) and
  `common.rs`'s `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`. Per the
  0.42.0 capture (`chores-06` 0.41.0-4): `-R` is dropped, not
  aliased; arbitrary multi-repo (`-R . -R .claude`) is not
  preserved — `--scope=<path>` covers single-repo, the default
  is `default_scope()`. `for_each_repo` then takes an
  already-resolved `Vec<PathBuf>` (comma-expansion + `["."]`
  default move to the boundary). `init`'s `--scope` (the
  separate `ScopeKind` `code,bot|por`) is left alone —
  reconciling the two vocabularies is the "por/dual parity"
  todo.
- **Migration A** (0.49.0-3..-6) — `XxxParams` +
  `fn x(&Context, &XxxParams)` ports against the post-B/-scope
  `CommonArgs`; introduces the shared clap-free `CommonParams`
  (resolved `DotSpec` + `Header` + repos). All four take a
  fallible `TryFrom<&XxxArgs>` (scope→repo resolution can
  error); `show` also parses `--files` into `FileLimit` at the
  boundary.

Revised ladder (supersedes the 0.49.0-0 one):

- 0.49.0-1 Migration B — `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/`
  leaf-bundle + relocate; this re-scope. Sub-steps:
  - 0.49.0-1.1 leaves + bundle + migrate `chid`
  - 0.49.0-1.2 docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; start chores-10
  - 0.49.0-1.3 migrate `desc`
  - 0.49.0-1.4 migrate `list`
  - 0.49.0-1.5 migrate `show`; remove `CommonArgs`
  - 0.49.0-1 close-out (squash to one commit or keep separate —
    decided then)
- 0.49.0-2 `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope` (wire `parse_scope` /
  `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`; slim `for_each_repo`) —
  may split into `-2.N`
- 0.49.0-3 chid Migration A + introduce `CommonParams`
- 0.49.0-4 desc Migration A
- 0.49.0-5 list Migration A
- 0.49.0-6 show Migration A (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse)
- 0.49.0 close-out — drop suffix, todo→Done (Migration A
  12/12 + CommonArgs sweep), README + ARCHITECTURE.md

## docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; chores-10 (0.49.0-1.2)

Acting on review feedback that `ARCHITECTURE.md` had drifted
into "how" / transient territory — per-subcommand migration
status, version-by-version tables, sub-step ladders. Pull that
out so `ARCHITECTURE.md` is "what + some why, generic" and the
transient tracking lives here.

- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — keeps the Overview, the two-layer
  (CLI-args vs `Context`+`Params`) section + rationale + the
  `Naming` subsection, a generic module map (the *kinds* of
  module and their roles, no per-subcommand table or version
  annotations), the Subcommand-model recipe, and the See-also.
  The `## Migration A` / `## Migration B` sections become short
  "what it is + why" with a pointer here for live status — the
  per-subcommand status table, the version-by-version "done"
  list, and the "State today" bullets are gone.
- `notes/chores-10.md` — new (this file). Carries the
  `## Migration A / Migration B — live tracking` section (the
  tables that came out of `ARCHITECTURE.md`) plus the `0.49.0-0`
  and `0.49.0-1` sections moved out of `chores-09.md`.
- `notes/chores-09.md` — the `0.49.0-*` sections removed; it now
  ends at `0.48.4` (all done/closed).
- `notes/README.md` — notes that `chores-09` is closed and
  `chores-10` is the active file with the tracking section.
- `notes/todo.md` — "Design:" link repointed to `chores-10.md`;
  the `0.49.0-1` sub-step ladder inserts `-1.2` (this) and
  shifts `desc` → `-1.3`, `list` → `-1.4`, `show` → `-1.5`.

content.content:
1	# Architecture
2	
3	How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI
4	layer and the (emerging) clap-free subcommand layer.
5	
6	This is a living document; the app is under continuous
7	development and the repo will keep changing. Per-cycle design
8	discussion lives in `notes/chores-*.md`; near-term tasks in
9	`notes/todo.md`.
10	
11	## Overview
12	
13	`vc-x1` is a single binary that supports subcommands,
14	`vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace**
15	with an app repo plus a `.claude` bot-session repo. The commits
16	cross-reference each other via `ochid:` trailers at the bottom
17	of each commit message. See [`README.md`](README.md)
18	for the user-facing picture and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for
19	the bot workflow.
20	
21	## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params
22	
23	Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
24	operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library
25	embedding) can call the same core without dragging clap
26	along. The trigger was `args.account.account`-style
27	nested-leaf access leaking into subcommand bodies; accessor
28	shortcuts were rejected as hiding the mismatch rather than
29	fixing it.
30	
31	- **CLI layer (clap-aware)**:
32	  Builds `XxxParams` from the user-facing `XxxArgs`
33	  `#[derive(Args)]` clap struct. Options/flags used in more
34	  than one subcommand live as flattened leaves in
35	  `src/options_flags/`.
36	- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)**:
37	  Entrypoint shape: `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams) -> Result<…>`.
38	  And this isolates the subcommand from user and clap.
39	  - `Context` — common state shared with all subcommands.
40	  - `XxxParams` — a subcommand's parameters, derived from
41	    config or command-line arguments.
42	- **Boundary conversion**:
43	  Converting `XxxArgs` to `XxxParams` uses either
44	  `impl From<&XxxArgs> for XxxParams` or
45	  `impl TryFrom<&XxxArgs> for XxxParams`, depending on whether
46	  the conversion can fail.
47	
48	Two parameters per subcommand; the signature documents what
49	the subcommand depends on. `Context` is the same across all
50	subcommands; `XxxParams` carry the per-subcommand inputs.
51	
52	Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
53	[Migration A](#migration-a--args--context--params)):
54	
55	1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
56	   `XxxParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
57	   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
58	2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a
59	   TUI matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
60	3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each subcommand
61	   returns a structured result; CLI formats it, a library
62	   writes nothing to stdout itself.
63	4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
64	   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests a
65	   recording sink.
66	5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
67	   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.
68	
69	None of this is sacred — the whole structure is an experiment
70	and the repo keeps changing. The current scope is deliberately
71	narrow:
72	
73	- a small `Context` — two params, not one god blob;
74	- no trait-based DI yet;
75	- one crate, not a workspace split;
76	- `src/options_flags/` leaves left in place — only their
77	  consumers change shape.
78	
79	Widen any of it when the evidence (a second front-end, a second
80	consumer crate, a real platform-surface divergence) calls for
81	it — not before.
82	
83	### Naming
84	
85	Earlier drafts of this design (and the `notes/chores-09.md`
86	capture) used `Workspace` / `XOptions`. The implementation
87	(0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XxxParams` instead:
88	
89	- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
90	  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the
91	  dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
92	- `XOptions` → `XxxParams` — avoids visual collision with
93	  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
94	  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.
95	
96	## Module map
97	
98	CLI layer:
99	
100	- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge:
101	  - `pub struct Cli` — clap `Parser`.
102	  - `pub enum Commands` — clap `Subcommand`; matched in the
103	    dispatch arms.
104	  - `cli_with_banner()` — walks the command tree adding the
105	    `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as `before_help` on every subcommand.
106	  - `CompleteEnv` wiring for dynamic completion.
107	  - `bm_track` — a permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
108	    diagnostic printed on entry/exit of every command.
109	
110	- `src/options_flags/` — options/flags shared across
111	  subcommands, so a flag is defined once (parser, help,
112	  completer); Migration B tracks what's been moved here so
113	  far. See
114	  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
115	  - reusable **leaves**: `account`, `commit_limit`, `config`,
116	    `dry_run`, `private`, `push_retry`, `repo`, `repo_label`,
117	    `revision`, `scope`, `squash`, `use_template`.
118	  - reusable **bundles**: `common_bundle`
119	    (`CommonOptionFlagBundle` — the read-only commit-query OFs,
120	    flattened by `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`),
121	    `provision_bundle`.
122	- `src/common.rs` — the repo-iteration and revision-resolution
123	  helpers the read-only commit-query subcommands share:
124	  `for_each_repo`, `collect_ids`, `resolve_revset`, `DotSpec` /
125	  `parse_dot_rev` / `resolve_spec` (`..` notation + count
126	  reconciliation), `Header` / `resolve_header`, the `format_*`
127	  printers, plus `find_workspace_root` / `default_scope` /
128	  `scope_to_repos`. Also still holds `CommonArgs` — being
129	  retired: the 0.49.0-1 sub-steps move the four read-only
130	  subcommands onto `options_flags::common_bundle::CommonOptionFlagBundle`
131	  one at a time (`chid` at 0.49.0-1.1), and `CommonArgs` is
132	  removed once the last lands; 0.49.0-2 then swaps `-R` for
133	  `-s`/`--scope`.
134	
135	Subcommand layer scaffolding:
136	
137	- `src/context.rs` — `Context`: the shared platform handle
138	  every subcommand runs against. Today it carries the loaded
139	  `UserConfig` and the `--log` path; built once at startup
140	  via `Context::load(log)`.
141	- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
142	- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
143	  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for
144	  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`. `parse_scope` /
145	  `Scope::Single` are `#[allow(dead_code)]` until 0.49.0-2
146	  wires `-s`/`--scope` into the read-only commit-query commands.
147	
148	Subcommand modules — each holds an `XxxArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
149	struct and a `pub fn x(...)` entrypoint:
150	
151	| Module | Subcommand | Notes |
152	| --- | --- | --- |
153	| `chid.rs` | `chid` | flattens `options_flags::common_bundle::CommonOptionFlagBundle` (0.49.0-1.1; Migration A → 0.49.0-3) |
154	| `desc.rs` (+ `desc_helpers.rs`) | `desc` | flattens `common::CommonArgs` → `CommonOptionFlagBundle` in 0.49.0-1.2; Migration A → 0.49.0-4 |
155	| `list.rs` | `list` | flattens `common::CommonArgs` + `-w`/`--width` → bundle in 0.49.0-1.3; Migration A → 0.49.0-5 |
156	| `show.rs` | `show` | flattens `common::CommonArgs` + `-f`/`--files` → bundle in 0.49.0-1.4; Migration A → 0.49.0-6 |
157	| `validate_desc.rs` | `validate-desc` | migrated (0.48.0-4) — `validate_desc(&Context, &ValidateDescParams)`, `From<&ValidateDescArgs>` |
158	| `fix_desc.rs` | `fix-desc` | migrated (0.48.0-5) — `fix_desc(&Context, &FixDescParams)`, `From<&FixDescArgs>` |
159	| `clone.rs` | `clone` | migrated (0.48.0-2) — `clone_repo(&Context, &CloneParams)`, `From<&CloneArgs>` |
160	| `init.rs` (+ `init/params.rs`) | `init` | subcommand-layer worked example (0.44.0) |
161	| `symlink.rs` | `symlink` | migrated (0.48.0-1) — `symlink(&Context, &SymlinkParams)`, `From<&SymlinkArgs>` |
162	| `sync.rs` | `sync` | migrated (0.48.0-3) — `sync(&Context, &SyncParams)`, `From<&SyncArgs>` |
163	| `finalize.rs` | `finalize` | migrated (0.46.0) — `finalize(&Context, &FinalizeParams)`, `TryFrom<&FinalizeArgs>` |
164	| `push.rs` | `push` | resumable state machine; migrated (0.48.0-6) — `push(&Context, &PushParams)`, `From<&PushArgs>` |
165	
166	Support:
167	
168	- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
169	  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
170	  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
171	- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
172	  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures (`Fixture` / `FixturePor`) and
173	  tempdir resolution. Subcommand modules also carry their
174	  own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and `integration_tests` for
175	  `sync` / `push`).
176	
177	## Subcommand model
178	
179	Adding a subcommand `x`:
180	
181	1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XxxArgs`
182	   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
183	   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
184	2. Add a variant `X(x::XxxArgs)` to `Commands` in
185	   `src/main.rs` with a `///` summary (and
186	   `#[command(long_about = …)]` for the long form).
187	3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
188	   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
189	4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
190	   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
191	   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
192	   prose).
193	5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
194	   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
195	   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
196	   and stays at this layer.
197	
198	## Migration A — args → Context + Params
199	
200	Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` to
201	`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`, adding an
202	`XxxParams` flat struct + a `From` (or `TryFrom`, if the
203	conversion is fallible) at the binary edge. `init` (0.44.0) is
204	the worked example; `finalize` (0.46.0) added the `TryFrom` /
205	`Context.log` variant; the `0.48.0` cycle swept the standalone
206	subcommands; the `0.49.0` cycle is the "CommonArgs sweep" for
207	the four `CommonArgs`-flattening ones — first Migration B
208	(decompose `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/` leaf-bundle, -1) and
209	the `--scope` rollout (`-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope`, -2), then
210	Migration A against the final shape (`chid` -3, `desc` -4,
211	`list` -5, `show` -6; these embed a shared clap-free
212	`CommonParams`, and all four take a fallible `TryFrom<&XxxArgs>`
213	because scope→repo resolution can error). See the Migration B
214	section below and `notes/chores-09.md`. The live checklist is
215	the "Subcommand layer / CLI decoupling" item in
216	[`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).
217	
218	| Subcommand | Status |
219	| --- | --- |
220	| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)`; `From<&InitArgs>` |
221	| `finalize` | done (0.46.0) — `finalize(&Context, &FinalizeParams)`; `TryFrom<&FinalizeArgs>` (fallible boundary); `--log` moved onto `Context` |
222	| `symlink` | done (0.48.0-1) — `symlink(&Context, &SymlinkParams)`; `From<&SymlinkArgs>` |
223	| `clone` | done (0.48.0-2) — `clone_repo(&Context, &CloneParams)`; `From<&CloneArgs>` |
224	| `sync` | done (0.48.0-3) — `sync(&Context, &SyncParams)`; `From<&SyncArgs>` |
225	| `validate-desc` | done (0.48.0-4) — `validate_desc(&Context, &ValidateDescParams)`; `From<&ValidateDescArgs>` |
226	| `fix-desc` | done (0.48.0-5) — `fix_desc(&Context, &FixDescParams)`; `From<&FixDescArgs>` |
227	| `push` | done (0.48.0-6) — `push(&Context, &PushParams)`; `From<&PushArgs>` (collapses the two bookmark spellings) |
228	| `chid` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-3) — the 0.49.0 CommonArgs sweep; introduces the shared `CommonParams` |
229	| `desc` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-4) — the 0.49.0 CommonArgs sweep |
230	| `list` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-5) — the 0.49.0 CommonArgs sweep |
231	| `show` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-6) — the 0.49.0 CommonArgs sweep (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse) |
232	
233	Out of scope for these ports (deferred until a real consumer
234	surfaces): typed errors, returned outcomes vs `println!`, the
235	`ProgressSink`, and `Context` fields beyond `UserConfig` + the
236	`--log` path (`finalize` surfaced `log`).
237	
238	## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`
239	
240	Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
241	fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
242	`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
243	completer) exactly once. See
244	[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
245	for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
246	Flag-vs-Option classification.
247	
248	State today:
249	
250	- **`init`** — fully composed from leaves/bundles
251	  (`account`, `repo`, `scope`, the `provision` bundle,
252	  `use_template`, `config`).
253	- **`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`** — being migrated off
254	  `common::CommonArgs` onto
255	  `options_flags::common_bundle::CommonOptionFlagBundle` (the
256	  0.49.0 "CommonArgs sweep" cycle, one subcommand per sub-step:
257	  `chid` 0.49.0-1.1, `desc` -1.2, `list` -1.3, `show` -1.4 —
258	  `CommonArgs` removed when the last lands). The bundle is
259	  composed of the `revision` and `commit_limit` leaves, the
260	  `repo_label` leaf (the `-l`/`-L` pair), plus the two
261	  positionals (`REVISION` / `COMMITS`) and the `-R`/`--repo`
262	  list inline; `-R`/`--repo` is then swapped for a `--scope`
263	  leaf (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>` — wiring up `scope.rs`'s
264	  `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single`) in 0.49.0-2; the positionals
265	  stay inline. (Two of the leaves — `RevisionOption`,
266	  `CommitLimitOption` — both use the `value` field-name
267	  convention, so each carries an explicit `#[arg(id = …)]` to
268	  keep clap arg ids unique when flattened into the same struct.)
269	- **`finalize`** — `--squash` lifted to the `squash` leaf
270	  (which carries the `value`-field naming convention — see
271	  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
272	  "Adding a new leaf"). `--delay` / `--detach` / `--exec` /
273	  `--repo` / `--push` stay inline (no second consumer yet).
274	- **`sync` / `push` / `clone` / `validate-desc` /
275	  `fix-desc` / `symlink`** — still mostly inline `#[arg]`
276	  fields. The `--scope` retrofits queued in
277	  [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) are the usual entry
278	  point for converting one.
279	
280	## See also
281	
282	- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
283	  per-subcommand usage.
284	- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
285	  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
286	- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
287	  — leaf/bundle patterns for Migration B.
288	- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list,
289	  including both migrations.
290	- [`notes/chores-09.md`](notes/chores-09.md) — the
291	  0.43.0 / 0.44.0 design capture this document supersedes.

file.content:
# Architecture

How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI
layer and the (emerging) clap-free subcommand layer.

This is a living document; the app is under continuous
development and the repo will keep changing. Per-cycle design
discussion lives in `notes/chores-*.md`; near-term tasks in
`notes/todo.md`.

## Overview

`vc-x1` is a single binary that supports subcommands,
`vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace**
with an app repo plus a `.claude` bot-session repo. The commits
cross-reference each other via `ochid:` trailers at the bottom
of each commit message. See [`README.md`](README.md)
for the user-facing picture and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for
the bot workflow.

## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params

Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library
embedding) can call the same core without dragging clap
along. The trigger was `args.account.account`-style
nested-leaf access leaking into subcommand bodies; accessor
shortcuts were rejected as hiding the mismatch rather than
fixing it.

- **CLI layer (clap-aware)**:
  Builds `XxxParams` from the user-facing `XxxArgs`
  `#[derive(Args)]` clap struct. Options/flags used in more
  than one subcommand live as flattened leaves in
  `src/options_flags/`.
- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)**:
  Entrypoint shape: `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams) -> Result<…>`.
  And this isolates the subcommand from user and clap.
  - `Context` — common state shared with all subcommands.
  - `XxxParams` — a subcommand's parameters, derived from
    config or command-line arguments.
- **Boundary conversion**:
  Converting `XxxArgs` to `XxxParams` uses either
  `impl From<&XxxArgs> for XxxParams` or
  `impl TryFrom<&XxxArgs> for XxxParams`, depending on whether
  the conversion can fail.

Two parameters per subcommand; the signature documents what
the subcommand depends on. `Context` is the same across all
subcommands; `XxxParams` carry the per-subcommand inputs.

Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
[Migration A](#migration-a--args--context--params)):

1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
   `XxxParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a
   TUI matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each subcommand
   returns a structured result; CLI formats it, a library
   writes nothing to stdout itself.
4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests a
   recording sink.
5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.

None of this is sacred — the whole structure is an experiment
and the repo keeps changing. The current scope is deliberately
narrow:

- a small `Context` — two params, not one god blob;
- no trait-based DI yet;
- one crate, not a workspace split;
- `src/options_flags/` leaves left in place — only their
  consumers change shape.

Widen any of it when the evidence (a second front-end, a second
consumer crate, a real platform-surface divergence) calls for
it — not before.

### Naming

Earlier drafts of this design (and the `notes/chores-09.md`
capture) used `Workspace` / `XOptions`. The implementation
(0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XxxParams` instead:

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the
  dual-repo project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XxxParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Module map

CLI layer:

- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge:
  - `pub struct Cli` — clap `Parser`.
  - `pub enum Commands` — clap `Subcommand`; matched in the
    dispatch arms.
  - `cli_with_banner()` — walks the command tree adding the
    `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as `before_help` on every subcommand.
  - `CompleteEnv` wiring for dynamic completion.
  - `bm_track` — a permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
    diagnostic printed on entry/exit of every command.

- `src/options_flags/` — options/flags shared across
  subcommands, so a flag is defined once (parser, help,
  completer); Migration B tracks what's been moved here so
  far. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
  - reusable **leaves**: `account`, `commit_limit`, `config`,
    `dry_run`, `private`, `push_retry`, `repo`, `repo_label`,
    `revision`, `scope`, `squash`, `use_template`.
  - reusable **bundles**: `common_bundle`
    (`CommonOptionFlagBundle` — the read-only commit-query OFs,
    flattened by `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`),
    `provision_bundle`.
- `src/common.rs` — the repo-iteration and revision-resolution
  helpers the read-only commit-query subcommands share:
  `for_each_repo`, `collect_ids`, `resolve_revset`, `DotSpec` /
  `parse_dot_rev` / `resolve_spec` (`..` notation + count
  reconciliation), `Header` / `resolve_header`, the `format_*`
  printers, plus `find_workspace_root` / `default_scope` /
  `scope_to_repos`. Also still holds `CommonArgs` — being
  retired: the 0.49.0-1 sub-steps move the four read-only
  subcommands onto `options_flags::common_bundle::CommonOptionFlagBundle`
  one at a time (`chid` at 0.49.0-1.1), and `CommonArgs` is
  removed once the last lands; 0.49.0-2 then swaps `-R` for
  `-s`/`--scope`.

Subcommand layer scaffolding:

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`: the shared platform handle
  every subcommand runs against. Today it carries the loaded
  `UserConfig` and the `--log` path; built once at startup
  via `Context::load(log)`.
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`. `parse_scope` /
  `Scope::Single` are `#[allow(dead_code)]` until 0.49.0-2
  wires `-s`/`--scope` into the read-only commit-query commands.

Subcommand modules — each holds an `XxxArgs` `#[derive(Args)]`
struct and a `pub fn x(...)` entrypoint:

| Module | Subcommand | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `chid.rs` | `chid` | flattens `options_flags::common_bundle::CommonOptionFlagBundle` (0.49.0-1.1; Migration A → 0.49.0-3) |
| `desc.rs` (+ `desc_helpers.rs`) | `desc` | flattens `common::CommonArgs` → `CommonOptionFlagBundle` in 0.49.0-1.2; Migration A → 0.49.0-4 |
| `list.rs` | `list` | flattens `common::CommonArgs` + `-w`/`--width` → bundle in 0.49.0-1.3; Migration A → 0.49.0-5 |
| `show.rs` | `show` | flattens `common::CommonArgs` + `-f`/`--files` → bundle in 0.49.0-1.4; Migration A → 0.49.0-6 |
| `validate_desc.rs` | `validate-desc` | migrated (0.48.0-4) — `validate_desc(&Context, &ValidateDescParams)`, `From<&ValidateDescArgs>` |
| `fix_desc.rs` | `fix-desc` | migrated (0.48.0-5) — `fix_desc(&Context, &FixDescParams)`, `From<&FixDescArgs>` |
| `clone.rs` | `clone` | migrated (0.48.0-2) — `clone_repo(&Context, &CloneParams)`, `From<&CloneArgs>` |
| `init.rs` (+ `init/params.rs`) | `init` | subcommand-layer worked example (0.44.0) |
| `symlink.rs` | `symlink` | migrated (0.48.0-1) — `symlink(&Context, &SymlinkParams)`, `From<&SymlinkArgs>` |
| `sync.rs` | `sync` | migrated (0.48.0-3) — `sync(&Context, &SyncParams)`, `From<&SyncArgs>` |
| `finalize.rs` | `finalize` | migrated (0.46.0) — `finalize(&Context, &FinalizeParams)`, `TryFrom<&FinalizeArgs>` |
| `push.rs` | `push` | resumable state machine; migrated (0.48.0-6) — `push(&Context, &PushParams)`, `From<&PushArgs>` |

Support:

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures (`Fixture` / `FixturePor`) and
  tempdir resolution. Subcommand modules also carry their
  own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and `integration_tests` for
  `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XxxArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XxxArgs)` to `Commands` in
   `src/main.rs` with a `///` summary (and
   `#[command(long_about = …)]` for the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.

## Migration A — args → Context + Params

Port each subcommand's `pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` to
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`, adding an
`XxxParams` flat struct + a `From` (or `TryFrom`, if the
conversion is fallible) at the binary edge. `init` (0.44.0) is
the worked example; `finalize` (0.46.0) added the `TryFrom` /
`Context.log` variant; the `0.48.0` cycle swept the standalone
subcommands; the `0.49.0` cycle is the "CommonArgs sweep" for
the four `CommonArgs`-flattening ones — first Migration B
(decompose `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/` leaf-bundle, -1) and
the `--scope` rollout (`-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope`, -2), then
Migration A against the final shape (`chid` -3, `desc` -4,
`list` -5, `show` -6; these embed a shared clap-free
`CommonParams`, and all four take a fallible `TryFrom<&XxxArgs>`
because scope→repo resolution can error). See the Migration B
section below and `notes/chores-09.md`. The live checklist is
the "Subcommand layer / CLI decoupling" item in
[`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md).

| Subcommand | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | done (0.44.0) — `init(&Context, &InitParams)`; `From<&InitArgs>` |
| `finalize` | done (0.46.0) — `finalize(&Context, &FinalizeParams)`; `TryFrom<&FinalizeArgs>` (fallible boundary); `--log` moved onto `Context` |
| `symlink` | done (0.48.0-1) — `symlink(&Context, &SymlinkParams)`; `From<&SymlinkArgs>` |
| `clone` | done (0.48.0-2) — `clone_repo(&Context, &CloneParams)`; `From<&CloneArgs>` |
| `sync` | done (0.48.0-3) — `sync(&Context, &SyncParams)`; `From<&SyncArgs>` |
| `validate-desc` | done (0.48.0-4) — `validate_desc(&Context, &ValidateDescParams)`; `From<&ValidateDescArgs>` |
| `fix-desc` | done (0.48.0-5) — `fix_desc(&Context, &FixDescParams)`; `From<&FixDescArgs>` |
| `push` | done (0.48.0-6) — `push(&Context, &PushParams)`; `From<&PushArgs>` (collapses the two bookmark spellings) |
| `chid` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-3) — the 0.49.0 CommonArgs sweep; introduces the shared `CommonParams` |
| `desc` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-4) — the 0.49.0 CommonArgs sweep |
| `list` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-5) — the 0.49.0 CommonArgs sweep |
| `show` | Migration A pending (0.49.0-6) — the 0.49.0 CommonArgs sweep (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse) |

Out of scope for these ports (deferred until a real consumer
surfaces): typed errors, returned outcomes vs `println!`, the
`ProgressSink`, and `Context` fields beyond `UserConfig` + the
`--log` path (`finalize` surfaced `log`).

## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification.

State today:

- **`init`** — fully composed from leaves/bundles
  (`account`, `repo`, `scope`, the `provision` bundle,
  `use_template`, `config`).
- **`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`** — being migrated off
  `common::CommonArgs` onto
  `options_flags::common_bundle::CommonOptionFlagBundle` (the
  0.49.0 "CommonArgs sweep" cycle, one subcommand per sub-step:
  `chid` 0.49.0-1.1, `desc` -1.2, `list` -1.3, `show` -1.4 —
  `CommonArgs` removed when the last lands). The bundle is
  composed of the `revision` and `commit_limit` leaves, the
  `repo_label` leaf (the `-l`/`-L` pair), plus the two
  positionals (`REVISION` / `COMMITS`) and the `-R`/`--repo`
  list inline; `-R`/`--repo` is then swapped for a `--scope`
  leaf (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>` — wiring up `scope.rs`'s
  `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single`) in 0.49.0-2; the positionals
  stay inline. (Two of the leaves — `RevisionOption`,
  `CommitLimitOption` — both use the `value` field-name
  convention, so each carries an explicit `#[arg(id = …)]` to
  keep clap arg ids unique when flattened into the same struct.)
- **`finalize`** — `--squash` lifted to the `squash` leaf
  (which carries the `value`-field naming convention — see
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  "Adding a new leaf"). `--delay` / `--detach` / `--exec` /
  `--repo` / `--push` stay inline (no second consumer yet).
- **`sync` / `push` / `clone` / `validate-desc` /
  `fix-desc` / `symlink`** — still mostly inline `#[arg]`
  fields. The `--scope` retrofits queued in
  [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) are the usual entry
  point for converting one.

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
  per-subcommand usage.
- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  — leaf/bundle patterns for Migration B.
- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list,
  including both migrations.
- [`notes/chores-09.md`](notes/chores-09.md) — the
  0.43.0 / 0.44.0 design capture this document supersedes.

# Architecture

How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI layer
and the (emerging) clap-free subcommand layer. This is the
"what + some why" — generic and slow-moving. The *how* and the
per-cycle / per-subcommand status live in `notes/chores-*.md`
and `notes/todo.md`.

This is a living document; the app is under continuous
development and the repo will keep changing.

## Overview

`vc-x1` is a single binary that supports subcommands,
`vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace**
with an app repo plus a `.claude` bot-session repo. The commits
cross-reference each other via `ochid:` trailers at the bottom
of each commit message. See [`README.md`](README.md) for the
user-facing picture and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for the bot
workflow.

## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params

Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library embedding)
can call the same core without dragging clap along. The trigger
was `args.account.account`-style nested-leaf access leaking into
subcommand bodies; accessor shortcuts were rejected as hiding
the mismatch rather than fixing it.

- **CLI layer (clap-aware)**: builds `XxxParams` from the
  user-facing `XxxArgs` `#[derive(Args)]` clap struct.
  Options/flags used in more than one subcommand live as
  flattened leaves/bundles in `src/options_flags/`.
- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)**: entrypoint shape
  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams) -> Result<…>` —
  isolates the subcommand from clap and from the user.
  - `Context` — platform state shared by every subcommand.
  - `XxxParams` — that subcommand's inputs, derived from
    config / command-line args.
- **Boundary conversion**: `XxxArgs → XxxParams` via
  `impl From<&XxxArgs>` or `impl TryFrom<&XxxArgs>`, depending
  on whether the conversion can fail.

Two parameters per subcommand; the signature documents what the
subcommand depends on. `Context` is the same across all
subcommands; `XxxParams` carry the per-subcommand inputs.

Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
[Migration A](#migration-a--args--context--params)):

1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
   `XxxParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a TUI
   matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each subcommand returns
   a structured result; CLI formats it, a library writes
   nothing to stdout itself.
4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests
   a recording sink.
5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.

None of this is sacred — the whole structure is an experiment.
The current scope is deliberately narrow: a small `Context`
(two params, not one god blob); no trait-based DI yet; one
crate, not a workspace split; `src/options_flags/` leaves left
in place — only their consumers change shape. Widen any of it
when the evidence (a second front-end, a second consumer crate,
a real platform-surface divergence) calls for it — not before.

### Naming

Earlier drafts of this design used `Workspace` / `XOptions`;
the implementation (0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XxxParams`:

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the dual-repo
  project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XxxParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Module map

The *kinds* of module and what they're for — not an exhaustive
catalog (read the `//!` docstrings; `notes/chores-*.md` for
migration status):

**CLI layer:**

- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge: `Cli` (`Parser`), `Commands`
  (`Subcommand`) + the dispatch arms, `cli_with_banner()` (the
  per-subcommand `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner), `CompleteEnv` wiring,
  and `bm_track` (the permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic on every command's entry/exit).
- `src/options_flags/` — reusable `#[derive(Args)]` *leaves*
  (one flag, or a small fixed pair) and *bundles*
  (`#[command(flatten)]` of N leaves) shared across
  subcommands, so a flag is defined (parser, help, completer)
  exactly once. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
- `src/common.rs` — helpers the read-only commit-query
  subcommands (`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`) share:
  `for_each_repo`, `collect_ids`, `resolve_revset`, the
  `DotSpec` / `..`-notation parsing, `Header` /
  `resolve_header`, the `format_*` printers, and
  `find_workspace_root` / `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`.

**Subcommand-layer scaffolding:**

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`, the shared platform handle
  (today: the loaded `UserConfig` + the `--log` path; built
  once at startup via `Context::load(log)`).
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for explicit
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

**Subcommand modules** — `src/<x>.rs` holds the subcommand's
`XxxArgs` (`#[derive(Args)]`, composing `options_flags/` leaves
where one exists) and the entrypoint `pub fn x(...)`. Modules:
`chid`, `desc` (+ `desc_helpers`), `list`, `show`,
`validate_desc`, `fix_desc`, `clone`, `init` (+ `init/params`),
`symlink`, `sync`, `finalize`, `push` (a resumable state
machine). Which ones have ported to `(ctx, params)` (Migration
A) and which compose `options_flags/` leaves (Migration B):
`notes/chores-*.md` + `notes/todo.md`.

**Support:**

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures and tempdir resolution. Subcommand
  modules also carry their own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and
  `integration_tests` for `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XxxArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XxxArgs)` to `Commands` in `src/main.rs`
   with a `///` summary (and `#[command(long_about = …)]` for
   the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.

## Migration A — args → Context + Params

The ongoing port of each subcommand's
`pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` to
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`, adding an
`XxxParams` flat struct + a `From` (or `TryFrom`, if the
conversion is fallible) at the binary edge. `init` (0.44.0) is
the worked example. Out of scope for the ports until a real
consumer surfaces: typed errors,
returned-outcomes-vs-`println!`, `ProgressSink`, `Context`
fields beyond `UserConfig` + `--log`. Per-subcommand status:
`notes/chores-*.md` (the "Migration A / Migration B — live
tracking" section).

## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification; `notes/chores-*.md` for
per-subcommand status.

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
  per-subcommand usage.
- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  — leaf/bundle patterns for Migration B.
- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list, including
  both migrations.
- `notes/chores-*.md` — per-cycle design captures and the
  Migration A/B live-tracking tables (currently
  [`chores-10.md`](notes/chores-10.md); `chores-09.md` and
  earlier are closed).

toolUseResult.content:
# Architecture

How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI layer
and the (emerging) clap-free subcommand layer. This is the
"what + some why" — generic and slow-moving. The *how* and the
per-cycle / per-subcommand status live in `notes/chores-*.md`
and `notes/todo.md`.

This is a living document; the app is under continuous
development and the repo will keep changing.

## Overview

`vc-x1` is a single binary that supports subcommands,
`vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace**
with an app repo plus a `.claude` bot-session repo. The commits
cross-reference each other via `ochid:` trailers at the bottom
of each commit message. See [`README.md`](README.md) for the
user-facing picture and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for the bot
workflow.

## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params

Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library embedding)
can call the same core without dragging clap along. The trigger
was `args.account.account`-style nested-leaf access leaking into
subcommand bodies; accessor shortcuts were rejected as hiding
the mismatch rather than fixing it.

- **CLI layer (clap-aware)**: builds `XxxParams` from the
  user-facing `XxxArgs` `#[derive(Args)]` clap struct.
  Options/flags used in more than one subcommand live as
  flattened leaves/bundles in `src/options_flags/`.
- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)**: entrypoint shape
  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams) -> Result<…>` —
  isolates the subcommand from clap and from the user.
  - `Context` — platform state shared by every subcommand.
  - `XxxParams` — that subcommand's inputs, derived from
    config / command-line args.
- **Boundary conversion**: `XxxArgs → XxxParams` via
  `impl From<&XxxArgs>` or `impl TryFrom<&XxxArgs>`, depending
  on whether the conversion can fail.

Two parameters per subcommand; the signature documents what the
subcommand depends on. `Context` is the same across all
subcommands; `XxxParams` carry the per-subcommand inputs.

Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
[Migration A](#migration-a--args--context--params)):

1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
   `XxxParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a TUI
   matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each subcommand returns
   a structured result; CLI formats it, a library writes
   nothing to stdout itself.
4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests
   a recording sink.
5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.

None of this is sacred — the whole structure is an experiment.
The current scope is deliberately narrow: a small `Context`
(two params, not one god blob); no trait-based DI yet; one
crate, not a workspace split; `src/options_flags/` leaves left
in place — only their consumers change shape. Widen any of it
when the evidence (a second front-end, a second consumer crate,
a real platform-surface divergence) calls for it — not before.

### Naming

Earlier drafts of this design used `Workspace` / `XOptions`;
the implementation (0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XxxParams`:

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the dual-repo
  project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XxxParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Module map

The *kinds* of module and what they're for — not an exhaustive
catalog (read the `//!` docstrings; `notes/chores-*.md` for
migration status):

**CLI layer:**

- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge: `Cli` (`Parser`), `Commands`
  (`Subcommand`) + the dispatch arms, `cli_with_banner()` (the
  per-subcommand `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner), `CompleteEnv` wiring,
  and `bm_track` (the permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic on every command's entry/exit).
- `src/options_flags/` — reusable `#[derive(Args)]` *leaves*
  (one flag, or a small fixed pair) and *bundles*
  (`#[command(flatten)]` of N leaves) shared across
  subcommands, so a flag is defined (parser, help, completer)
  exactly once. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
- `src/common.rs` — helpers the read-only commit-query
  subcommands (`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`) share:
  `for_each_repo`, `collect_ids`, `resolve_revset`, the
  `DotSpec` / `..`-notation parsing, `Header` /
  `resolve_header`, the `format_*` printers, and
  `find_workspace_root` / `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`.

**Subcommand-layer scaffolding:**

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`, the shared platform handle
  (today: the loaded `UserConfig` + the `--log` path; built
  once at startup via `Context::load(log)`).
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for explicit
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

**Subcommand modules** — `src/<x>.rs` holds the subcommand's
`XxxArgs` (`#[derive(Args)]`, composing `options_flags/` leaves
where one exists) and the entrypoint `pub fn x(...)`. Modules:
`chid`, `desc` (+ `desc_helpers`), `list`, `show`,
`validate_desc`, `fix_desc`, `clone`, `init` (+ `init/params`),
`symlink`, `sync`, `finalize`, `push` (a resumable state
machine). Which ones have ported to `(ctx, params)` (Migration
A) and which compose `options_flags/` leaves (Migration B):
`notes/chores-*.md` + `notes/todo.md`.

**Support:**

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures and tempdir resolution. Subcommand
  modules also carry their own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and
  `integration_tests` for `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XxxArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XxxArgs)` to `Commands` in `src/main.rs`
   with a `///` summary (and `#[command(long_about = …)]` for
   the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.

## Migration A — args → Context + Params

The ongoing port of each subcommand's
`pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` to
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`, adding an
`XxxParams` flat struct + a `From` (or `TryFrom`, if the
conversion is fallible) at the binary edge. `init` (0.44.0) is
the worked example. Out of scope for the ports until a real
consumer surfaces: typed errors,
returned-outcomes-vs-`println!`, `ProgressSink`, `Context`
fields beyond `UserConfig` + `--log`. Per-subcommand status:
`notes/chores-*.md` (the "Migration A / Migration B — live
tracking" section).

## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification; `notes/chores-*.md` for
per-subcommand status.

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
  per-subcommand usage.
- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  — leaf/bundle patterns for Migration B.
- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list, including
  both migrations.
- `notes/chores-*.md` — per-cycle design captures and the
  Migration A/B live-tracking tables (currently
  [`chores-10.md`](notes/chores-10.md); `chores-09.md` and
  earlier are closed).

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Notes

This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.

By default there are chores-*.md and todo.md. Chores are general notes
about tasks and todo.md contains short term tasks and their status.

App architecture — module map, the CLI-args / `Context`+`Params`
split, the subcommand model, and the in-flight migrations — lives
at the repo root in [`../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ARCHITECTURE.md).

In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
database to better manage the information, TBD.

Examples chore file:
```
# Chores-01.md
 
General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
how and why of a particular chore.

## Create a binary that lists jj info 

This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
and using `jj-lib`
```

## Workflow and conventions

Bot-facing workflow, versioning, and code conventions live in
[`../CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md). Start there for:

- **Versioning during development** — single-step vs multi-step,
  `-N` pre-release suffixes, done-marker discipline.
- **Code Conventions** — doc comments on every file / fn / method,
  `// OK: …` justifications on `unwrap*` calls, ask-on-ambiguity,
  stuck detection.
- **Commit-Push-Finalize Flow** — two-checkpoint per-step
  discipline with hard stop after finalize.

## Todo format

Todo.md contains two main sections "Todo" and "Done" each item is a
short explanations of a tasks and links to more details using 1 or more
references.

Todo items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. ` and the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them. Reorder or insert items without
renumbering, and reference an entry by its displayed number ("let's
work on #3"). The Done section keeps `-` bullets — items aren't
referenced by number once completed.

Reference citations are **double-bracketed** — `[[1]]`, or
`[[2]],[[3]]` for several — so the brackets show when rendered
(a bare `[1]` renders as just `1`, which doesn't read as a
reference). Separate multiple with a comma: `[[2]],[[3]]`, not
`[2,3]` (a single ref key — won't resolve) or `[[2]][[3]]`. The
`[N]:` definition lines and inline `[text](url)` / `[text](#anchor)`
links stay single-bracketed (their brackets already show in
source, and the link text reads as a link when rendered).

Examples:

# Todo
- Add new feature X [details](features.md#feature-x)
- Fix bug Y [[1]]

# Done
- Fixed issue Z [[2]],[[3]]

[1]: bugs.md#bug-y
[2]: issues.md#issue-z
[3]: fixes.md#fix-z

## Reference numbering

Every note file (`todo.md`, `chores-NN.md`, `done.md`) keeps a
file-local `# References` section at the bottom. Reference numbers
are scoped to that file — `[1]` in `chores-07.md` and `[1]` in
`chores-01.md` are independent slots that may point at completely
different URLs. New chores files start their numbering at `[1]`.

Treat `[N]` like a **footnote**: the number is a local slot, only
meaningful within its file's `# References`. So a `[N]` *citation*
(bare `[N]`, or doubled `[[N]]` — see below) never reuses another
file's number; to cite a target a sibling file references, pick
your own next-local slot and define it (the same target may carry
a different number in each file). A `[N]` *inside a code span*
(`` `[72]` ``) is different — that's a quoted identifier: literal
text naming a ref-key (often from another file's namespace), data,
not a citation; it needs no definition here. To point at a section
of another file from prose, use an inline link with an anchor —
`[that section](../chores-07.md#…)` — not a bare number.

A `chores-NN.md` `# References` entry is usually a
`/notes/<file>.md#anchor` (or `/ARCHITECTURE.md`) path, but may
also be a **commit reference** —
`[N]: <commit-url-with-12-hex-SHA> "<full-40-hex-SHA>"`, cited by
a section's `Commits:` line. See
[`../CLAUDE.md`](../CLAUDE.md#chores-commit-references) for the
why and the exact shape.

A file's `# References` can be **re-packed** to a contiguous
`[1]..[N]` in first-citation-appearance order — walk the file's
prose in document order (`todo.md`: `## Todo` then `## Done`;
`chores-NN.md`: top to bottom) and number refs as their first
`[[N]]` citation appears. This is a file-local rewrite — only
that file's `[[N]]` citations and `[N]:` definitions move; every
target and sibling file is untouched. A `[[N]]` inside a `` ` ``
code span is a literal token, not a citation, and is left alone.
Do it opportunistically (when the namespace has drifted enough to
annoy), not on a schedule: `todo.md` fragments fastest (entries
land and get pruned every cycle) and is the usual candidate;
`chores-NN.md` / `done.md` are append-mostly and only need it
after an unusual event (e.g. a bulk retrofit that allocated slots
out of document order).

## Retiring Done entries

`todo.md`'s `## Done` section is a rolling buffer of recently shipped
work, not a permanent log. Move entries into `done.md` at two natural
beats:

- **Closing a ladder** — when the final `X.Y.Z` (no suffix) commit
  ships, decide which prior entries are no longer needed for nearby
  context and migrate them.
- **Opening a new ladder** — at `X.Y.Z-0`, do the same sweep before
  bumping the version.

Migration mechanics:

- Move the bullet itself from `todo.md > ## Done` to
  `done.md` (preserving the original ref number).
- Copy any references the moved entries cite into
  `done.md`'s `# References` section (those refs are file-local,
  so coexisting with `todo.md`'s namespace is fine).
- Prune any references in `todo.md > # References` no longer cited
  by anything in `## In Progress` / `## Todo` / `## Done`. This
  frees the numbers for future reuse.

lines: By default there are chores-*.md and todo.md. Chores are general notes
about tasks and todo.md contains short term tasks and their status.
+The chores-NN files are numbered in sequence; older ones are closed
+(chores-09.md and earlier — through the 0.48.x cycles), and the
+also carries the **Migration A / Migration B live-tracking** tables.
-split, the subcommand model, and the in-flight migrations — lives
+generic module map, the subcommand model, and what Migration A / B
+*are* (the *live status* is in chores-10.md) — lives at the repo
In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
database to better manage the information, TBD.

- **`ARCHITECTURE.md`** (~290 → ~150 lines): keeps the Overview, the two-layer (CLI-args vs `Context`+`Params`) section + rules + rationale + Naming, a *generic* module map (the kinds of module and their roles — no per-subcommand `| Module | Subcommand | Notes |` table, no version annotations), the Subcommand-model recipe, See-also. Carries the `## Migration A / Migration B — live tracking` section (the two per-subcommand status tables that came out of `ARCHITECTURE.md`), the `0.49.0-0` and `0.49.0-1` sections moved out of `chores-09.md` (the `0.49.0-0` Commits ref `[[1]]` backfilled → commit `10788bd1`), and a `## docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; chores-10 (0.49.0-1.2)` section recording this. message.content: I'm concerned about the "value-field clash", the rename using "id" works, but it's not obvious to me when "id" is used and when "value" is used and maybe we're heading down a dead end. Also, in CommonOptionFlagBundle we have "plain values" pos_rev, pos_count and repos, plus "typed" values, revision, limit and repo_label. And on RepoLabelOption couldn't this have just one `label: Option<string>,` or maybe an empty string is not used?

lastPrompt: I'm concerned about the "value-field clash", the rename using "id" works, but it's not obvious to me when "id" is used and when "value" is used and maybe we're heading down a dead end. Also, in Common…

content.text:
Those are all fair — and I think they point at the same root cause: `0.49.0-1.1` decomposed `CommonArgs` into single-field leaves (`revision`, `commit_limit`) that buy *zero* reuse, and that decomposition is what dragged in the `value`/`id` awkwardness and the mixed-shape bundle. My take on each:

**1. `value` vs `id` — sharp edge, and not worth it here.** The `value`-field convention (`squash.rs`) is fine when exactly one such leaf sits in a struct — clap derives the arg *id* from the field name, so a lone `value` field is unambiguous. The moment you flatten *two* `value` leaves, the ids collide and you need `#[arg(id=…)]` to disambiguate. So the rule would be "field is `value`; if it can be flattened next to another `value` leaf, also give it an explicit `id`" — which is exactly the "not obvious when" you're flagging. For `revision`/`commit_limit` this paid nothing: no second consumer, so they're not "defined once" any more than they would be as inline fields of the shared bundle. Not a dead end for `squash.rs`'s case, but the wrong tool here.

**2. The bundle mixes inline (`pos_rev`/`pos_count`/`repos`) and leaf-flattened (`revision`/`limit`/`repo_label`) fields** — a symptom of (1). The fix: don't decompose at all. `CommonArgs` becomes a shared `#[derive(Args)]` struct relocated into `options_flags/` (all 7 fields inline, no sub-leaves) — uniform, no `value`/`id`, and it still satisfies "the shared arg set lives in `options_flags/`, defined once". (`options_flags/` then has three shapes: leaves, bundles-of-leaves like `provision_bundle`, and shared-args groups like this — worth a README note. Or keep it a "bundle" in name; bikeshed.)

**3. `RepoLabelOptions` → one `label: Option<String>`?** Doesn't quite work in clap-derive: there's no clean way for `--no-label` to write `None` into a `label: Option<String>` field, and `--label` alone never yields `None` — so you'd still end up with a `no_label: bool` companion. `label: String` (default `===`) + `no_label: bool` is the natural "value flag + its negation flag" shape; `resolve_header` combines them. (`--label ""` is *technically* distinct from `--no-label` — empty-decoration header line vs no header at all — though obscure. And re "is empty string used": not as a default, but a user could pass it; the `String` field allows it.) A *possible future CLI simplification* is dropping `-L`/`--no-label` and using `-l ""` for "no header" — but that's a behavior change, not part of this refactor.

So I'd propose: **amend `0.49.0-1.1` (and the matching bits of `0.49.0-1.2`'s `chores-10.md`)** to drop the three leaf files — `CommonArgs` just relocates into `options_flags/` (e.g. `options_flags/common_args.rs`, fields inline), `chid` flattens it, the `### value-field clash` chores subsection and the `options_flags/README.md` `id`-caveat note go away. Then `0.49.0-1.3/.4/.5` migrate `desc`/`list`/`show` onto the relocated struct, `0.49.0-1.5` removes `src/common.rs`'s `CommonArgs`, and `0.49.0-2` swaps the inline `repos` for `--scope` as before.

That's a two-commit amend (`0.49.0-1.1` is `@--`, `0.49.0-1.2` is `@-`; jj rebases the latter over the former). Want me to do that? And — keep the name `CommonOptionFlagBundle` in `common_bundle.rs`, or go `CommonArgs` in `common_args.rs`?

The statement "The fix: don't decompose at all". Are you saying we should only have the notiion of "Bundles" and only if two or more subcommands have the exact same set of arguments would anything be shared?

content.text:
Not quite — I'm not saying drop the leaf concept. Leaves and bundles-of-leaves stay; they're earning their keep (`init` is fully leaf-composed; `provision_bundle` flattens `dry_run`/`private`/`push_retry`, and each of those *is* independently meaningful — `--dry-run` will land on `clone`, etc.).

The distinction I'm drawing is about *granularity vs. actual reuse*:

- **Make a leaf when that specific flag / small group is genuinely shared at that granularity** — `--dry-run`, `--squash`, `--scope`, `--account`, `--push-retries`+`--push-retry-delay`. Those show up (or will) on commands that *don't* share the rest of each other's args, so a per-flag leaf is the unit of sharing.
- **When a whole *set* of args is shared by N commands but the individual args inside it aren't reused anywhere else, the right shape is one shared `#[derive(Args)]` struct with inline fields** — not N per-flag leaves flattened into it. Atomizing it preemptively (a `revision.rs` leaf nobody else composes) buys nothing and drags in the `value`/`id` friction. That's the README's "Layout note" ("Mechanical when needed; not done preemptively") applied at flag granularity.

And sharing isn't only "exact same set":
- `init` / `clone` share a *sub-set* (the provision leaves) + each has its own args → leaves + a bundle of them.
- `chid` / `desc` share *exactly* `CommonArgs`; `list` adds `--width`, `show` adds `--files` → they flatten the shared struct **plus** their own extras. So `CommonArgs` is the shared whole-set; the extras stay per-command.

So `CommonArgs` → one shared struct in `options_flags/`, fields inline, flattened by all four (`list`/`show` adding their extra `#[arg]` alongside). Call it a "bundle" or a "shared args group" — either label is fine; the README documents the shape. If some future command wants just `--revision`, *then* extract a `revision` leaf — when the need is real.

So my proposal stands: amend `0.49.0-1.1` (+ the matching `chores-10` bits in `0.49.0-1.2`) to drop the three leaf files — `CommonArgs` relocates into `options_flags/` with inline fields, `chid` flattens it, the `value`-field-clash subsection and the README `id`-caveat go away. Then `-1.3/.4/.5` migrate `desc`/`list`/`show`, `-1.5` removes `src/common.rs`'s `CommonArgs`, `0.49.0-2` does `--scope`.

Go ahead with that amend? (And: `CommonArgs` in `options_flags/common_args.rs`, or keep `CommonOptionFlagBundle` in `common_bundle.rs`?)

`CommonArgs` would compose the extracted leaf — **one definition**. When `--revision` becomes a leaf (`RevisionOption`), `CommonArgs` switches `pub revision: String` (inline) → `#[command(flatten)] pub revision: RevisionOption` (the composed leaf), and the new subcommand also flattens `RevisionOption`. No duplication — that's the point of extracting. At that point `CommonArgs` becomes a hybrid (some inline fields, some composed leaves) — which is fine *when the extraction is justified by real reuse*, unlike `0.49.0-1.1` where it wasn't (the mix there was the symptom).
