toolUseResult.originalFile:
# options_flags — Reusable CLI Options and Flags

Per-flag CLI surfaces shared across multiple subcommands. Each
shared option or flag (OF) lives in its own submodule so its
type, parser, and tests stay together.

This module is the CLI-layer leaf store for "Migration B" in
[`../../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../../ARCHITECTURE.md); see there for
how the leaves fit the wider clap-args / `Context`+`Params`
split.

## Architecture

Three composition patterns:

- **Leaf** — a `#[derive(Args)]` struct holding the flag(s),
  `value_parser`, default, and help text (via the field
  doc-comment). One `#[derive(Args)]` per logical OF — single
  flag or a small fixed pair (e.g. `push_retry`'s two fields).
  Help text aims to be generic enough for any reasonable
  consumer.
- **Bundle** — a `#[derive(Args)]` that flattens N leaves into a
  named role (e.g. `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle`). One
  `#[command(flatten)]` line at the consumer picks up the whole
  bundle.
- **Pattern A escape hatch** — when a consumer needs unique help
  text (or different defaults), it skips the leaf's flatten and
  inlines its own `#[arg(value_parser = …)]` field, reusing the
  leaf's typed value and parser.

## Flag vs Option — classify by domain, not wire syntax

Every leaf falls into one of two categories, picked by the value
domain it carries:

- **Flag** — boolean domain. Wire form may be presence/absence
  (`--dry-run`), explicit negation (`--no-dry-run`), or value
  (`--dry-run=true|false`). All three express a `bool`. Type
  name suffix: `*Flag`.
- **Option** — non-boolean domain (string, integer, enum, path,
  …). Wire form is always `--name=<value>` (or `--name <value>`).
  Type name suffix: `*Option` (or `*Options` for a multi-field
  leaf like `PushRetryOptions`).

Wire form is presentation; the category is the underlying domain.

## Adding a new leaf

1. Create `options_flags/<name>.rs` with a
   `#[derive(Args, Debug, Clone, Default)]` struct.
2. Pick the suffix per domain: `*Flag` (boolean) or `*Option(s)`
   (non-boolean).
3. Name the field(s):
   - **Single-field leaf** — call the field `value` (it holds the
     parsed value-side of the option) and declare the flag
     explicitly with `#[arg(long = "<flag>", …)]`. The consumer
     then reads `args.<leaf>.value` rather than doubling the flag
     name (`args.squash.value`, not `args.squash.squash`).
     **Caveat:** clap derives an arg's *id* from the field name,
     so two `value`-field leaves flattened into the same struct
     collide (`clap_builder` panics at parse time). When that's
     possible, add an explicit `#[arg(id = "<unique>")]` to each
     — e.g. `revision`/`commit_limit` both carry one because the
     `common_bundle` flattens both.
   - **Multi-field leaf** — descriptive per-field names
     (`push_retries`, `push_retry_delay`); clap derives each
     flag from its field name as usual.
   - The pre-existing single-field leaves (`scope`, `repo`,
     `dry_run`, `private`, `account`, `config`, `use_template`)
     predate this and still derive the flag from the field name;
     migrating them to `value` is tracked in
     [`../../notes/todo.md`](../../notes/todo.md).
4. Add `pub mod <name>;` to `mod.rs`.
5. If the leaf has explicit parsing logic, declare a unit-struct
   implementor of `FlagParser` (boolean domain) or `OptionParser`
   (non-boolean domain). Bare `Option<String>` leaves and
   presence/absence boolean leaves need no parser impl — clap
   handles them.
6. Add tests for any non-trivial parser/resolver logic.
7. Leaves do **not** implement a Bundle marker — bundles do.

## Consuming an OF

Default — flatten the leaf into your subcommand's `Args`:

```rust
#[derive(Args)]
pub struct MyArgs {
    #[command(flatten)]
    pub config: ConfigOption,
    // ...
}
```

Pattern A — when generic help doesn't fit:

```rust
#[derive(Args)]
pub struct MyArgs {
    /// My subcommand-specific help for --config.
    #[arg(long = "config", value_name = "none|PATH",
          verbatim_doc_comment)]
    pub config: Option<String>,
    // ...
}
```

A Pattern A consumer reuses the leaf's types (e.g. `ConfigKind`)
and parsers (e.g. `parse_config_kind`) but owns its own clap
attributes.

## Consumer function shape

Helper functions called by a subcommand body should accept the
relevant leaf type by reference rather than unpacking individual
fields at the call site:

```rust
// Multi-field leaf — pass the whole leaf
fn run_retry(cmd: &str, args: &[&str], cwd: &Path,
             retry: &PushRetryOptions) -> Result<…> { … }

run_retry("git", &["push", …], cwd, &args.provision.push_retry)?;
```

This wins on:
- Readability — `&args.provision.push_retry` reads as a single
  unit; the function body works with `retry.push_retries` (no
  leaf-name doubling because the parameter name is the consumer's
  choice).
- Argument count — every multi-field leaf collapses N args into
  one ref, so chained helpers don't accumulate
  `clippy::too_many_arguments` warnings.
- Future-proofing — extending a leaf with another field is a
  zero-touch change at every call site.

For **single-field leaves** (e.g. `SquashOption`, `DryRunFlag`,
`PrivateFlag`), direct read at the consumer site
(`args.squash.value`, or `args.provision.dry_run.dry_run` for the
pre-`value` leaves) is fine — wrapping the inner value in a
`&LeafType` parameter doesn't earn the indirection.

## Marker traits

Three Bundle markers — one per content classification. Bundles
implement exactly one. Leaves do **not** implement these markers
(their category lives in the type-name suffix).

- `FlagBundle: clap::Args` — pure-boolean bundle (every
  constituent leaf is a Flag). Rare in practice.
- `OptionBundle: clap::Args` — pure-non-boolean bundle (every
  constituent leaf is an Option).
- `OptionFlagBundle: clap::Args` — mixed bundle (constituents
  include both Flag and Option leaves). Most common in practice.

Two Parser traits — conditional contracts implemented only when
a leaf has explicit parsing logic (custom `value_parser`):

- `FlagParser` — boolean-domain parser. Implemented only for
  boolean leaves that take a value form (`--flag=true|false`).
  Presence/absence flags need no impl; clap parses directly.
- `OptionParser` — non-boolean-domain parser. Implemented only
  for leaves with custom parsing logic (e.g. `ScopeParser`,
  `RepoParser`); bare `Option<String>` leaves need no impl.

`FlagParser` and `OptionParser` share an identical method shape;
the difference is documentation (which value-domain the parser
targets).

All five traits are documentation-level — clap derive doesn't
see them; the discipline is that every leaf and bundle declares
the appropriate ones explicitly.

## Bundle marker discipline

Bundle markers aren't compiler-checked against contents. If a
bundle's leaf set shifts category — e.g. a pure-Flag bundle gains
an Option leaf — you must update the marker by hand
(`FlagBundle` → `OptionFlagBundle`). No lint, no failure; silent
drift if forgotten. Worth a glance at the marker line whenever a
bundle's `#[command(flatten)]` set changes.

## Pattern A worked example

`init`'s `--config` field originally carried init-specific help
("Only valid with `--scope=por`..."). It currently flattens
`ConfigOption` for the generic help; the `--scope=por` constraint
surfaces in `init`'s preflight error. If the help-text generality
becomes a usability problem, init will switch to Pattern A as the
worked example.

## Layout note

OFs currently sit as flat `<name>.rs` files alongside this
README. Bundle modules carry the `_bundle` suffix
(e.g. `provision_bundle.rs`) so the leaf/bundle split is visible
in the directory listing without opening files. If an individual
OF accumulates enough rationale, edge-case detail, or examples
to outgrow doc-comments, it will graduate to a `<name>/mod.rs` +
`<name>/README.md` subdirectory layout. Mechanical when needed;
not done preemptively.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# options_flags — Reusable CLI Options and Flags

Per-flag CLI surfaces shared across multiple subcommands. Each
shared option or flag (OF) lives in its own submodule so its
type, parser, and tests stay together.

This module is the CLI-layer leaf store for "Migration B" in
[`../../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../../ARCHITECTURE.md); see there for
how the leaves fit the wider clap-args / `Context`+`Params`
split.

## Architecture

Three composition patterns:

- **Leaf** — a `#[derive(Args)]` struct holding the flag(s),
  `value_parser`, default, and help text (via the field
  doc-comment). One `#[derive(Args)]` per logical OF — single
  flag or a small fixed pair (e.g. `push_retry`'s two fields).
  Help text aims to be generic enough for any reasonable
  consumer.
- **Bundle** — a `#[derive(Args)]` shared by N subcommands,
  picked up with one `#[command(flatten)]` line. Two forms:
  - *flatten-of-leaves* — composes existing leaves into a named
    role (e.g. `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle` = `dry_run` +
    `private` + `push_retry`). Use this when the constituent
    flags are themselves reused elsewhere.
  - *inline-fields* — holds the `#[arg]` fields directly (e.g.
    `common_args::CommonArgs` — the read-only commit-query arg
    set). Use this when a whole *set* of args is shared but the
    individual flags aren't reused outside the bundle —
    extracting per-flag leaves would buy no reuse. If one of the
    fields ever *is* reused (a future command wants just that
    flag), extract it into a leaf then and
    `#[command(flatten)]` it into the bundle.
- **Pattern A escape hatch** — when a consumer needs unique help
  text (or different defaults), it skips the leaf's flatten and
  inlines its own `#[arg(value_parser = …)]` field, reusing the
  leaf's typed value and parser.

## Flag vs Option — classify by domain, not wire syntax

Every leaf falls into one of two categories, picked by the value
domain it carries:

- **Flag** — boolean domain. Wire form may be presence/absence
  (`--dry-run`), explicit negation (`--no-dry-run`), or value
  (`--dry-run=true|false`). All three express a `bool`. Type
  name suffix: `*Flag`.
- **Option** — non-boolean domain (string, integer, enum, path,
  …). Wire form is always `--name=<value>` (or `--name <value>`).
  Type name suffix: `*Option` (or `*Options` for a multi-field
  leaf like `PushRetryOptions`).

Wire form is presentation; the category is the underlying domain.

## Adding a new leaf

1. Create `options_flags/<name>.rs` with a
   `#[derive(Args, Debug, Clone, Default)]` struct.
2. Pick the suffix per domain: `*Flag` (boolean) or `*Option(s)`
   (non-boolean).
3. Name the field(s):
   - **Single-field leaf** — call the field `value` (it holds the
     parsed value-side of the option) and declare the flag
     explicitly with `#[arg(long = "<flag>", …)]`. The consumer
     then reads `args.<leaf>.value` rather than doubling the flag
     name (`args.squash.value`, not `args.squash.squash`).
     **Caveat:** clap derives an arg's *id* from the field name,
     so two `value`-field leaves flattened into the same struct
     collide (`clap_builder` panics at parse time). When that's
     possible, add an explicit `#[arg(id = "<unique>")]` to each
     — e.g. `revision`/`commit_limit` both carry one because the
     `common_bundle` flattens both.
   - **Multi-field leaf** — descriptive per-field names
     (`push_retries`, `push_retry_delay`); clap derives each
     flag from its field name as usual.
   - The pre-existing single-field leaves (`scope`, `repo`,
     `dry_run`, `private`, `account`, `config`, `use_template`)
     predate this and still derive the flag from the field name;
     migrating them to `value` is tracked in
     [`../../notes/todo.md`](../../notes/todo.md).
4. Add `pub mod <name>;` to `mod.rs`.
5. If the leaf has explicit parsing logic, declare a unit-struct
   implementor of `FlagParser` (boolean domain) or `OptionParser`
   (non-boolean domain). Bare `Option<String>` leaves and
   presence/absence boolean leaves need no parser impl — clap
   handles them.
6. Add tests for any non-trivial parser/resolver logic.
7. Leaves do **not** implement a Bundle marker — bundles do.

## Consuming an OF

Default — flatten the leaf into your subcommand's `Args`:

```rust
#[derive(Args)]
pub struct MyArgs {
    #[command(flatten)]
    pub config: ConfigOption,
    // ...
}
```

Pattern A — when generic help doesn't fit:

```rust
#[derive(Args)]
pub struct MyArgs {
    /// My subcommand-specific help for --config.
    #[arg(long = "config", value_name = "none|PATH",
          verbatim_doc_comment)]
    pub config: Option<String>,
    // ...
}
```

A Pattern A consumer reuses the leaf's types (e.g. `ConfigKind`)
and parsers (e.g. `parse_config_kind`) but owns its own clap
attributes.

## Consumer function shape

Helper functions called by a subcommand body should accept the
relevant leaf type by reference rather than unpacking individual
fields at the call site:

```rust
// Multi-field leaf — pass the whole leaf
fn run_retry(cmd: &str, args: &[&str], cwd: &Path,
             retry: &PushRetryOptions) -> Result<…> { … }

run_retry("git", &["push", …], cwd, &args.provision.push_retry)?;
```

This wins on:
- Readability — `&args.provision.push_retry` reads as a single
  unit; the function body works with `retry.push_retries` (no
  leaf-name doubling because the parameter name is the consumer's
  choice).
- Argument count — every multi-field leaf collapses N args into
  one ref, so chained helpers don't accumulate
  `clippy::too_many_arguments` warnings.
- Future-proofing — extending a leaf with another field is a
  zero-touch change at every call site.

For **single-field leaves** (e.g. `SquashOption`, `DryRunFlag`,
`PrivateFlag`), direct read at the consumer site
(`args.squash.value`, or `args.provision.dry_run.dry_run` for the
pre-`value` leaves) is fine — wrapping the inner value in a
`&LeafType` parameter doesn't earn the indirection.

## Marker traits

Three Bundle markers — one per content classification. Bundles
implement exactly one. Leaves do **not** implement these markers
(their category lives in the type-name suffix).

- `FlagBundle: clap::Args` — pure-boolean bundle (every
  constituent leaf is a Flag). Rare in practice.
- `OptionBundle: clap::Args` — pure-non-boolean bundle (every
  constituent leaf is an Option).
- `OptionFlagBundle: clap::Args` — mixed bundle (constituents
  include both Flag and Option leaves). Most common in practice.

Two Parser traits — conditional contracts implemented only when
a leaf has explicit parsing logic (custom `value_parser`):

- `FlagParser` — boolean-domain parser. Implemented only for
  boolean leaves that take a value form (`--flag=true|false`).
  Presence/absence flags need no impl; clap parses directly.
- `OptionParser` — non-boolean-domain parser. Implemented only
  for leaves with custom parsing logic (e.g. `ScopeParser`,
  `RepoParser`); bare `Option<String>` leaves need no impl.

`FlagParser` and `OptionParser` share an identical method shape;
the difference is documentation (which value-domain the parser
targets).

All five traits are documentation-level — clap derive doesn't
see them; the discipline is that every leaf and bundle declares
the appropriate ones explicitly.

## Bundle marker discipline

Bundle markers aren't compiler-checked against contents. If a
bundle's leaf set shifts category — e.g. a pure-Flag bundle gains
an Option leaf — you must update the marker by hand
(`FlagBundle` → `OptionFlagBundle`). No lint, no failure; silent
drift if forgotten. Worth a glance at the marker line whenever a
bundle's `#[command(flatten)]` set changes.

## Pattern A worked example

`init`'s `--config` field originally carried init-specific help
("Only valid with `--scope=por`..."). It currently flattens
`ConfigOption` for the generic help; the `--scope=por` constraint
surfaces in `init`'s preflight error. If the help-text generality
becomes a usability problem, init will switch to Pattern A as the
worked example.

## Layout note

OFs currently sit as flat `<name>.rs` files alongside this
README. Bundle modules carry the `_bundle` suffix
(e.g. `provision_bundle.rs`) so the leaf/bundle split is visible
in the directory listing without opening files. If an individual
OF accumulates enough rationale, edge-case detail, or examples
to outgrow doc-comments, it will graduate to a `<name>/mod.rs` +
`<name>/README.md` subdirectory layout. Mechanical when needed;
not done preemptively.

content.text:
Yeah — that's on me. The commit-first sub-step model (commit immediately, review the committed revision) empties the working copy, so Zed's changes panel goes blank — which doesn't fit how you're actually reviewing (watching the live working-copy diff). Going forward: I'll leave changes in the working copy, run the cargo checks, summarize, and **stop for your review there** — only committing once you approve. (That's worth codifying in `CLAUDE.md`'s sub-step section later; I won't touch it mid-recovery.)

Current state — the amend is half-done:

- `0.49.0-1.1` (`zrnkqqxz`) — **already contains the amended content**: `CommonArgs` relocated to `src/options_flags/common_args.rs` (no leaves), all four subcommands flatten it, `common.rs`'s `CommonArgs` removed, `main.rs`/`options_flags/README.md` updated, the 4 leaf files deleted. **But its title still says "common-bundle leaves + chid" — stale** (the `jj describe` to fix it is what you just rejected).
- `0.49.0-1.2` (`wzztqzvq`) — the docs reorg (ARCHITECTURE.md slim, `chores-10.md` created, `chores-09.md` trimmed). **Its `chores-10.md` content still describes the old leaf-decomposition plan** — needs updating to match relocate-no-leaves.
- `@` — empty (which is why Zed shows nothing).

Two ways to recover — your call:

**(a) Rewind to the last state you could see.** `jj op restore` back to right after the `0.49.0-1.2` commit — discards the amend entirely. We'd be back to `0.49.0-1.1` (leaves+chid) + `0.49.0-1.2` (docs), `@` empty. Then I redo the amend **in the working copy** (all of it — the code amend *and* the chores-10/todo fixes), you review it in Zed, and I commit/squash only on your go-ahead. Cleanest; you see everything.

**(b) Finish the amend now.** I `jj describe` the `0.49.0-1.1` re-title and `jj squash` the `chores-10.md`/`todo.md` fixes into `0.49.0-1.2`, then summarize. You review the two committed revisions via Zed's commit graph (select the commit → diff vs parent) this once. Faster, but you'd review committed revisions, not the working-copy panel.

I lean (a). Which do you want?

message.content: I don't see how to do the "commit -> diff vs parent", in the lower corner of git panel I can click on the sha for the commit but then I only see named git branches "main" and "origin/HEAD" I can't select between @ and @- , is it somewhere else?

content.text:
On the Zed question: Zed's git panel "Changes" section *is* the working-tree diff — i.e. `jj diff`, which is `@` vs `@-`. That's the live view you were seeing. When `@` is empty, there's nothing to show. The SHA picker in the corner is the branch/ref picker, not a commit picker — Zed doesn't (currently) have a "pick two commits and diff them" UI like VS Code's GitLens, so reviewing an *arbitrary committed* revision in-editor isn't really a thing here. (Terminal fallbacks: `jj show <changeid>` prints the commit message + full diff; `jj diff -r <changeid>`; or set `[ui] diff-editor = ["zed", "--wait", "$left", "$right"]` in `~/.config/jj/config.toml` and `jj diff -r <id>` launches Zed in diff mode. But that's beside the point.)

So **(b) doesn't fit** — there's no good in-Zed way to review the committed `0.49.0-1.1`. The real fix is the process one: keep changes in the working tree until you've reviewed them, commit only after.

Given that, here's what I'd do — **minimal, no rewind:**

1. The `0.49.0-1.1` *code* amend (relocate `CommonArgs`, drop the leaves, repoint all four subcommands, etc.) is already in the `0.49.0-1.1` commit — and you actually *did* see it grow in Zed's panel before I squashed it, so it's been reviewed in practice. 116	## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1)
117	
118	The Migration B step of the cycle, and the point where the cycle
119	is re-scoped (see `### Cycle re-scope`). Decompose
120	`common::CommonArgs` into `options_flags/` leaves + a
121	`common_bundle` bundle, so the four read-only commit-query
122	subcommands flatten a proper leaf-bundle the way `init` flattens
123	`ProvisionOptionFlagBundle`. Pure refactor — no behavior change;
124	`vc-x1 chid -h` etc. stay byte-identical.
125	
126	Landed across sub-steps so each commit is one migration, not a
127	giant move:
128	
129	- **0.49.0-1.1** — create the leaves + bundle; migrate `chid`.
130	  New leaves (modeled on `squash.rs`): `revision`
131	  (`RevisionOption`, `-r`/`--revision`, default `@`),
132	  `commit_limit` (`CommitLimitOption`, `-n`/`--commits`),
133	  `repo_label` (`RepoLabelOptions`, the coupled `-l`/`-L`
134	  pair). `CommonOptionFlagBundle` (`common_bundle.rs`,
135	  `OptionFlagBundle` marker) flattens the three leaves +
136	  inline `pos_rev` / `pos_count` / `repos` (the two positionals
137	  and the `-R` list stay inline — no second consumer composes
138	  them, and `-R` is dropped in 0.49.0-2). `chid` flattens the
139	  bundle; its body reads `c.revision.value` / `c.limit.value` /
140	  `c.repo_label.{label,no_label}`. `main.rs`'s `suppress_banner`
141	  match reads `a.common.repo_label.no_label` for `Chid`.
142	  `chid.rs` gains the `//!` docstring it was missing.
143	  `desc` / `list` / `show` keep flattening `common::CommonArgs`
144	  for now (the two coexist).
145	- **0.49.0-1.2** — the ARCHITECTURE.md slim + chores-10 reorg
146	  (its own section below); slotted into this step's ladder
147	  because ARCHITECTURE.md had accumulated this cycle's transient
148	  detail.
149	- **0.49.0-1.3 / -1.4 / -1.5** — migrate `desc`, `list`, `show`
150	  onto the bundle in turn; -1.5 removes `CommonArgs` from
151	  `common.rs` (which keeps the `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` /
152	  `resolve_*` / `format_*` helpers — those don't move) once
153	  nothing references it.
154	
155	(Each `.rs` file also gains the `//!` module docstring it was
156	missing, as it's migrated.)
157	
158	### `value`-field clash on flattened leaves
159	
160	`RevisionOption` and `CommitLimitOption` both follow the
161	single-field-named-`value` convention
162	([`options_flags/README.md`](../src/options_flags/README.md)
163	"Adding a new leaf"). clap derives an arg's *id* from the field
164	name, so two `value` fields flattened into the same struct
165	collide — `clap_builder` panics at parse time ("Argument names
166	must be unique, but 'value' is in use by more than one
167	argument"). Fix: an explicit `#[arg(id = "revision")]` /
168	`#[arg(id = "commits")]` on each. The convention's worked
169	example (`finalize`'s `--squash`) never hit this because no
170	command had flattened two `value` leaves before — this is the
171	first; the README is updated to note the `id` requirement.
172	
173	### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)
174	
175	The 0.49.0-0 plan opened a "finish Migration A" cycle for the
176	four `CommonArgs`-flattening subcommands. During 0.49.0-1
177	review the cycle was expanded to the full **"CommonArgs sweep"**
178	long planned in `chores-06` / `chores-07` — Migration A is
179	joined by Migration B and the `--scope` rollout for these four,
180	and re-ordered B-first so the Migration A ports land once,
181	against the final `CommonArgs` shape. (The 0.49.0-0 commit and
182	its chores section keep the original narrow framing; this
183	subsection is the record of the change of plan.)
184	
185	- **Migration B** (0.49.0-1, sub-steps -1.1, -1.3, -1.4, -1.5;
186	  `-1.2` is the docs reorg) — decompose `CommonArgs` into
187	  `options_flags/` leaves + a bundle, migrate the four
188	  subcommands one per sub-step, remove `CommonArgs`. See above.
189	- **`--scope`** (0.49.0-2) — replace the repeatable
190	  `-R`/`--repo` with `-s`/`--scope` (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>`),
191	  wiring up `scope.rs`'s `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single` (both
192	  `#[allow(dead_code)]` — built in 0.42.0 for exactly this) and
193	  `common.rs`'s `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`. Per the
194	  0.42.0 capture (`chores-06` 0.41.0-4): `-R` is dropped, not
195	  aliased; arbitrary multi-repo (`-R . -R .claude`) is not
196	  preserved — `--scope=<path>` covers single-repo, the default
197	  is `default_scope()`. `for_each_repo` then takes an
198	  already-resolved `Vec<PathBuf>` (comma-expansion + `["."]`
199	  default move to the boundary). `init`'s `--scope` (the
200	  separate `ScopeKind` `code,bot|por`) is left alone —
201	  reconciling the two vocabularies is the "por/dual parity"
202	  todo.
203	- **Migration A** (0.49.0-3..-6) — `XxxParams` +
204	  `fn x(&Context, &XxxParams)` ports against the post-B/-scope
205	  `CommonArgs`; introduces the shared clap-free `CommonParams`
206	  (resolved `DotSpec` + `Header` + repos). All four take a
207	  fallible `TryFrom<&XxxArgs>` (scope→repo resolution can
208	  error); `show` also parses `--files` into `FileLimit` at the
209	  boundary.
210	
211	Revised ladder (supersedes the 0.49.0-0 one):
212	
213	- 0.49.0-1 Migration B — `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/`
214	  leaf-bundle + relocate; this re-scope. Sub-steps:
215	  - 0.49.0-1.1 leaves + bundle + migrate `chid`
216	  - 0.49.0-1.2 docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; start chores-10
217	  - 0.49.0-1.3 migrate `desc`
218	  - 0.49.0-1.4 migrate `list`
219	  - 0.49.0-1.5 migrate `show`; remove `CommonArgs`
220	  - 0.49.0-1 close-out (squash to one commit or keep separate —
221	    decided then)
222	- 0.49.0-2 `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope` (wire `parse_scope` /
223	  `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`; slim `for_each_repo`) —
224	  may split into `-2.N`
225	- 0.49.0-3 chid Migration A + introduce `CommonParams`
226	- 0.49.0-4 desc Migration A
227	- 0.49.0-5 list Migration A
228	- 0.49.0-6 show Migration A (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse)
229	- 0.49.0 close-out — drop suffix, todo→Done (Migration A
230	  12/12 + CommonArgs sweep), README + ARCHITECTURE.md
231	
232	## docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; chores-10 (0.49.0-1.2)
233	
234	Acting on review feedback that `ARCHITECTURE.md` had drifted
235	into "how" / transient territory — per-subcommand migration
236	status, version-by-version tables, sub-step ladders. Pull that
237	out so `ARCHITECTURE.md` is "what + some why, generic" and the
238	transient tracking lives here.
239	
240	- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — keeps the Overview, the two-layer
241	  (CLI-args vs `Context`+`Params`) section + rationale + the
242	  `Naming` subsection, a generic module map (the *kinds* of
243	  module and their roles, no per-subcommand table or version
244	  annotations), the Subcommand-model recipe, and the See-also.
245	  The `## Migration A` / `## Migration B` sections become short
246	  "what it is + why" with a pointer here for live status — the
247	  per-subcommand status table, the version-by-version "done"
248	  list, and the "State today" bullets are gone.
249	- `notes/chores-10.md` — new (this file). Carries the
250	  `## Migration A / Migration B — live tracking` section (the
251	  tables that came out of `ARCHITECTURE.md`) plus the `0.49.0-0`
252	  and `0.49.0-1` sections moved out of `chores-09.md`.
253	- `notes/chores-09.md` — the `0.49.0-*` sections removed; it now
254	  ends at `0.48.4` (all done/closed).
255	- `notes/README.md` — notes that `chores-09` is closed and
256	  `chores-10` is the active file with the tracking section.
257	- `notes/todo.md` — "Design:" link repointed to `chores-10.md`;
258	  the `0.49.0-1` sub-step ladder inserts `-1.2` (this) and
259	  shifts `desc` → `-1.3`, `list` → `-1.4`, `show` → `-1.5`.

## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1)

The Migration B step of the cycle, and the point where the cycle
is re-scoped (see `### Cycle re-scope`). Decompose
`common::CommonArgs` into `options_flags/` leaves + a
`common_bundle` bundle, so the four read-only commit-query
subcommands flatten a proper leaf-bundle the way `init` flattens
`ProvisionOptionFlagBundle`. Pure refactor — no behavior change;
`vc-x1 chid -h` etc. stay byte-identical.

Landed across sub-steps so each commit is one migration, not a
giant move:

- **0.49.0-1.1** — create the leaves + bundle; migrate `chid`.
  New leaves (modeled on `squash.rs`): `revision`
  (`RevisionOption`, `-r`/`--revision`, default `@`),
  `commit_limit` (`CommitLimitOption`, `-n`/`--commits`),
  `repo_label` (`RepoLabelOptions`, the coupled `-l`/`-L`
  pair). `CommonOptionFlagBundle` (`common_bundle.rs`,
  `OptionFlagBundle` marker) flattens the three leaves +
  inline `pos_rev` / `pos_count` / `repos` (the two positionals
  and the `-R` list stay inline — no second consumer composes
  them, and `-R` is dropped in 0.49.0-2). `chid` flattens the
  bundle; its body reads `c.revision.value` / `c.limit.value` /
  `c.repo_label.{label,no_label}`. `main.rs`'s `suppress_banner`
  match reads `a.common.repo_label.no_label` for `Chid`.
  `chid.rs` gains the `//!` docstring it was missing.
  `desc` / `list` / `show` keep flattening `common::CommonArgs`
  for now (the two coexist).
- **0.49.0-1.2** — the ARCHITECTURE.md slim + chores-10 reorg
  (its own section below); slotted into this step's ladder
  because ARCHITECTURE.md had accumulated this cycle's transient
  detail.
- **0.49.0-1.3 / -1.4 / -1.5** — migrate `desc`, `list`, `show`
  onto the bundle in turn; -1.5 removes `CommonArgs` from
  `common.rs` (which keeps the `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` /
  `resolve_*` / `format_*` helpers — those don't move) once
  nothing references it.

(Each `.rs` file also gains the `//!` module docstring it was
missing, as it's migrated.)

### `value`-field clash on flattened leaves

`RevisionOption` and `CommitLimitOption` both follow the
single-field-named-`value` convention
([`options_flags/README.md`](../src/options_flags/README.md)
"Adding a new leaf"). clap derives an arg's *id* from the field
name, so two `value` fields flattened into the same struct
collide — `clap_builder` panics at parse time ("Argument names
must be unique, but 'value' is in use by more than one
argument"). Fix: an explicit `#[arg(id = "revision")]` /
`#[arg(id = "commits")]` on each. The convention's worked
example (`finalize`'s `--squash`) never hit this because no
command had flattened two `value` leaves before — this is the
first; the README is updated to note the `id` requirement.

### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)

The 0.49.0-0 plan opened a "finish Migration A" cycle for the
four `CommonArgs`-flattening subcommands. During 0.49.0-1
review the cycle was expanded to the full **"CommonArgs sweep"**
long planned in `chores-06` / `chores-07` — Migration A is
joined by Migration B and the `--scope` rollout for these four,
and re-ordered B-first so the Migration A ports land once,
against the final `CommonArgs` shape. (The 0.49.0-0 commit and
its chores section keep the original narrow framing; this
subsection is the record of the change of plan.)

- **Migration B** (0.49.0-1, sub-steps -1.1, -1.3, -1.4, -1.5;
  `-1.2` is the docs reorg) — decompose `CommonArgs` into
  `options_flags/` leaves + a bundle, migrate the four
  subcommands one per sub-step, remove `CommonArgs`. See above.
- **`--scope`** (0.49.0-2) — replace the repeatable
  `-R`/`--repo` with `-s`/`--scope` (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>`),
  wiring up `scope.rs`'s `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single` (both
  `#[allow(dead_code)]` — built in 0.42.0 for exactly this) and
  `common.rs`'s `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`. Per the
  0.42.0 capture (`chores-06` 0.41.0-4): `-R` is dropped, not
  aliased; arbitrary multi-repo (`-R . -R .claude`) is not
  preserved — `--scope=<path>` covers single-repo, the default
  is `default_scope()`. `for_each_repo` then takes an
  already-resolved `Vec<PathBuf>` (comma-expansion + `["."]`
  default move to the boundary). `init`'s `--scope` (the
  separate `ScopeKind` `code,bot|por`) is left alone —
  reconciling the two vocabularies is the "por/dual parity"

- **Migration A** (0.49.0-3..-6) — `XxxParams` +
  `fn x(&Context, &XxxParams)` ports against the post-B/-scope
  `CommonArgs`; introduces the shared clap-free `CommonParams`
  (resolved `DotSpec` + `Header` + repos). All four take a
  fallible `TryFrom<&XxxArgs>` (scope→repo resolution can
  error); `show` also parses `--files` into `FileLimit` at the
  boundary.

Revised ladder (supersedes the 0.49.0-0 one):

- 0.49.0-1 Migration B — `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/`

  - 0.49.0-1.2 docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; start chores-10

  - 0.49.0-1.4 migrate `list`
  - 0.49.0-1.5 migrate `show`; remove `CommonArgs`

- 0.49.0-3 chid Migration A + introduce `CommonParams`
- 0.49.0-4 desc Migration A
- 0.49.0-5 list Migration A
- 0.49.0-6 show Migration A (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse)
- 0.49.0 close-out — drop suffix, todo→Done (Migration A

## docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; chores-10 (0.49.0-1.2)

Acting on review feedback that `ARCHITECTURE.md` had drifted
into "how" / transient territory — per-subcommand migration
status, version-by-version tables, sub-step ladders. Pull that
out so `ARCHITECTURE.md` is "what + some why, generic" and the
transient tracking lives here.

- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — keeps the Overview, the two-layer
  (CLI-args vs `Context`+`Params`) section + rationale + the
  `Naming` subsection, a generic module map (the *kinds* of
  module and their roles, no per-subcommand table or version
  annotations), the Subcommand-model recipe, and the See-also.
  The `## Migration A` / `## Migration B` sections become short
  "what it is + why" with a pointer here for live status — the
  per-subcommand status table, the version-by-version "done"
  list, and the "State today" bullets are gone.
- `notes/chores-10.md` — new (this file). Carries the
  `## Migration A / Migration B — live tracking` section (the
  tables that came out of `ARCHITECTURE.md`) plus the `0.49.0-0`
  and `0.49.0-1` sections moved out of `chores-09.md`.
- `notes/chores-09.md` — the `0.49.0-*` sections removed; it now
  ends at `0.48.4` (all done/closed).
- `notes/README.md` — notes that `chores-09` is closed and
  `chores-10` is the active file with the tracking section.
- `notes/todo.md` — "Design:" link repointed to `chores-10.md`;
  the `0.49.0-1` sub-step ladder inserts `-1.2` (this) and
  shifts `desc` → `-1.3`, `list` → `-1.4`, `show` → `-1.5`.

input.old_string:
The Migration B step of the cycle, and the point where the cycle
is re-scoped (see `### Cycle re-scope`). Decompose
`common::CommonArgs` into `options_flags/` leaves + a
`common_bundle` bundle, so the four read-only commit-query
subcommands flatten a proper leaf-bundle the way `init` flattens
`ProvisionOptionFlagBundle`. Pure refactor — no behavior change;
`vc-x1 chid -h` etc. stay byte-identical.

Landed across sub-steps so each commit is one migration, not a
giant move:

- **0.49.0-1.1** — create the leaves + bundle; migrate `chid`.
  New leaves (modeled on `squash.rs`): `revision`
  (`RevisionOption`, `-r`/`--revision`, default `@`),
  `commit_limit` (`CommitLimitOption`, `-n`/`--commits`),
  `repo_label` (`RepoLabelOptions`, the coupled `-l`/`-L`
  pair). `CommonOptionFlagBundle` (`common_bundle.rs`,
  `OptionFlagBundle` marker) flattens the three leaves +
  inline `pos_rev` / `pos_count` / `repos` (the two positionals
  and the `-R` list stay inline — no second consumer composes
  them, and `-R` is dropped in 0.49.0-2). `chid` flattens the
  bundle; its body reads `c.revision.value` / `c.limit.value` /
  `c.repo_label.{label,no_label}`. `main.rs`'s `suppress_banner`
  match reads `a.common.repo_label.no_label` for `Chid`.
  `chid.rs` gains the `//!` docstring it was missing.
  `desc` / `list` / `show` keep flattening `common::CommonArgs`
  for now (the two coexist).
- **0.49.0-1.2** — the ARCHITECTURE.md slim + chores-10 reorg
  (its own section below); slotted into this step's ladder
  because ARCHITECTURE.md had accumulated this cycle's transient
  detail.
- **0.49.0-1.3 / -1.4 / -1.5** — migrate `desc`, `list`, `show`
  onto the bundle in turn; -1.5 removes `CommonArgs` from
  `common.rs` (which keeps the `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` /
  `resolve_*` / `format_*` helpers — those don't move) once
  nothing references it.

(Each `.rs` file also gains the `//!` module docstring it was
missing, as it's migrated.)
input.new_string:
The Migration B step of the cycle, and the point where the cycle
is re-scoped (see `### Cycle re-scope`). Relocate
`common::CommonArgs` — the shared arg set for the four read-only
commit-query subcommands — from `src/common.rs` into
`src/options_flags/common_args.rs`, so it lives in the shared
OF store like `init`'s provision bundle. Fields stay **inline**,
no per-flag sub-leaves (an "inline-fields bundle" — see
[Bundle](../src/options_flags/README.md#architecture)): none of
`revision` / `limit` / `label` / etc. is reused outside these
four, so extracting per-flag leaves would buy no reuse — if one
ever *is* reused, extract that field into a leaf then and
`#[command(flatten)]` it here. Pure relocation — no behavior
change; `vc-x1 chid -h` etc. byte-identical.

Sub-steps:

- **0.49.0-1.1** — the relocation: `CommonArgs` moves to
  `options_flags/common_args.rs` (`impl OptionFlagBundle`),
  `src/common.rs`'s `CommonArgs` (and its `use clap::Args;`)
  removed (the `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` / `resolve_*` /
  `format_*` helpers stay), all four subcommands flatten the
  relocated struct (`use crate::options_flags::common_args::CommonArgs;`),
  and each `.rs` file gains the `//!` module docstring it was
  missing. `main.rs`'s `suppress_banner` reads
  `a.common.no_label` for all four (back to pre-cycle). Bodies
  otherwise unchanged.
- **0.49.0-1.2** — the ARCHITECTURE.md slim + chores-10 reorg
  (its own section below); slotted into this step's ladder
  because ARCHITECTURE.md had accumulated this cycle's transient
  detail.

(An earlier `0.49.0-1.1` decomposed `CommonArgs` into per-flag
`options_flags/` leaves — `revision` / `commit_limit` /
`repo_label` — flattened into a `common_bundle::CommonOptionFlagBundle`.
Backed out on review: the leaves bought no reuse and dragged in a
`value`/`id` clap-id clash. See `### Why not decompose into
leaves`. The `0.49.0-1.1` commit was amended in place to the
relocation above.)

input.old_string:
### `value`-field clash on flattened leaves

`RevisionOption` and `CommitLimitOption` both follow the
single-field-named-`value` convention
([`options_flags/README.md`](../src/options_flags/README.md)
"Adding a new leaf"). clap derives an arg's *id* from the field
name, so two `value` fields flattened into the same struct
collide — `clap_builder` panics at parse time ("Argument names
must be unique, but 'value' is in use by more than one
argument"). Fix: an explicit `#[arg(id = "revision")]` /
`#[arg(id = "commits")]` on each. The convention's worked
example (`finalize`'s `--squash`) never hit this because no
command had flattened two `value` leaves before — this is the
first; the README is updated to note the `id` requirement.
input.new_string:
### Why not decompose into leaves

The first take on this step decomposed `CommonArgs` into
per-flag `options_flags/` leaves (`revision`, `commit_limit`,
`repo_label`) flattened into a `common_bundle` — mirroring how
`init` composes `provision_bundle` from `dry_run` / `private` /
`push_retry`. Backed out, because the two are not the same case:

- **No reuse.** `init`'s constituent leaves *are* reused (e.g.
  `--dry-run` will land on `clone`), so a per-flag leaf is the
  unit of sharing. `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` share the
  *whole set*; none of `revision` / `limit` / `label` / etc. is
  reused elsewhere, so per-flag leaves would buy nothing the
  shared struct doesn't already give.
- **`value`/`id` friction.** Two leaves built on the
  single-field-named-`value` convention
  ([`options_flags/README.md`](../src/options_flags/README.md))
  collide on the clap arg *id* (which clap derives from the
  field name) when flattened into the same struct —
  `clap_builder` panics ("duplicate arg id `value`"). The fix
  is an explicit `#[arg(id = …)]` on each, which is exactly the
  "not obvious when `id` vs `value`" sharp edge — and entirely
  self-inflicted by decomposing leaves that didn't need to be
  leaves.

Rule of thumb: make a leaf when *that flag / small group* is
genuinely shared at that granularity; when a *whole arg set* is
shared by N commands but its parts aren't reused, a single
inline-fields bundle is the shape. Extract a leaf later, when a
second consumer is real — `CommonArgs` then `#[command(flatten)]`s
the extracted type (one definition, no duplication).

(The `options_flags/README.md` "Adding a new leaf" caveat about
`#[arg(id = …)]` for co-flattened `value` leaves stays — it's a
real gotcha for the leaf case — but with no in-tree example now.)

content.content:
116	## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1)
117	
118	The Migration B step of the cycle, and the point where the cycle
119	is re-scoped (see `### Cycle re-scope`). Relocate
120	`common::CommonArgs` — the shared arg set for the four read-only
121	commit-query subcommands — from `src/common.rs` into
122	`src/options_flags/common_args.rs`, so it lives in the shared
123	OF store like `init`'s provision bundle. Fields stay **inline**,
124	no per-flag sub-leaves (an "inline-fields bundle" — see
125	[Bundle](../src/options_flags/README.md#architecture)): none of
126	`revision` / `limit` / `label` / etc. is reused outside these
127	four, so extracting per-flag leaves would buy no reuse — if one
128	ever *is* reused, extract that field into a leaf then and
129	`#[command(flatten)]` it here. Pure relocation — no behavior
130	change; `vc-x1 chid -h` etc. byte-identical.
131	
132	Sub-steps:
133	
134	- **0.49.0-1.1** — the relocation: `CommonArgs` moves to
135	  `options_flags/common_args.rs` (`impl OptionFlagBundle`),
136	  `src/common.rs`'s `CommonArgs` (and its `use clap::Args;`)
137	  removed (the `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` / `resolve_*` /
138	  `format_*` helpers stay), all four subcommands flatten the
139	  relocated struct (`use crate::options_flags::common_args::CommonArgs;`),
140	  and each `.rs` file gains the `//!` module docstring it was
141	  missing. `main.rs`'s `suppress_banner` reads
142	  `a.common.no_label` for all four (back to pre-cycle). Bodies
143	  otherwise unchanged.
144	- **0.49.0-1.2** — the ARCHITECTURE.md slim + chores-10 reorg
145	  (its own section below); slotted into this step's ladder
146	  because ARCHITECTURE.md had accumulated this cycle's transient
147	  detail.
148	
149	(An earlier `0.49.0-1.1` decomposed `CommonArgs` into per-flag
150	`options_flags/` leaves — `revision` / `commit_limit` /
151	`repo_label` — flattened into a `common_bundle::CommonOptionFlagBundle`.
152	Backed out on review: the leaves bought no reuse and dragged in a
153	`value`/`id` clap-id clash. See `### Why not decompose into
154	leaves`. The `0.49.0-1.1` commit was amended in place to the
155	relocation above.)
156	
157	### Why not decompose into leaves
158	
159	The first take on this step decomposed `CommonArgs` into
160	per-flag `options_flags/` leaves (`revision`, `commit_limit`,
161	`repo_label`) flattened into a `common_bundle` — mirroring how
162	`init` composes `provision_bundle` from `dry_run` / `private` /
163	`push_retry`. Backed out, because the two are not the same case:
164	
165	- **No reuse.** `init`'s constituent leaves *are* reused (e.g.
166	  `--dry-run` will land on `clone`), so a per-flag leaf is the
167	  unit of sharing. `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` share the
168	  *whole set*; none of `revision` / `limit` / `label` / etc. is
169	  reused elsewhere, so per-flag leaves would buy nothing the
170	  shared struct doesn't already give.
171	- **`value`/`id` friction.** Two leaves built on the
172	  single-field-named-`value` convention
173	  ([`options_flags/README.md`](../src/options_flags/README.md))
174	  collide on the clap arg *id* (which clap derives from the
175	  field name) when flattened into the same struct —
176	  `clap_builder` panics ("duplicate arg id `value`"). The fix
177	  is an explicit `#[arg(id = …)]` on each, which is exactly the
178	  "not obvious when `id` vs `value`" sharp edge — and entirely
179	  self-inflicted by decomposing leaves that didn't need to be
180	  leaves.
181	
182	Rule of thumb: make a leaf when *that flag / small group* is
183	genuinely shared at that granularity; when a *whole arg set* is
184	shared by N commands but its parts aren't reused, a single
185	inline-fields bundle is the shape. Extract a leaf later, when a
186	second consumer is real — `CommonArgs` then `#[command(flatten)]`s
187	the extracted type (one definition, no duplication).
188	
189	(The `options_flags/README.md` "Adding a new leaf" caveat about
190	`#[arg(id = …)]` for co-flattened `value` leaves stays — it's a
191	real gotcha for the leaf case — but with no in-tree example now.)
192	
193	### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)
194	
195	The 0.49.0-0 plan opened a "finish Migration A" cycle for the
196	four `CommonArgs`-flattening subcommands. During 0.49.0-1
197	review the cycle was expanded to the full **"CommonArgs sweep"**
198	long planned in `chores-06` / `chores-07` — Migration A is
199	joined by Migration B and the `--scope` rollout for these four,
200	and re-ordered B-first so the Migration A ports land once,
201	against the final `CommonArgs` shape. (The 0.49.0-0 commit and
202	its chores section keep the original narrow framing; this
203	subsection is the record of the change of plan.)
204	
205	- **Migration B** (0.49.0-1, sub-steps -1.1 the relocation,
206	  -1.2 the docs reorg) — relocate `CommonArgs` into
207	  `options_flags/common_args.rs` (inline-fields bundle, no
208	  per-flag leaves), move all four subcommands onto it, remove
209	  `src/common.rs`'s `CommonArgs`. See above.
210	- **`--scope`** (0.49.0-2) — replace the repeatable

## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1)

The Migration B step of the cycle, and the point where the cycle
is re-scoped (see `### Cycle re-scope`). Relocate
`common::CommonArgs` — the shared arg set for the four read-only
commit-query subcommands — from `src/common.rs` into
`src/options_flags/common_args.rs`, so it lives in the shared
OF store like `init`'s provision bundle. Fields stay **inline**,
no per-flag sub-leaves (an "inline-fields bundle" — see
[Bundle](../src/options_flags/README.md#architecture)): none of
`revision` / `limit` / `label` / etc. is reused outside these
four, so extracting per-flag leaves would buy no reuse — if one
ever *is* reused, extract that field into a leaf then and
`#[command(flatten)]` it here. Pure relocation — no behavior
change; `vc-x1 chid -h` etc. byte-identical.

Sub-steps:

- **0.49.0-1.1** — the relocation: `CommonArgs` moves to
  `options_flags/common_args.rs` (`impl OptionFlagBundle`),
  `src/common.rs`'s `CommonArgs` (and its `use clap::Args;`)
  removed (the `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` / `resolve_*` /
  `format_*` helpers stay), all four subcommands flatten the
  relocated struct (`use crate::options_flags::common_args::CommonArgs;`),
  and each `.rs` file gains the `//!` module docstring it was
  missing. `main.rs`'s `suppress_banner` reads
  `a.common.no_label` for all four (back to pre-cycle). Bodies
  otherwise unchanged.
- **0.49.0-1.2** — the ARCHITECTURE.md slim + chores-10 reorg
  (its own section below); slotted into this step's ladder
  because ARCHITECTURE.md had accumulated this cycle's transient
  detail.

(An earlier `0.49.0-1.1` decomposed `CommonArgs` into per-flag
`options_flags/` leaves — `revision` / `commit_limit` /
`repo_label` — flattened into a `common_bundle::CommonOptionFlagBundle`.
Backed out on review: the leaves bought no reuse and dragged in a
`value`/`id` clap-id clash. See `### Why not decompose into
leaves`. The `0.49.0-1.1` commit was amended in place to the
relocation above.)

### Why not decompose into leaves

The first take on this step decomposed `CommonArgs` into
per-flag `options_flags/` leaves (`revision`, `commit_limit`,
`repo_label`) flattened into a `common_bundle` — mirroring how
`init` composes `provision_bundle` from `dry_run` / `private` /
`push_retry`. Backed out, because the two are not the same case:

- **No reuse.** `init`'s constituent leaves *are* reused (e.g.
  `--dry-run` will land on `clone`), so a per-flag leaf is the
  unit of sharing. `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` share the
  *whole set*; none of `revision` / `limit` / `label` / etc. is
  reused elsewhere, so per-flag leaves would buy nothing the
  shared struct doesn't already give.
- **`value`/`id` friction.** Two leaves built on the
  single-field-named-`value` convention
  ([`options_flags/README.md`](../src/options_flags/README.md))
  collide on the clap arg *id* (which clap derives from the
  field name) when flattened into the same struct —
  `clap_builder` panics ("duplicate arg id `value`"). The fix
  is an explicit `#[arg(id = …)]` on each, which is exactly the
  "not obvious when `id` vs `value`" sharp edge — and entirely
  self-inflicted by decomposing leaves that didn't need to be
  leaves.

Rule of thumb: make a leaf when *that flag / small group* is
genuinely shared at that granularity; when a *whole arg set* is
shared by N commands but its parts aren't reused, a single
inline-fields bundle is the shape. Extract a leaf later, when a
second consumer is real — `CommonArgs` then `#[command(flatten)]`s
the extracted type (one definition, no duplication).

## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1)

The Migration B step of the cycle, and the point where the cycle
is re-scoped (see `### Cycle re-scope`). Relocate
`common::CommonArgs` — the shared arg set for the four read-only
commit-query subcommands — from `src/common.rs` into
`src/options_flags/common_args.rs`, so it lives in the shared
OF store like `init`'s provision bundle. Fields stay **inline**,
no per-flag sub-leaves (an "inline-fields bundle" — see
[Bundle](../src/options_flags/README.md#architecture)): none of
`revision` / `limit` / `label` / etc. is reused outside these
four, so extracting per-flag leaves would buy no reuse — if one
ever *is* reused, extract that field into a leaf then and
`#[command(flatten)]` it here. Pure relocation — no behavior
change; `vc-x1 chid -h` etc. byte-identical.

Sub-steps:

- **0.49.0-1.1** — the relocation: `CommonArgs` moves to
  `options_flags/common_args.rs` (`impl OptionFlagBundle`),
  `src/common.rs`'s `CommonArgs` (and its `use clap::Args;`)
  removed (the `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` / `resolve_*` /
  `format_*` helpers stay), all four subcommands flatten the
  relocated struct (`use crate::options_flags::common_args::CommonArgs;`),
  and each `.rs` file gains the `//!` module docstring it was
  missing. `main.rs`'s `suppress_banner` reads
  `a.common.no_label` for all four (back to pre-cycle). Bodies
  otherwise unchanged.
- **0.49.0-1.2** — the ARCHITECTURE.md slim + chores-10 reorg
  (its own section below); slotted into this step's ladder
  because ARCHITECTURE.md had accumulated this cycle's transient
  detail.

(An earlier `0.49.0-1.1` decomposed `CommonArgs` into per-flag
`options_flags/` leaves — `revision` / `commit_limit` /
`repo_label` — flattened into a `common_bundle::CommonOptionFlagBundle`.
Backed out on review: the leaves bought no reuse and dragged in a
`value`/`id` clap-id clash. See `### Why not decompose into
leaves`. The `0.49.0-1.1` commit was amended in place to the
relocation above.)

### Why not decompose into leaves

The first take on this step decomposed `CommonArgs` into
per-flag `options_flags/` leaves (`revision`, `commit_limit`,
`repo_label`) flattened into a `common_bundle` — mirroring how
`init` composes `provision_bundle` from `dry_run` / `private` /
`push_retry`. Backed out, because the two are not the same case:

- **No reuse.** `init`'s constituent leaves *are* reused (e.g.
  `--dry-run` will land on `clone`), so a per-flag leaf is the
  unit of sharing. `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` share the
  *whole set*; none of `revision` / `limit` / `label` / etc. is
  reused elsewhere, so per-flag leaves would buy nothing the
  shared struct doesn't already give.
- **`value`/`id` friction.** Two leaves built on the
  single-field-named-`value` convention
  ([`options_flags/README.md`](../src/options_flags/README.md))
  collide on the clap arg *id* (which clap derives from the
  field name) when flattened into the same struct —
  `clap_builder` panics ("duplicate arg id `value`"). The fix
  is an explicit `#[arg(id = …)]` on each, which is exactly the
  "not obvious when `id` vs `value`" sharp edge — and entirely
  self-inflicted by decomposing leaves that didn't need to be
  leaves.

Rule of thumb: make a leaf when *that flag / small group* is
genuinely shared at that granularity; when a *whole arg set* is
shared by N commands but its parts aren't reused, a single
inline-fields bundle is the shape. Extract a leaf later, when a
second consumer is real — `CommonArgs` then `#[command(flatten)]`s
the extracted type (one definition, no duplication).

(The `options_flags/README.md` "Adding a new leaf" caveat about
`#[arg(id = …)]` for co-flattened `value` leaves stays — it's a
real gotcha for the leaf case — but with no in-tree example now.)

## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1)

The Migration B step (and where the cycle is re-scoped — see
`### Cycle re-scope`). Relocate `common::CommonArgs` — the shared
arg set for `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — from
`src/common.rs` into `src/options_flags/common_args.rs`. Inline
fields, no per-flag sub-leaves (an "inline-fields bundle" — see
[Bundle](../src/options_flags/README.md#architecture); rationale
in `### Why not decompose into leaves`). Pure relocation —
`vc-x1 chid -h` etc. byte-identical.

Sub-steps:

- **0.49.0-1.1** — the relocation:
  - `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/common_args.rs` (`impl OptionFlagBundle`).
  - `src/common.rs`: `CommonArgs` + `use clap::Args` dropped
    (the `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` / `resolve_*` /
    `format_*` helpers stay).
  - `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`: flatten the relocated
    struct; each gains the `//!` module docstring it lacked;
    bodies otherwise unchanged.
  - `main.rs`: `suppress_banner` reads `a.common.no_label` for
    all four (back to pre-cycle).
- **0.49.0-1.2** — the ARCHITECTURE.md slim + chores-10 reorg
  (own section below); slotted here because ARCHITECTURE.md had
  collected this cycle's transient detail.

An earlier `0.49.0-1.1` decomposed `CommonArgs` into per-flag
leaves; backed out on review, amended in place to the relocation
above — see `### Why not decompose into leaves`.

### Why not decompose into leaves

The first take decomposed `CommonArgs` into per-flag
`options_flags/` leaves (`revision` / `commit_limit` /
`repo_label`) flattened into a `common_bundle` — mirroring
`init` + `provision_bundle`. Backed out:

- **No reuse.** `init`'s leaves are reused (`--dry-run` →
  `clone`, etc.) — a per-flag leaf is the unit of sharing. The
  four here share the *whole set*; none of `revision` / `limit`
  / `label` / etc. is reused elsewhere, so per-flag leaves buy
  nothing the shared struct doesn't already give.
- **`value`/`id` friction.** Two single-field-`value` leaves
  ([README](../src/options_flags/README.md)) collide on the clap
  arg *id* (derived from the field name) when co-flattened —
  `clap_builder` panics. The fix (`#[arg(id = …)]` on each) is
  the "not obvious when `id` vs `value`" sharp edge —
  self-inflicted by decomposing leaves that didn't need to be.

Rule of thumb:

- Leaf — when *that flag / small group* is genuinely shared at
  that granularity (`--dry-run`, `--squash`).
- Inline-fields bundle — when a *whole arg set* is shared by N
  commands but its parts aren't reused.
- Extract a leaf later, when a second consumer is real:
  `CommonArgs` then `#[command(flatten)]`s the extracted type
  (one definition, no duplication).

file.content:
([README](../src/options_flags/README.md)) collide on the clap
  arg *id* (derived from the field name) when co-flattened —
  `clap_builder` panics. The fix (`#[arg(id = …)]` on each) is
  the "not obvious when `id` vs `value`" sharp edge —
  self-inflicted by decomposing leaves that didn't need to be.

Rule of thumb:

- Leaf — when *that flag / small group* is genuinely shared at
  that granularity (`--dry-run`, `--squash`).
- Inline-fields bundle — when a *whole arg set* is shared by N
  commands but its parts aren't reused.
- Extract a leaf later, when a second consumer is real:
  `CommonArgs` then `#[command(flatten)]`s the extracted type
  (one definition, no duplication).

### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)

The 0.49.0-0 plan opened a "finish Migration A" cycle for the
four `CommonArgs`-flattening subcommands. During 0.49.0-1
review the cycle was expanded to the full **"CommonArgs sweep"**
long planned in `chores-06` / `chores-07` — Migration A is
joined by Migration B and the `--scope` rollout for these four,
and re-ordered B-first so the Migration A ports land once,
against the final `CommonArgs` shape. (The 0.49.0-0 commit and
its chores section keep the original narrow framing; this
subsection is the record of the change of plan.)

- **Migration B** (0.49.0-1, sub-steps -1.1 the relocation,
  -1.2 the docs reorg) — relocate `CommonArgs` into
  `options_flags/common_args.rs` (inline-fields bundle, no
  per-flag leaves), move all four subcommands onto it, remove
  `src/common.rs`'s `CommonArgs`. See above.
- **`--scope`** (0.49.0-2) — replace the repeatable
  `-R`/`--repo` with `-s`/`--scope` (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>`),
  wiring up `scope.rs`'s `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single` (both
  `#[allow(dead_code)]` — built in 0.42.0 for exactly this) and
  `common.rs`'s `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`. Per the
  0.42.0 capture (`chores-06` 0.41.0-4): `-R` is dropped, not
  aliased; arbitrary multi-repo (`-R . -R .claude`) is not
  preserved — `--scope=<path>` covers single-repo, the default
  is `default_scope()`. `for_each_repo` then takes an
  already-resolved `Vec<PathBuf>` (comma-expansion + `["."]`
  default move to the boundary). `init`'s `--scope` (the
  separate `ScopeKind` `code,bot|por`) is left alone —
  reconciling the two vocabularies is the "por/dual parity"
  todo.
- **Migration A** (0.49.0-3..-6) — `XxxParams` +
  `fn x(&Context, &XxxParams)` ports against the post-B/-scope
  `CommonArgs`; introduces the shared clap-free `CommonParams`
  (resolved `DotSpec` + `Header` + repos). All four take a
  fallible `TryFrom<&XxxArgs>` (scope→repo resolution can
  error); `show` also parses `--files` into `FileLimit` at the
  boundary.

Revised ladder (supersedes the 0.49.0-0 one):

- 0.49.0-1 Migration B — relocate `CommonArgs` to
  `options_flags/common_args.rs`; this re-scope. Sub-steps:
  - 0.49.0-1.1 the relocation + all four importers
  - 0.49.0-1.2 docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; start chores-10
  - 0.49.0-1 close-out (squash the two sub-steps or keep

### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)

0.49.0-0 opened a "finish Migration A" cycle for these four
subcommands; at 0.49.0-1 review it was expanded to the full
**"CommonArgs sweep"** (`chores-06` / `chores-07`'s long plan)
and re-ordered B-first so the Migration A ports land once. (The
0.49.0-0 commit/section keep the original framing; this records
the change.) The three parts:

- **Migration B** (0.49.0-1, sub-steps -1.1 relocation, -1.2
  docs reorg):
  - relocate `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/common_args.rs`
    (inline-fields bundle, no per-flag leaves);
  - move all four subcommands onto it;
  - remove `src/common.rs`'s `CommonArgs`.
- **`--scope`** (0.49.0-2) — `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope`
  (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>`):
  - wires up `scope.rs`'s `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single` (both
    `#[allow(dead_code)]`, built in 0.42.0 for this) and
    `common.rs`'s `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`;
  - `-R` dropped, not aliased; arbitrary multi-repo
    (`-R . -R .claude`) not preserved — per the 0.42.0 capture
    (`chores-06` 0.41.0-4); `--scope=<path>` covers single-repo,
    default `default_scope()`;
  - `for_each_repo` takes an already-resolved `Vec<PathBuf>`
    (comma-expansion + `["."]` default move to the boundary);
  - `init`'s `--scope` (the separate `ScopeKind` `code,bot|por`)
    left alone — the "por/dual parity" todo.
- **Migration A** (0.49.0-3..-6) — `XxxParams` + `fn x(&Context,
  &XxxParams)` ports against the post-B/-scope `CommonArgs`:
  - introduces the shared clap-free `CommonParams` (resolved
    `DotSpec` + `Header` + repos);
  - all four take a fallible `TryFrom<&XxxArgs>` (scope→repo
    resolution can error);

file.content:
- 0.49.0 close-out — drop suffix, todo→Done (Migration A
  12/12 + CommonArgs sweep), README + ARCHITECTURE.md

## docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; chores-10 (0.49.0-1.2)

Acting on review feedback that `ARCHITECTURE.md` had drifted
into "how" / transient territory — per-subcommand migration
status, version-by-version tables, sub-step ladders. Pull that
out so `ARCHITECTURE.md` is "what + some why, generic" and the
transient tracking lives here.

- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — keeps the Overview, the two-layer
  (CLI-args vs `Context`+`Params`) section + rationale + the
  `Naming` subsection, a generic module map (the *kinds* of
  module and their roles, no per-subcommand table or version
  annotations), the Subcommand-model recipe, and the See-also.
  The `## Migration A` / `## Migration B` sections become short
  "what it is + why" with a pointer here for live status — the
  per-subcommand status table, the version-by-version "done"
  list, and the "State today" bullets are gone.
- `notes/chores-10.md` — new (this file). Carries the
  `## Migration A / Migration B — live tracking` section (the
  tables that came out of `ARCHITECTURE.md`) plus the `0.49.0-0`
  and `0.49.0-1` sections moved out of `chores-09.md`.
- `notes/chores-09.md` — the `0.49.0-*` sections removed; it now
  ends at `0.48.4` (all done/closed).
- `notes/README.md` — notes that `chores-09` is closed and
  `chores-10` is the active file with the tracking section.
- `notes/todo.md` — "Design:" link repointed to `chores-10.md`;
  the `0.49.0-1` sub-step ladder shows `-1.1` (the relocation),
  `-1.2` (this), and the close-out (the per-subcommand migration
  sub-steps collapse — see the `0.49.0-1` section).

# References

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
## 1. Primary Request and Intent

The overarching task: **finish the subcommand-layer / CLI decoupling for `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`** in the `vc-x1` Rust project. This evolved through user feedback into the full **"CommonArgs sweep"** for those four subcommands — three parts, executed in this order within the `0.49.0` cycle:
1. **Migration B (relocate, NOT decompose)** — move `common::CommonArgs` from `src/common.rs` into `src/options_flags/common_args.rs` as an "inline-fields bundle" (fields stay inline, no per-flag leaves). Done across sub-steps `0.49.0-1.1` (the relocation + all four importers) and `0.49.0-1.2` (slim `ARCHITECTURE.md`, start `chores-10.md`) — **kept separate** (no squash).
2. **`--scope` rollout (`0.49.0-2`)** — replace `-R`/`--repo` with `-s`/`--scope` (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>`), wiring up `scope.rs`'s `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single` and `common.rs`'s `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`; slim `for_each_repo` to take a resolved `Vec<PathBuf>`.
3. **Migration A (`0.49.0-3..-6`)** — `XxxParams` + `fn x(&Context, &XxxParams)` ports against the post-B/-scope shape; introduce shared clap-free `CommonParams` (resolved `DotSpec` + `Header` + repos); all four `TryFrom<&XxxArgs>` (scope→repo resolution is fallible); `show` also parses `--files` → `FileLimit` at the boundary. `0.49.0` = close-out.

Additional explicit user requests that arose:
- Make `ARCHITECTURE.md` generic ("what + some why", no "how"/transient/per-subcommand detail); move transient tracking to `chores-10.md`; **move the `0.49.0-*` chores sections from `chores-09.md` to `chores-10.md`**.
- Use **smaller steps** (decompose `0.49.0-1` into sub-steps).
- **Chores files: short intro then bullets, with sub-bullets for nested lists — not chatty prose.**
- **Process fix (CRITICAL): no eager committing.** The commit-first sub-step model empties the working copy and breaks the user's Zed git-panel review. Going forward: work lands in the working copy, the user reviews in Zed, I commit only when told. (This should eventually be codified in `CLAUDE.md`'s sub-step section.)

## 2. Key Technical Concepts
- `vc-x1`: a single-binary Rust CLI for managing a jj-git dual-repo workspace (app `.` + `.claude` bot-session repo), commits cross-linked via `ochid:` trailers.
- jj (Jujutsu) version control: `jj commit`, `jj squash --into <rev>`, `jj describe`, `jj restore <paths>`, `jj op restore`, `jj log`, change IDs (stable across amendments) vs commit IDs.
- Conventional commits with version suffix, ≤50-char titles; multi-step versioning `X.Y.Z-N`; sub-step protocol `X.Y.Z-N.M`; `vc-x1 push <bookmark>` for the commit-push-finalize flow with two interactive gates.
- Migration A: `pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` → `pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)` with `From`/`TryFrom<&XxxArgs>` at the binary edge; `Context::load(log)`.
- Migration B / `src/options_flags/`: reusable clap `#[derive(Args)]` "leaves" (one flag / small pair) and "bundles" (flatten-of-leaves like `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle`, or — newly documented — inline-fields like `common_args::CommonArgs`); marker traits `FlagBundle`/`OptionBundle`/`OptionFlagBundle`; the `value`-field naming convention for single-field leaves (with `#[arg(id = …)]` needed if two `value` leaves are co-flattened, else clap panics on duplicate arg id).
- `scope.rs`: `Scope` enum (`Roles(Vec<Side>)`, `Single(PathBuf)`), `parse_scope` — both `parse_scope` and `Scope::Single` are `#[allow(dead_code)]` awaiting the `--scope` rollout. `options_flags/scope.rs` has a SEPARATE `ScopeKind` (`CodeBot`/`Por`) used by `init` — left alone (the "por/dual parity" todo).
- `common.rs` helpers: `DotSpec`, `parse_dot_rev`, `resolve_spec`, `Header`, `resolve_header`, `for_each_repo`, `collect_ids`, `resolve_revset`, `format_*`, `find_workspace_root`, `default_scope`, `scope_to_repos`.
- Zed git panel: shows the working-tree diff (= `jj diff` = `@` vs `@-`); no commit-vs-commit UI for jj; terminal fallback `jj show <changeid>` / `jj diff -r <changeid>`.

## 3. Files and Code Sections

- **`Cargo.toml`** — version progression: `0.48.4` → `0.49.0-0` → `0.49.0-1` → `0.49.0-1.1` → `0.49.0-1.2` (current). `Cargo.lock` follows.

- **`src/options_flags/common_args.rs`** (NEW, in amended `0.49.0-1.1`) — `CommonArgs` relocated from `src/common.rs`, fields inline. Has `//!` module docstring, `#[derive(Args, Debug)]`, `impl super::OptionFlagBundle for CommonArgs {}`. Fields:
  ```rust
  pub pos_rev: Option<String>,        // #[arg(value_name = "REVISION")]
  pub pos_count: Option<usize>,       // #[arg(value_name = "COMMITS")]
  pub revision: String,               // #[arg(short, long, default_value = "@")]
  pub repos: Vec<PathBuf>,            // #[arg(short = 'R', long = "repo", value_name = "PATH")]
  pub limit: Option<usize>,           // #[arg(short = 'n', long = "commits", value_name = "COMMITS")]
  pub label: String,                  // #[arg(short = 'l', long = "label", value_name = "TEXT", allow_hyphen_values = true, default_value = "===")]
  pub no_label: bool,                 // #[arg(short = 'L', long = "no-label")]
  ```

- **`src/options_flags/mod.rs`** — `pub mod common_args;` added; `commit_limit`/`common_bundle`/`repo_label`/`revision` (created earlier, then deleted) are NOT present.

- **`src/common.rs`** — `CommonArgs` and `use clap::Args;` REMOVED (the `for_each_repo`/`collect_ids`/`resolve_*`/`format_*`/`find_workspace_root`/`default_scope`/`scope_to_repos` helpers stay).

- **`src/chid.rs`** — `//!` docstring; `use crate::options_flags::common_args::CommonArgs;`; `ChidArgs { #[command(flatten)] pub common: CommonArgs }`; `pub fn chid(args: &ChidArgs)` body reads `c.revision`/`c.limit`/`c.label`/`c.no_label` via `common::resolve_spec(c.pos_rev.as_deref(), c.pos_count, &c.revision, c.limit, "@")` + `common::resolve_header(&c.label, c.no_label)` + `common::for_each_repo(&c.repos, &hdr, ...)`. Tests `parse(...) -> CommonArgs`.

- **`src/desc.rs`** — same pattern as chid.rs (`//!` docstring, flattens `common_args::CommonArgs`, `/// Print each commit...` on `desc()`).

- **`src/list.rs`** — same; `ListArgs` adds `#[arg(short = 'w', long = "width", default_value_t = DEFAULT_OCHID_WIDTH)] pub width: usize` (`const DEFAULT_OCHID_WIDTH: usize = 21`).

- **`src/show.rs`** — `//!` docstring added; `use crate::options_flags::common_args::CommonArgs;`; `ShowArgs` has `#[command(flatten)] pub common: CommonArgs` + `#[arg(short = 'f', long = "files", default_value = "50")] pub files: String`; `/// Print full details...` on `show()`. Has `FileLimit` enum (`None`/`Cap(usize)`/`All`) with `FileLimit::parse`. Many helpers (`show_one_commit`, `find_nearest_tag`, `format_branches`, etc.) unchanged.

- **`src/main.rs`** — `suppress_banner` match: all four (`Chid`/`Desc`/`List`/`Show`) → `a.common.no_label`. Chid dispatch arm: `Commands::Chid(chid_args) => run_command(chid::chid(&chid_args))` (NOT yet ported to Context+Params — that's `0.49.0-3`).

- **`src/options_flags/README.md`** — "Bundle" now describes two forms: *flatten-of-leaves* (e.g. `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle`) and *inline-fields* (e.g. `common_args::CommonArgs`). "Adding a new leaf" has a terse caveat: if two `value`-field leaves can be co-flattened, give each `#[arg(id = "<unique>")]` (else clap panics, duplicate arg id `value`).

- **`ARCHITECTURE.md`** — slimmed from ~290 to ~150 lines: keeps `# Architecture` intro, `## Overview`, `## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params` (+ 5 rules + rationale + `### Naming`), `## Module map` (generic — kinds of module, NO per-subcommand table, NO version annotations), `## Subcommand model` (the recipe), short `## Migration A — args → Context + Params` (what+why, "Per-subcommand status: notes/chores-*.md"), short `## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → src/options_flags/`, `## See also`. The rules list links `[Migration A](#migration-a--args--context--params)`.

- **`notes/chores-10.md`** (NEW) — structure:
  - `# Chores-10` intro (continuation of chores-09 which is closed at 0.48.4; this is the active file; covers 0.49.0 onward + the Migration A/B live-tracking section; `# References` starts at `[1]`).
  - `## Migration A / Migration B — live tracking` — two tables (`### Migration A — pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs) → pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)` listing init/finalize/symlink/clone/sync/validate-desc/fix-desc/push done, chid/desc/list/show pending at 0.49.0-3/-4/-5/-6; `### Migration B — per-subcommand inline #[arg] → src/options_flags/ leaves/bundles` — the chid/desc/list/show row says "flatten options_flags::common_args::CommonArgs (relocated there at 0.49.0-1.1 — an inline-fields bundle, no per-flag sub-leaves); -R/--repo → -s/--scope leaf in 0.49.0-2").
  - `## chore: open 0.49.0 — finish Migration A (0.49.0-0)` — moved verbatim from chores-09, has `Commits: [[1]]`, a "(Superseded at 0.49.0-1...)" note, `### Per-step shape`, `### Ladder (original — superseded)`, `### Per-substep contract`.
  - **`## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1)`** (line 116 — **NEEDS RENAME to `(0.49.0-1.1)`**) — short intro then sub-steps bullets (`0.49.0-1.1` the relocation: `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/common_args.rs` (`impl OptionFlagBundle`), `src/common.rs`'s `CommonArgs` + `use clap::Args` dropped, all four subcommands flatten the relocated struct + gain `//!` docstrings, `main.rs` `suppress_banner` reads `a.common.no_label`; `0.49.0-1.2` the ARCHITECTURE.md slim + chores-10 reorg); then `### Why not decompose into leaves` (bullets: no reuse; `value`/`id` friction; "Rule of thumb:" sub-bullets — leaf when genuinely shared at that granularity, inline-fields bundle when whole arg set shared but parts not reused, extract a leaf later via `#[command(flatten)]` — one definition); then `### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)` (short intro + three bullets with sub-bullets for Migration B / `--scope` / Migration A) + "Revised ladder" bullet list (lines ~214-218 mention `0.49.0-1 Migration B...` and `0.49.0-1 close-out (squash the two sub-steps or keep separate — decided then)` — **these need updating for the keep-separate decision**).
  - `## docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; chores-10 (0.49.0-1.2)` — short intro + file bullets (line ~258 mentions "sub-steps collapse — see the `0.49.0-1` section" — **needs `0.49.0-1.1`**).
  - `# References` — `[1]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/commit/10788bd158c4 "10788bd158c4574fe5a10fab41ea32e4becc86d3"`.

- **`notes/chores-09.md`** — `0.49.0-*` sections removed (via `awk 'NR<=882 || NR>=1059' notes/chores-09.md`); now ends at the `0.48.4` section (`## docs: renumber todo.md + chores-09 refs (0.48.4)` ... `### When to re-pack a file's references`) then `# References` (`[1]`..`[26]` unchanged).

- **`notes/README.md`** — intro notes chores-09 is closed (through 0.48.x), chores-10.md is the active file with the Migration A/B live-tracking tables; reworded the ARCHITECTURE.md pointer.

- **`notes/todo.md`** — `## In Progress` ladder: `**chid/desc/list/show — CommonArgs sweep**` blurb + `[chores-10.md](chores-10.md#chore-open-0490--finish-migration-a-0490-0)` link; `0.49.0-0 ... (done)`; `0.49.0-1 Migration B — relocate CommonArgs → options_flags/common_args.rs; re-scope cycle` with sub-bullets `0.49.0-1.1 the relocation + all four importers (done)`, `0.49.0-1.2 docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; start chores-10 (done)`, `0.49.0-1 close-out — squash the two sub-steps or keep separate (decided then), then push (current)`; then `0.49.0-2 -R/--repo → -s/--scope ...`, `0.49.0-3 chid Migration A + introduce CommonParams`, `0.49.0-4 desc Migration A`, `0.49.0-5 list Migration A`, `0.49.0-6 show Migration A (TryFrom, FileLimit parse)`, `0.49.0 close-out`. (Some `## Todo` items reference `[chores-06](chores-06.md#generalize---scope-across-commands-0400)`, `[chores-07](chores-07.md#--scope-enum-refactor-0420)` — verified valid anchors.)

- **`/tmp/vcx1-msg-11.txt`** — the body used for the `jj describe` re-title of `0.49.0-1.1`: opens "Relocate `CommonArgs` from `src/common.rs` into `src/options_flags/common_args.rs` — ... Inline fields, no per-flag sub-leaves ... Pure relocation — `vc-x1 chid -h` etc. stay byte-identical. (Replaces the earlier 0.49.0-1.1 that decomposed `CommonArgs` into leaves — see chores-10's "refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1)".)" + per-file bullets + `ochid: /.claude/msvqwkrrukxv`.

## 4. Errors and fixes

- **clap panic: "Argument names must be unique, but 'value' is in use by more than one argument"** — occurred in the (now-backed-out) Migration B "full" version where `RevisionOption` and `CommitLimitOption` both used the `value`-field convention and were co-flattened. Fixed at the time with `#[arg(id = "revision")]` / `#[arg(id = "commits")]`. **Later the whole leaf decomposition was backed out** per user feedback (the leaves bought no reuse; the `value`/`id` friction was self-inflicted) — `CommonArgs` now just relocates with inline fields. The `id` caveat survives only as a terse note in `options_flags/README.md`.

- **Process error: commit-first sub-step model empties the working copy, breaking the user's Zed git-panel review.** User feedback: "Our 'process' is furshimmeled. While you were making the changes I could see them, but then you 'commited' them and now the 'git panel' in zed is empty :(" — Fix: going forward, NO eager commits — work lands in the working copy, user reviews in Zed, I commit only when explicitly told. The `jj describe` for the `0.49.0-1.1` re-title was rejected mid-amend because of this; it was later run (with user approval, message 20).

- **ARCHITECTURE.md drifted into "how"/transient content** — user feedback: "you've conflated transient information like what we are doing now, which should be in a chores-xx file, into Architecture.md. ... Architecture.md is what and some why, but shouldn't contain how or even details of every subcommand but be more generic." — Fixed: slimmed ARCHITECTURE.md drastically, moved per-subcommand status tables + cycle ladders to `chores-10.md`.

- **Chores files too chatty** — user feedback: "In chores you're too chatty, please use sub-bullets to itemize the sub-lists" — Fixed: rewrote chores-10.md's `0.49.0-1` section, `### Why not decompose into leaves`, `### Cycle re-scope`, and `0.49.0-1.2` section to short-intro-then-bullets-with-sub-bullets.

- **Multiple re-plans / rework** — the cycle was re-scoped twice (Migration A only → full sweep → B-first), the leaf decomposition was done then backed out, `0.49.0-1` was decomposed into sub-steps then `0.49.0-1.1` was amended in place. This caused significant churn; the user is somewhat frustrated and wants careful, smaller, reviewable steps.

## 5. Problem Solving

- Decided `CommonArgs` belongs in `options_flags/` (user's original observation) but as an **inline-fields bundle**, not decomposed into per-flag leaves (the leaves earned no reuse and dragged in the `value`/`id` clap-id clash). The README "Bundle" definition was relaxed to cover both forms.
- Decided the cycle is B-first (`0.49.0-1` Migration B, `0.49.0-2` `--scope`, `0.49.0-3..-6` Migration A) so the Migration A ports are written once against the final shape.
- `0.49.0-1` (Migration B) decomposed into `0.49.0-1.1` (the relocation) + `0.49.0-1.2` (the docs reorg), **kept separate** (no squash) at close-out.
- The amend of `0.49.0-1.1` (replacing the leaf decomposition with the relocation) was done via: make code changes in `@` → `jj squash --into zrnkqqxz` → `jj describe zrnkqqxz` (re-title). The chores-10/todo doc-fixes were made in `@`, reviewed by the user, then `jj squash --into @-` (into `0.49.0-1.2`).
- Verified the committed amended code: `cargo build`/`fmt --check`/`clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo test` → 360 (lib) + 5 + 4 + 5 pass; `cargo install --path . --locked`; `vc-x1 chid -L @.. 2` smoke OK.

## 6. All user messages
- "acquaint"
