ochid: /.claude/msvqwkrrukxv

# options_flags — Reusable CLI Options and Flags

Per-flag CLI surfaces shared across multiple subcommands. Each
shared option or flag (OF) lives in its own submodule so its
type, parser, and tests stay together.

This module is the CLI-layer leaf store for "Migration B" in
[`../../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../../ARCHITECTURE.md); see there for
how the leaves fit the wider clap-args / `Context`+`Params`
split.

## Architecture

Three composition patterns:

- **Leaf** — a `#[derive(Args)]` struct holding the flag(s),
  `value_parser`, default, and help text (via the field
  doc-comment). One `#[derive(Args)]` per logical OF — single
  flag or a small fixed pair (e.g. `push_retry`'s two fields).
  Help text aims to be generic enough for any reasonable
  consumer.
- **Bundle** — a `#[derive(Args)]` shared by N subcommands,
  picked up with one `#[command(flatten)]` line. Two forms:
  - *flatten-of-leaves* — composes existing leaves into a named
    role (e.g. `ProvisionOptionFlagBundle` = `dry_run` +
    `private` + `push_retry`). Use this when the constituent
    flags are themselves reused elsewhere.
  - *inline-fields* — holds the `#[arg]` fields directly (e.g.
    `common_args::CommonArgs` — the read-only commit-query arg
    set). Use this when a whole *set* of args is shared but the
    individual flags aren't reused outside the bundle —
    extracting per-flag leaves would buy no reuse. If one of the
    fields ever *is* reused (a future command wants just that
    flag), extract it into a leaf then and
    `#[command(flatten)]` it into the bundle.
- **Pattern A escape hatch** — when a consumer needs unique help
  text (or different defaults), it skips the leaf's flatten and
  inlines its own `#[arg(value_parser = …)]` field, reusing the
  leaf's typed value and parser.

## Flag vs Option — classify by domain, not wire syntax

Every leaf falls into one of two categories, picked by the value
domain it carries:

- **Flag** — boolean domain. Wire form may be presence/absence
  (`--dry-run`), explicit negation (`--no-dry-run`), or value
  (`--dry-run=true|false`). All three express a `bool`. Type
  name suffix: `*Flag`.
- **Option** — non-boolean domain (string, integer, enum, path,
  …). Wire form is always `--name=<value>` (or `--name <value>`).
  Type name suffix: `*Option` (or `*Options` for a multi-field
  leaf like `PushRetryOptions`).

Wire form is presentation; the category is the underlying domain.

## Adding a new leaf

1. Create `options_flags/<name>.rs` with a
   `#[derive(Args, Debug, Clone, Default)]` struct.
2. Pick the suffix per domain: `*Flag` (boolean) or `*Option(s)`
   (non-boolean).
3. Name the field(s):
   - **Single-field leaf** — call the field `value` (it holds the
     parsed value-side of the option) and declare the flag
     explicitly with `#[arg(long = "<flag>", …)]`. The consumer
     then reads `args.<leaf>.value` rather than doubling the flag
     name (`args.squash.value`, not `args.squash.squash`).
     Caveat: clap derives an arg's *id* from the field name, so
     if two `value`-field leaves can ever be flattened into the
     same struct, give each an explicit `#[arg(id = "<unique>")]`
     (else `clap_builder` panics — duplicate arg id `value`).
   - **Multi-field leaf** — descriptive per-field names
     (`push_retries`, `push_retry_delay`); clap derives each
     flag from its field name as usual.
   - The pre-existing single-field leaves (`scope`, `repo`,
     `dry_run`, `private`, `account`, `config`, `use_template`)
     predate this and still derive the flag from the field name;
     migrating them to `value` is tracked in
     [`../../notes/todo.md`](../../notes/todo.md).
4. Add `pub mod <name>;` to `mod.rs`.
5. If the leaf has explicit parsing logic, declare a unit-struct
   implementor of `FlagParser` (boolean domain) or `OptionParser`
   (non-boolean domain). Bare `Option<String>` leaves and
   presence/absence boolean leaves need no parser impl — clap
   handles them.
6. Add tests for any non-trivial parser/resolver logic.
7. Leaves do **not** implement a Bundle marker — bundles do.

## Consuming an OF

Default — flatten the leaf into your subcommand's `Args`:

```rust
#[derive(Args)]
pub struct MyArgs {
    #[command(flatten)]
    pub config: ConfigOption,
    // ...
}
```

Pattern A — when generic help doesn't fit:

```rust
#[derive(Args)]
pub struct MyArgs {
    /// My subcommand-specific help for --config.
    #[arg(long = "config", value_name = "none|PATH",
          verbatim_doc_comment)]
    pub config: Option<String>,
    // ...
}
```

A Pattern A consumer reuses the leaf's types (e.g. `ConfigKind`)
and parsers (e.g. `parse_config_kind`) but owns its own clap
attributes.

## Consumer function shape

Helper functions called by a subcommand body should accept the
relevant leaf type by reference rather than unpacking individual
fields at the call site:

```rust
// Multi-field leaf — pass the whole leaf
fn run_retry(cmd: &str, args: &[&str], cwd: &Path,
             retry: &PushRetryOptions) -> Result<…> { … }

run_retry("git", &["push", …], cwd, &args.provision.push_retry)?;
```

This wins on:
- Readability — `&args.provision.push_retry` reads as a single
  unit; the function body works with `retry.push_retries` (no
  leaf-name doubling because the parameter name is the consumer's
  choice).
- Argument count — every multi-field leaf collapses N args into
  one ref, so chained helpers don't accumulate
  `clippy::too_many_arguments` warnings.
- Future-proofing — extending a leaf with another field is a
  zero-touch change at every call site.

For **single-field leaves** (e.g. `SquashOption`, `DryRunFlag`,
`PrivateFlag`), direct read at the consumer site
(`args.squash.value`, or `args.provision.dry_run.dry_run` for the
pre-`value` leaves) is fine — wrapping the inner value in a
`&LeafType` parameter doesn't earn the indirection.

## Marker traits

Three Bundle markers — one per content classification. Bundles
implement exactly one. Leaves do **not** implement these markers
(their category lives in the type-name suffix).

- `FlagBundle: clap::Args` — pure-boolean bundle (every
  constituent leaf is a Flag). Rare in practice.
- `OptionBundle: clap::Args` — pure-non-boolean bundle (every
  constituent leaf is an Option).
- `OptionFlagBundle: clap::Args` — mixed bundle (constituents
  include both Flag and Option leaves). Most common in practice.

Two Parser traits — conditional contracts implemented only when
a leaf has explicit parsing logic (custom `value_parser`):

- `FlagParser` — boolean-domain parser. Implemented only for
  boolean leaves that take a value form (`--flag=true|false`).
  Presence/absence flags need no impl; clap parses directly.
- `OptionParser` — non-boolean-domain parser. Implemented only
  for leaves with custom parsing logic (e.g. `ScopeParser`,
  `RepoParser`); bare `Option<String>` leaves need no impl.

`FlagParser` and `OptionParser` share an identical method shape;
the difference is documentation (which value-domain the parser
targets).

All five traits are documentation-level — clap derive doesn't
see them; the discipline is that every leaf and bundle declares
the appropriate ones explicitly.

## Bundle marker discipline

Bundle markers aren't compiler-checked against contents. If a
bundle's leaf set shifts category — e.g. a pure-Flag bundle gains
an Option leaf — you must update the marker by hand
(`FlagBundle` → `OptionFlagBundle`). No lint, no failure; silent
drift if forgotten. Worth a glance at the marker line whenever a
bundle's `#[command(flatten)]` set changes.

## Pattern A worked example

`init`'s `--config` field originally carried init-specific help
("Only valid with `--scope=por`..."). It currently flattens
`ConfigOption` for the generic help; the `--scope=por` constraint
surfaces in `init`'s preflight error. If the help-text generality
becomes a usability problem, init will switch to Pattern A as the
worked example.

## Layout note

OFs currently sit as flat `<name>.rs` files alongside this
README. Bundle modules carry the `_bundle` suffix
(e.g. `provision_bundle.rs`) so the leaf/bundle split is visible
in the directory listing without opening files. If an individual
OF accumulates enough rationale, edge-case detail, or examples
to outgrow doc-comments, it will graduate to a `<name>/mod.rs` +
`<name>/README.md` subdirectory layout. Mechanical when needed;
not done preemptively.

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

147	### Why not decompose into leaves
148	
149	The first take decomposed `CommonArgs` into per-flag

file.content:
Per `notes/substep-protocol.md`: `cargo fmt` / `clippy
--all-targets -- -D warnings` / `test` / `install --path .
--locked` + retest before each commit; bump `Cargo.toml` at
sub-step start; flip todo ladder markers; pair commits across
both repos with ochid trailers.

## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1)

The Migration B step (and where the cycle is re-scoped — see
`### Cycle re-scope`). Relocate `common::CommonArgs` — the shared
arg set for `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — from
`src/common.rs` into `src/options_flags/common_args.rs`. Inline
fields, no per-flag sub-leaves (an "inline-fields bundle" — see
[Bundle](../src/options_flags/README.md#architecture); rationale
in `### Why not decompose into leaves`). Pure relocation —
`vc-x1 chid -h` etc. byte-identical.

Sub-steps:

- **0.49.0-1.1** — the relocation:
  - `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/common_args.rs` (`impl OptionFlagBundle`).
  - `src/common.rs`: `CommonArgs` + `use clap::Args` dropped
    (the `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` / `resolve_*` /
    `format_*` helpers stay).
  - `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`: flatten the relocated
    struct; each gains the `//!` module docstring it lacked;
    bodies otherwise unchanged.
  - `main.rs`: `suppress_banner` reads `a.common.no_label` for
    all four (back to pre-cycle).
- **0.49.0-1.2** — the ARCHITECTURE.md slim + chores-10 reorg
  (own section below); slotted here because ARCHITECTURE.md had
  collected this cycle's transient detail.

An earlier `0.49.0-1.1` decomposed `CommonArgs` into per-flag
leaves; backed out on review, amended in place to the relocation
above — see `### Why not decompose into leaves`.

### Why not decompose into leaves

The first take decomposed `CommonArgs` into per-flag
`options_flags/` leaves (`revision` / `commit_limit` /
`repo_label`) flattened into a `common_bundle` — mirroring
`init` + `provision_bundle`. Backed out:

- **No reuse.** `init`'s leaves are reused (`--dry-run` →
  `clone`, etc.) — a per-flag leaf is the unit of sharing. The
  four here share the *whole set*; none of `revision` / `limit`
  / `label` / etc. is reused elsewhere, so per-flag leaves buy
  nothing the shared struct doesn't already give.
- **`value`/`id` friction.** Two single-field-`value` leaves
  ([README](../src/options_flags/README.md)) collide on the clap
  arg *id* (derived from the field name) when co-flattened —
  `clap_builder` panics. The fix (`#[arg(id = …)]` on each) is
  the "not obvious when `id` vs `value`" sharp edge —
  self-inflicted by decomposing leaves that didn't need to be.

Rule of thumb:

- Leaf — when *that flag / small group* is genuinely shared at
  that granularity (`--dry-run`, `--squash`).
- Inline-fields bundle — when a *whole arg set* is shared by N
  commands but its parts aren't reused.
- Extract a leaf later, when a second consumer is real:
  `CommonArgs` then `#[command(flatten)]`s the extracted type
  (one definition, no duplication).

### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)

0.49.0-0 opened a "finish Migration A" cycle for these four
subcommands; at 0.49.0-1 review it was expanded to the full
**"CommonArgs sweep"** (`chores-06` / `chores-07`'s long plan)
and re-ordered B-first so the Migration A ports land once. (The
0.49.0-0 commit/section keep the original framing; this records
the change.) The three parts:

- **Migration B** (0.49.0-1, sub-steps -1.1 relocation, -1.2
  docs reorg):
  - relocate `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/common_args.rs`
    (inline-fields bundle, no per-flag leaves);
  - move all four subcommands onto it;
  - remove `src/common.rs`'s `CommonArgs`.
- **`--scope`** (0.49.0-2) — `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope`
  (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>`):
  - wires up `scope.rs`'s `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single` (both
    `#[allow(dead_code)]`, built in 0.42.0 for this) and
    `common.rs`'s `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`;
  - `-R` dropped, not aliased; arbitrary multi-repo
    (`-R . -R .claude`) not preserved — per the 0.42.0 capture
    (`chores-06` 0.41.0-4); `--scope=<path>` covers single-repo,
    default `default_scope()`;
  - `for_each_repo` takes an already-resolved `Vec<PathBuf>`
    (comma-expansion + `["."]` default move to the boundary);
  - `init`'s `--scope` (the separate `ScopeKind` `code,bot|por`)
    left alone — the "por/dual parity" todo.
- **Migration A** (0.49.0-3..-6) — `XxxParams` + `fn x(&Context,
  &XxxParams)` ports against the post-B/-scope `CommonArgs`:
  - introduces the shared clap-free `CommonParams` (resolved
    `DotSpec` + `Header` + repos);
  - all four take a fallible `TryFrom<&XxxArgs>` (scope→repo
    resolution can error);
  - `show` also parses `--files` → `FileLimit` at the boundary.

Revised ladder (supersedes the 0.49.0-0 one):

- 0.49.0-1 Migration B — relocate `CommonArgs` to
  `options_flags/common_args.rs`; this re-scope. Sub-steps:
  - 0.49.0-1.1 the relocation + all four importers
  - 0.49.0-1.2 docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; start chores-10
  - 0.49.0-1 close-out (squash the two sub-steps or keep
    separate — decided then)
- 0.49.0-2 `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope` (wire `parse_scope` /
  `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`; slim `for_each_repo`) —
  may split into `-2.N`
- 0.49.0-3 chid Migration A + introduce `CommonParams`
- 0.49.0-4 desc Migration A
- 0.49.0-5 list Migration A
- 0.49.0-6 show Migration A (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse)
- 0.49.0 close-out — drop suffix, todo→Done (Migration A
  12/12 + CommonArgs sweep), README + ARCHITECTURE.md

## docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; chores-10 (0.49.0-1.2)

Acting on review feedback that `ARCHITECTURE.md` had drifted
into "how" / transient territory — per-subcommand migration
status, version-by-version tables, sub-step ladders. Pull that
out so `ARCHITECTURE.md` is "what + some why, generic" and the
transient tracking lives here.

- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — slimmed to "what + some why, generic":
  - kept: Overview, the two-layer section + rationale + `Naming`,
    a generic module map (kinds of module — no per-subcommand
    table, no version annotations), the Subcommand-model recipe,
    See-also;
  - the `## Migration A` / `## Migration B` sections → short
    "what + why" + a pointer here for live status (the
    per-subcommand status table, the version-by-version "done"
    list, the "State today" bullets are gone).
- `notes/chores-10.md` — new (this file): the
  `## Migration A / Migration B — live tracking` tables (out of
  `ARCHITECTURE.md`) + the `0.49.0-0` / `0.49.0-1` sections
  moved out of `chores-09.md`.
- `notes/chores-09.md` — the `0.49.0-*` sections removed; it now
  ends at `0.48.4` (all done/closed).
- `notes/README.md` — notes that `chores-09` is closed and
  `chores-10` is the active file with the tracking section.
- `notes/todo.md` — "Design:" link repointed to `chores-10.md`;
  the `0.49.0-1` sub-step ladder shows `-1.1` (the relocation),
  `-1.2` (this), and the close-out (the per-subcommand migration
  sub-steps collapse — see the `0.49.0-1` section).

# References

[1]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/commit/10788bd158c4 "10788bd158c4574fe5a10fab41ea32e4becc86d3"

both repos with ochid trailers.

## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1.1)

The Migration B relocation, and where the cycle is re-scoped (see
`### Cycle re-scope`). Relocate `common::CommonArgs` — the shared
arg set for `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — from
`src/common.rs` into `src/options_flags/common_args.rs`. Inline
fields, no per-flag sub-leaves (an "inline-fields bundle" — see
[Bundle](../src/options_flags/README.md#architecture); rationale
in `### Why not decompose into leaves`). Pure relocation —
`vc-x1 chid -h` etc. byte-identical.

- `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/common_args.rs` (`impl OptionFlagBundle`).
- `src/common.rs`: `CommonArgs` + `use clap::Args` dropped (the
  `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` / `resolve_*` / `format_*`
  helpers stay).
- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`: flatten the relocated
  struct; each gains the `//!` module docstring it lacked;
  bodies otherwise unchanged.
- `main.rs`: `suppress_banner` reads `a.common.no_label` for all
  four (back to pre-cycle).

`0.49.0-1.2` (the ARCHITECTURE.md slim + chores-10 reorg, own
section below) follows; the two are kept separate at close-out —
no squash, no extra `0.49.0-1` commit.

An earlier `0.49.0-1.1` decomposed `CommonArgs` into per-flag
leaves; backed out on review, amended in place to the relocation
above — see `### Why not decompose into leaves`.

file.content:
## docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; chores-10 (0.49.0-1.2)

Acting on review feedback that `ARCHITECTURE.md` had drifted
into "how" / transient territory — per-subcommand migration
status, version-by-version tables, sub-step ladders. Pull that
out so `ARCHITECTURE.md` is "what + some why, generic" and the
transient tracking lives here.

- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — slimmed to "what + some why, generic":
  - kept: Overview, the two-layer section + rationale + `Naming`,
    a generic module map (kinds of module — no per-subcommand
    table, no version annotations), the Subcommand-model recipe,
    See-also;
  - the `## Migration A` / `## Migration B` sections → short
    "what + why" + a pointer here for live status (the
    per-subcommand status table, the version-by-version "done"
    list, the "State today" bullets are gone).
- `notes/chores-10.md` — new (this file): the
  `## Migration A / Migration B — live tracking` tables (out of
  `ARCHITECTURE.md`) + the `0.49.0-0` / `0.49.0-1` sections
  moved out of `chores-09.md`.
- `notes/chores-09.md` — the `0.49.0-*` sections removed; it now
  ends at `0.48.4` (all done/closed).
- `notes/README.md` — notes that `chores-09` is closed and
  `chores-10` is the active file with the tracking section.
- `notes/todo.md` — "Design:" link repointed to `chores-10.md`;
  the `0.49.0-1` sub-step ladder shows `-1.1` (the relocation)
  and `-1.2` (this), kept separate — see the `0.49.0-1.1`
  section.

# References

[1]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/commit/10788bd158c4 "10788bd158c4574fe5a10fab41ea32e4becc86d3"

=== app -1.1 (zrnkqqxz) ===
refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1.1)

Relocate `CommonArgs` from `src/common.rs` into
`src/options_flags/common_args.rs` — the read-only commit-query
subcommands' shared arg set now lives in `options_flags/` (like
`init`'s provision bundle). Fields stay inline, no per-flag
sub-leaves: none of them is reused outside these four, so
extracting leaves would buy no reuse and pulls in the
`value`/`id` friction (the README documents this "inline-fields
bundle" shape). Pure relocation — `vc-x1 chid -h` etc. stay
byte-identical. (Replaces the earlier 0.49.0-1.1 that decomposed
`CommonArgs` into leaves — see chores-10's "refactor: CommonArgs
→ options_flags (0.49.0-1)".)

- src/options_flags/common_args.rs: new — `CommonArgs` (the
  7-field struct relocated; `impl OptionFlagBundle`)
- src/options_flags/mod.rs: + `common_args` (− the 4 leaf mods)
- src/common.rs: `CommonArgs` removed; dropped `use clap::Args`
- src/chid.rs / desc.rs / list.rs / show.rs: flatten
  `options_flags::common_args::CommonArgs`; each gains the `//!`
  module docstring it was missing
- src/main.rs: `suppress_banner` reads `a.common.no_label` for
  all four (back to pre-cycle)
- src/options_flags/README.md: "Bundle" now covers two forms
  (flatten-of-leaves / inline-fields — `common_args` the
  example); a terse `#[arg(id=…)]` caveat replaces the
  leaf-clash note

content.text:
I did the app-side squash — the `chores-10.md` + `todo.md` doc fixes are now folded into the `0.49.0-1.2` commit (`@` is empty again). But before pushing I hit a few things that make `vc-x1 push main` the wrong tool here, so I want your call on a manual close-out.

**Current stacks**

App repo:
- `main` → `0.49.0-0` (pushed)
- `0.49.0-1.1` — `refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1.1)` ✓ (but body cross-refs chores-10's section as `(0.49.0-1)` — now stale, I renamed it to `(0.49.0-1.1)`)
- `0.49.0-1.2` — `docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; chores-10 (0.49.0-1.2)` ✓ (now includes the doc-fix squash)
- `@` empty; `Cargo.toml` = `0.49.0-1.2`

`.claude` repo:
- `main` → `0.49.0-0` (pushed)
- `-1.1` — **title is the old leaf-decomposition framing**: `refactor: common-bundle leaves + chid (0.49.0-1.1)`, body says "created the options_flags/ leaves + common_bundle bundle" — both stale, needs re-describe to match the app's `-1.1`
- `-1.2` — `docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; chores-10 (0.49.0-1.2)` ✓
- `@` — uncommitted: just this session's `.jsonl` update

**Why not `vc-x1 push main`:** push's `commit-app` stage commits `@` and `bookmark-both` advances `main` to `@-`. With `@` empty and the real work already in committed `-1.1`/`-1.2`, that path would manufacture a spurious third commit (or choke on the empty `@`). Push fits "uncommitted work in `@`" — not "advance the bookmark over a pre-committed sub-step stack."

**Proposed manual close-out:**

1. **App `-1.1` body fix** — `jj describe zrnkqqxz -R .` changing the body's `chores-10's "…(0.49.0-1)"` → `…(0.49.0-1.1)` (mutable, not yet pushed).
2. **`.claude` `-1.1` re-describe** — title → `refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1.1)`; body → relocation + cycle-rescope framing; keep `ochid: /zrnkqqxzrvon`.
3. **`.claude` `@` squash into `-1.2`** — `jj squash -R .claude` folds this session's trailing work into the `.claude` `-1.2` commit (mirrors what just happened on the app side).
4. **Cargo cycle** — `cargo fmt && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test && cargo install --path . --locked` + retest (doc-only changes since the last verified-clean state, so expected no-op, but per the checklist).
5. **Push app** — `jj bookmark set main -r wzztqzvq -R .` (= `0.49.0-1.2`); `jj git push --bookmark main -R .`.
6. **Push `.claude`** — `jj bookmark set main -r xuqukovk -R .claude` (= `0.49.0-1.2`); `jj git push --bookmark main -R .claude`. (Or `vc-x1 finalize --repo .claude --squash --push main --delay 10 --detach --log /tmp/vc-x1-finalize.log` — same effect.)

This lands `-1.1` + `-1.2` on both `main`s, kept separate, no spurious commits.

Want me to proceed with steps 1–6? (And do you want to eyeball the `.claude` `-1.1` re-describe text before I run it, or trust me to mirror the app side?)

message.content:
In notes/README.md mentions "Migration A / B", you surfaced that recently
and how is that different from "steps", "sub-steps"?

content.text:
Different axes — one is *what work*, the other is *how a cycle is chopped up*.

**Migration A / Migration B — a named, long-running refactor program.** Each is a theme spanning many version cycles and many subcommands:
- **Migration A** — port every subcommand from `fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` to `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`, with clap kept at the binary edge via `From`/`TryFrom<&XxxArgs>`.
- **Migration B** — pull per-subcommand inline `#[arg]` fields into shared `src/options_flags/` leaves/bundles.

They carry no version number of their own; each is "done" when the last subcommand is converted. Progress is a checklist *over the codebase surface* — which subcommands are ported — which is exactly what the `chores-10.md` "live tracking" tables hold. (`init/finalize/symlink/clone/sync/validate-desc/fix-desc/push` already did Migration A in earlier cycles; `chid/desc/list/show` are the last four. That's why these terms aren't new — they were in `ARCHITECTURE.md`; I just moved the *status* out of there this session, since per-subcommand progress is transient tracking, not architecture.)

**Steps / sub-steps — the version-suffix workflow for one cycle.** Pure process machinery from `CLAUDE.md`:
- **step** = an `X.Y.Z-N` commit in a multi-step cycle (`X.Y.Z-0` … `X.Y.Z`), each with its own commit + push + finalize.
- **sub-step** = `X.Y.Z-N.M`, a finer split inside one step (sub-sub-step `X.Y.Z-N.M-K`).

Progress here is a checklist *over this cycle's commits* — the `todo.md > ## In Progress` ladder with `(done)`/`(current)` markers.

**How they relate:** a step/sub-step is a *unit of delivery*; it may or may not advance a migration, and most cycles touch neither A nor B. The `0.49.0` cycle happens to advance both — `0.49.0-1.x` does Migration B for the four subcommands, `0.49.0-3..-6` does Migration A for them — but that's this cycle's *content*, not a property of the workflow. Migration A could've been done one subcommand per release, or all in one giant cycle; the step ladder would look completely different while "Migration A" stayed the same thing.

Rough analogy: Migration A/B ≈ an epic; steps/sub-steps ≈ the commits that chip away at it (plus all the commits that have nothing to do with it). And they live in different files for that reason — the migration is "what + why" (chores / formerly ARCHITECTURE), the step ladder is "how it's sliced this time" (CLAUDE.md + todo.md's In Progress).

Want me to add a short version of this distinction to `chores-10.md`'s tracking section (or `notes/README.md`)? Otherwise I'll go back to waiting on your call for the steps 1–6 close-out.

lastPrompt: In notes/README.md mentions "Migration A / B", you surfaced that recently and how is that different from "steps", "sub-steps"?

message.content: Hmm, I don't like migration for some reason, how about "task" or "task set" or suggest other synonyms

content.text:
"Migration" carries data/schema/framework-migration baggage that doesn't fit — these are uniform *internal-API refactors* applied across every subcommand, not a move between platforms. I lean toward keeping the one-letter handle (it's a genuinely useful short token in cross-refs — "see X A status") but swapping the noun. Candidates I'd weigh:

- **Refactor** — *literally* what each is; plain word; smallest swap. "Refactor A — args → Context+Params" / "Refactor B — flags → options_flags".
- **No category word at all** — just name them by target: "the Context+Params refactor" / "the options_flags extraction". Most self-documenting; loses the `A`/`B` shorthand.
- **Workstream / Track** — conveys "long-running parallel line of work"; reads a bit corporate.
- **Task set** (your suggestion) — fine, but "task" overlaps with todo-list items *and* the `Task` tool in this environment, so it's slightly ambiguous in context.
- Also-rans: *campaign* (nice "sustained effort" vibe, informal), *pass* ("the Context+Params pass", like a compiler pass), *overhaul*, *conversion*/*transition* (same migration-ish vibe, doesn't fix it), *sweep* (good fit — but already taken by "the CommonArgs sweep" for the *cycle*, so it'd collide).

Scope if we rename: `chores-10.md` (the `## … live tracking` heading + the two table sub-headings + the `### Cycle re-scope` body), `ARCHITECTURE.md` (`## Migration A` / `## Migration B` section headings + their anchors), `notes/README.md` and `notes/todo.md` (the cross-ref links/anchors), and the two unpushed commit bodies (`-1.1` already needs a re-describe, so that's nearly free). None of those headings is a commit-recording one, so it's a mechanical find-replace + anchor fixup — fits into the `0.49.0-1.2` close-out.

# Chores-10

Continuation of `chores-09.md` (which is closed at `0.48.4` —
the `0.42.0`–`0.48.x` cycles). This file covers the `0.49.0`
cycle onward, plus a living **Migration A / Migration B
tracking** section (the per-subcommand status that used to sit
in `ARCHITECTURE.md`).

See [`../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ARCHITECTURE.md) for what
Migration A / B *are* and why; this file is the *how* and the
*live status*. Reference numbering is file-local — see
[`README.md`](README.md#reference-numbering); chores-10 starts
at `[1]`.

## Migration A / Migration B — live tracking

Two parallel refactors of the subcommand layer (see
`ARCHITECTURE.md`). Status as of the entries below; update in
place as cycles land.

### Migration A — `pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` → `pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`

| Subcommand | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | done (0.44.0) — worked example; `From<&InitArgs>` |
| `finalize` | done (0.46.0) — `TryFrom<&FinalizeArgs>` (fallible); `--log` onto `Context` |
| `symlink` | done (0.48.0-1) — `From<&SymlinkArgs>` |
| `clone` | done (0.48.0-2) — `From<&CloneArgs>` |
| `sync` | done (0.48.0-3) — `From<&SyncArgs>` |
| `validate-desc` | done (0.48.0-4) — `From<&ValidateDescArgs>` |
| `fix-desc` | done (0.48.0-5) — `From<&FixDescArgs>` |
| `push` | done (0.48.0-6) — `From<&PushArgs>` (collapses the two bookmark spellings) |
| `chid` | pending (0.49.0-3) — introduces the shared `CommonParams`; `TryFrom` |
| `desc` | pending (0.49.0-4) — `TryFrom` |
| `list` | pending (0.49.0-5) — `TryFrom` |
| `show` | pending (0.49.0-6) — `TryFrom`; also parses `--files` → `FileLimit` at the boundary |

Out of scope for the ports (deferred until a real consumer
surfaces): typed errors, returned-outcomes-vs-`println!`,
`ProgressSink`, `Context` fields beyond `UserConfig` + `--log`.

### Migration B — per-subcommand inline `#[arg]` → `src/options_flags/` leaves/bundles

| Subcommand(s) | Status |
| --- | --- |
| `init` | fully composed — `account` / `repo` / `scope` / the `provision` bundle / `use_template` / `config` |

file.content:
config / command-line args.
- **Boundary conversion**: `XxxArgs → XxxParams` via
  `impl From<&XxxArgs>` or `impl TryFrom<&XxxArgs>`, depending
  on whether the conversion can fail.

Two parameters per subcommand; the signature documents what the
subcommand depends on. `Context` is the same across all
subcommands; `XxxParams` carry the per-subcommand inputs.

Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
[Migration A](#migration-a--args--context--params)):

1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
   `XxxParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a TUI
   matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each subcommand returns
   a structured result; CLI formats it, a library writes
   nothing to stdout itself.
4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests
   a recording sink.
5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.

None of this is sacred — the whole structure is an experiment.
The current scope is deliberately narrow: a small `Context`
(two params, not one god blob); no trait-based DI yet; one
crate, not a workspace split; `src/options_flags/` leaves left
in place — only their consumers change shape. Widen any of it
when the evidence (a second front-end, a second consumer crate,
a real platform-surface divergence) calls for it — not before.

### Naming

Earlier drafts of this design used `Workspace` / `XOptions`;
the implementation (0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XxxParams`:

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the dual-repo
  project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XxxParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Module map

The *kinds* of module and what they're for — not an exhaustive
catalog (read the `//!` docstrings; `notes/chores-*.md` for
migration status):

**CLI layer:**

- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge: `Cli` (`Parser`), `Commands`
  (`Subcommand`) + the dispatch arms, `cli_with_banner()` (the
  per-subcommand `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner), `CompleteEnv` wiring,
  and `bm_track` (the permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic on every command's entry/exit).
- `src/options_flags/` — reusable `#[derive(Args)]` *leaves*

121	
122	**Subcommand modules** — `src/<x>.rs` holds the subcommand's
123	`XxxArgs` (`#[derive(Args)]`, composing `options_flags/` leaves
124	where one exists) and the entrypoint `pub fn x(...)`. Modules:
125	`chid`, `desc` (+ `desc_helpers`), `list`, `show`,
126	`validate_desc`, `fix_desc`, `clone`, `init` (+ `init/params`),
127	`symlink`, `sync`, `finalize`, `push` (a resumable state

129	A) and which compose `options_flags/` leaves (Migration B):
130	`notes/chores-*.md` + `notes/todo.md`.
131	
132	**Support:**
133	
134	- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
135	  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
136	  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
137	- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
138	  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures and tempdir resolution. Subcommand
139	  modules also carry their own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and
140	  `integration_tests` for `sync` / `push`).
141	
142	## Subcommand model
143	
144	Adding a subcommand `x`:
145	
146	1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XxxArgs`
147	   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
148	   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
149	2. Add a variant `X(x::XxxArgs)` to `Commands` in `src/main.rs`
150	   with a `///` summary (and `#[command(long_about = …)]` for
151	   the long form).
152	3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
153	   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
154	4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
155	   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
156	   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
157	   prose).
158	5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
159	   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
160	   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
161	   and stays at this layer.
162	
163	## Migration A — args → Context + Params
164	
165	The ongoing port of each subcommand's
166	`pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` to
167	`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`, adding an
168	`XxxParams` flat struct + a `From` (or `TryFrom`, if the
169	conversion is fallible) at the binary edge. `init` (0.44.0) is
170	the worked example. Out of scope for the ports until a real
171	consumer surfaces: typed errors,
172	returned-outcomes-vs-`println!`, `ProgressSink`, `Context`
173	fields beyond `UserConfig` + `--log`. Per-subcommand status:
174	`notes/chores-*.md` (the "Migration A / Migration B — live
175	tracking" section).
176	
177	## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`
178	
179	Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
180	fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
181	`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
182	completer) exactly once. See
183	[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)

single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

**Subcommand modules** — `src/<x>.rs` holds the subcommand's
`XxxArgs` (`#[derive(Args)]`, composing `options_flags/` leaves
where one exists) and the entrypoint `pub fn x(...)`. Modules:
`chid`, `desc` (+ `desc_helpers`), `list`, `show`,
`validate_desc`, `fix_desc`, `clone`, `init` (+ `init/params`),
`symlink`, `sync`, `finalize`, `push` (a resumable state
machine). Which ones have ported to `(ctx, params)` (Migration
A) and which compose `options_flags/` leaves (Migration B):
`notes/chores-*.md` + `notes/todo.md`.

**Support:**

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures and tempdir resolution. Subcommand
  modules also carry their own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and
  `integration_tests` for `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XxxArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XxxArgs)` to `Commands` in `src/main.rs`
   with a `///` summary (and `#[command(long_about = …)]` for
   the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.

## Migration A — args → Context + Params

The ongoing port of each subcommand's
`pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` to
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`, adding an
`XxxParams` flat struct + a `From` (or `TryFrom`, if the
conversion is fallible) at the binary edge. `init` (0.44.0) is
the worked example. Out of scope for the ports until a real
consumer surfaces: typed errors,
returned-outcomes-vs-`println!`, `ProgressSink`, `Context`
fields beyond `UserConfig` + `--log`. Per-subcommand status:
`notes/chores-*.md` (the "Migration A / Migration B — live
tracking" section).

## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification; `notes/chores-*.md` for
per-subcommand status.

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
  per-subcommand usage.
- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  — leaf/bundle patterns for Migration B.
- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list, including
  both migrations.
- `notes/chores-*.md` — per-cycle design captures and the
  Migration A/B live-tracking tables (currently
  [`chores-10.md`](notes/chores-10.md); `chores-09.md` and
  earlier are closed).

Good — "Context+Params port" and "options_flags extraction" it is. Here's what the rename touches:

**New names** (informal handles, no `A`/`B`):
- "Migration A" → **the Context+Params port** — the `pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` → `pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)` work.
- "Migration B" → **the options_flags extraction** — inline `#[arg]` fields → shared `src/options_flags/` leaves/bundles.

**Headings:**
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — `## Migration A — args → Context + Params` → `## args → Context + Params`; `## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → src/options_flags/` → `## per-subcommand flags → src/options_flags/` (drop the `Migration X —` prefix; the body sentence carries "the Context+Params port" / "the options_flags extraction"). Fix the one anchor link (line 50, `[Migration A](#migration-a--args--context--params)` → `[the Context+Params port](#args--context--params)`).
- `chores-10.md` — `## Migration A / Migration B — live tracking` → `## Refactor tracking — Context+Params / options_flags`; the two `###` sub-headings drop `Migration A —` / `Migration B —` and lead with `Context+Params port —` / `options_flags extraction —`.

**Prose swaps** of the term across: `chores-10.md` (`### Cycle re-scope`, the `0.49.0-1.x` sections, the file intro), `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `notes/README.md`, `notes/todo.md` (the In-Progress blurb + ladder + the `## Todo` "Context+Params port rides…" item), `src/options_flags/README.md` (the `"Migration B"` mention → `"the options_flags extraction"`).

**Frozen — NOT touched** (records of pushed commits):
- All of `chores-09.md` (`## chore: open 0.48.0 cycle — Migration A sweep (0.48.0-0)`, `## chore: close Migration A sweep cycle (0.48.0)`, etc.).
- `chores-10.md`'s `## chore: open 0.49.0 — finish Migration A (0.49.0-0)` heading **and that section's body** (it records the `0.49.0-0` commit's plan, which was framed as "Migration A").
- `notes/todo.md`'s `## Done` lines (`Migration A sweep — … (0.48.0)`, `finalize Migration B — … (0.47.0)`).
- A one-line "renamed from 'Migration A' / 'Migration B'" note goes in `chores-10.md`'s `### Cycle re-scope` so the old terms are findable.

**Where it lands:** folded into the `0.49.0-1.2` commit — it already touches 4 of the 5 files; title stays `docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; chores-10 (0.49.0-1.2)`, body bullets gain the rename, the chores `0.49.0-1.2` intro gets a sentence.

Note the `0.49.0-1` close-out is still queued behind this: app `-1.1` body fix (`(0.49.0-1)` → `(0.49.0-1.1)`), `.claude` `-1.1` re-describe (its title is still the old leaf-decomposition framing), squash `.claude` `@` into `.claude` `-1.2`, cargo cycle, then push both.

Want me to do the rename now (into `-1.2`), or close out `0.49.0-1` first with "Migration" and rename in a follow-up step?

content.text:
The command was `jj squash --into @- -R .` — that takes whatever's in the working copy (`@`) and folds it *into the parent commit* (`@-`), leaving `@` empty. So `jj st` / Zed's git panel (which shows `@` vs `@-`) goes blank. (`jj commit` is different — it *finalizes* `@` as its own new commit and starts a fresh empty `@` on top; that's the eager-commit thing from last session.) Going forward I won't run either until you say so — the rename edits below stay in `@`.

Doing the rename now. Starting with `notes/chores-10.md`:

116	## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1.1)
117	
118	The Migration B relocation, and where the cycle is re-scoped (see
119	`### Cycle re-scope`). Relocate `common::CommonArgs` — the shared
120	arg set for `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — from
121	`src/common.rs` into `src/options_flags/common_args.rs`. Inline
122	fields, no per-flag sub-leaves (an "inline-fields bundle" — see
123	[Bundle](../src/options_flags/README.md#architecture); rationale
124	in `### Why not decompose into leaves`). Pure relocation —
125	`vc-x1 chid -h` etc. byte-identical.
126	
127	- `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/common_args.rs` (`impl OptionFlagBundle`).
128	- `src/common.rs`: `CommonArgs` + `use clap::Args` dropped (the
129	  `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` / `resolve_*` / `format_*`
130	  helpers stay).
131	- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`: flatten the relocated
132	  struct; each gains the `//!` module docstring it lacked;
133	  bodies otherwise unchanged.
134	- `main.rs`: `suppress_banner` reads `a.common.no_label` for all
135	  four (back to pre-cycle).
136	
137	`0.49.0-1.2` (the ARCHITECTURE.md slim + chores-10 reorg, own
138	section below) follows; the two are kept separate at close-out —
139	no squash, no extra `0.49.0-1` commit.
140	
141	An earlier `0.49.0-1.1` decomposed `CommonArgs` into per-flag
142	leaves; backed out on review, amended in place to the relocation
143	above — see `### Why not decompose into leaves`.
144	
145	### Why not decompose into leaves
146	
147	The first take decomposed `CommonArgs` into per-flag
148	`options_flags/` leaves (`revision` / `commit_limit` /
149	`repo_label`) flattened into a `common_bundle` — mirroring
150	`init` + `provision_bundle`. Backed out:
151	
152	- **No reuse.** `init`'s leaves are reused (`--dry-run` →
153	  `clone`, etc.) — a per-flag leaf is the unit of sharing. The
154	  four here share the *whole set*; none of `revision` / `limit`
155	  / `label` / etc. is reused elsewhere, so per-flag leaves buy
156	  nothing the shared struct doesn't already give.
157	- **`value`/`id` friction.** Two single-field-`value` leaves
158	  ([README](../src/options_flags/README.md)) collide on the clap
159	  arg *id* (derived from the field name) when co-flattened —
160	  `clap_builder` panics. The fix (`#[arg(id = …)]` on each) is
161	  the "not obvious when `id` vs `value`" sharp edge —
162	  self-inflicted by decomposing leaves that didn't need to be.
163	
164	Rule of thumb:
165	
166	- Leaf — when *that flag / small group* is genuinely shared at
167	  that granularity (`--dry-run`, `--squash`).
168	- Inline-fields bundle — when a *whole arg set* is shared by N
169	  commands but its parts aren't reused.
170	- Extract a leaf later, when a second consumer is real:
171	  `CommonArgs` then `#[command(flatten)]`s the extracted type
172	  (one definition, no duplication).
173	
174	### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)
175	
176	0.49.0-0 opened a "finish Migration A" cycle for these four
177	subcommands; at 0.49.0-1 review it was expanded to the full
178	**"CommonArgs sweep"** (`chores-06` / `chores-07`'s long plan)
179	and re-ordered B-first so the Migration A ports land once. (The
180	0.49.0-0 commit/section keep the original framing; this records
181	the change.) The three parts:
182	
183	- **Migration B** (0.49.0-1, sub-steps -1.1 relocation, -1.2
184	  docs reorg):
185	  - relocate `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/common_args.rs`
186	    (inline-fields bundle, no per-flag leaves);
187	  - move all four subcommands onto it;
188	  - remove `src/common.rs`'s `CommonArgs`.
189	- **`--scope`** (0.49.0-2) — `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope`
190	  (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>`):
191	  - wires up `scope.rs`'s `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single` (both
192	    `#[allow(dead_code)]`, built in 0.42.0 for this) and
193	    `common.rs`'s `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`;
194	  - `-R` dropped, not aliased; arbitrary multi-repo
195	    (`-R . -R .claude`) not preserved — per the 0.42.0 capture
196	    (`chores-06` 0.41.0-4); `--scope=<path>` covers single-repo,
197	    default `default_scope()`;
198	  - `for_each_repo` takes an already-resolved `Vec<PathBuf>`
199	    (comma-expansion + `["."]` default move to the boundary);
200	  - `init`'s `--scope` (the separate `ScopeKind` `code,bot|por`)
201	    left alone — the "por/dual parity" todo.
202	- **Migration A** (0.49.0-3..-6) — `XxxParams` + `fn x(&Context,
203	  &XxxParams)` ports against the post-B/-scope `CommonArgs`:
204	  - introduces the shared clap-free `CommonParams` (resolved
205	    `DotSpec` + `Header` + repos);
206	  - all four take a fallible `TryFrom<&XxxArgs>` (scope→repo
207	    resolution can error);
208	  - `show` also parses `--files` → `FileLimit` at the boundary.
209	
210	Revised ladder (supersedes the 0.49.0-0 one):
211	
212	- 0.49.0-1 Migration B — relocate `CommonArgs` to
213	  `options_flags/common_args.rs`; this re-scope. Landed as two
214	  kept-separate sub-steps (no `0.49.0-1` close-out commit):
215	  - 0.49.0-1.1 the relocation + all four importers
216	  - 0.49.0-1.2 docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; start chores-10
217	- 0.49.0-2 `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope` (wire `parse_scope` /
218	  `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`; slim `for_each_repo`) —
219	  may split into `-2.N`
220	- 0.49.0-3 chid Migration A + introduce `CommonParams`
221	- 0.49.0-4 desc Migration A
222	- 0.49.0-5 list Migration A
223	- 0.49.0-6 show Migration A (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse)
224	- 0.49.0 close-out — drop suffix, todo→Done (Migration A
225	  12/12 + CommonArgs sweep), README + ARCHITECTURE.md
226	
227	## docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; chores-10 (0.49.0-1.2)
228	
229	Acting on review feedback that `ARCHITECTURE.md` had drifted
230	into "how" / transient territory — per-subcommand migration
231	status, version-by-version tables, sub-step ladders. Pull that
232	out so `ARCHITECTURE.md` is "what + some why, generic" and the
233	transient tracking lives here.
234	
235	- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — slimmed to "what + some why, generic":
236	  - kept: Overview, the two-layer section + rationale + `Naming`,
237	    a generic module map (kinds of module — no per-subcommand
238	    table, no version annotations), the Subcommand-model recipe,
239	    See-also;
240	  - the `## Migration A` / `## Migration B` sections → short
241	    "what + why" + a pointer here for live status (the
242	    per-subcommand status table, the version-by-version "done"
243	    list, the "State today" bullets are gone).
244	- `notes/chores-10.md` — new (this file): the
245	  `## Migration A / Migration B — live tracking` tables (out of
246	  `ARCHITECTURE.md`) + the `0.49.0-*` sections moved out of
247	  `chores-09.md`.
248	- `notes/chores-09.md` — the `0.49.0-*` sections removed; it now
249	  ends at `0.48.4` (all done/closed).
250	- `notes/README.md` — notes that `chores-09` is closed and
251	  `chores-10` is the active file with the tracking section.
252	- `notes/todo.md` — "Design:" link repointed to `chores-10.md`;
253	  the `0.49.0-1` sub-step ladder shows `-1.1` (the relocation)
254	  and `-1.2` (this), kept separate — see the `0.49.0-1.1`
255	  section.
256	
257	# References
258	
259	[1]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/commit/10788bd158c4 "10788bd158c4574fe5a10fab41ea32e4becc86d3"

## refactor: CommonArgs → options_flags (0.49.0-1.1)

The Migration B relocation, and where the cycle is re-scoped (see
`### Cycle re-scope`). Relocate `common::CommonArgs` — the shared
arg set for `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show` — from
`src/common.rs` into `src/options_flags/common_args.rs`. Inline
fields, no per-flag sub-leaves (an "inline-fields bundle" — see
[Bundle](../src/options_flags/README.md#architecture); rationale
in `### Why not decompose into leaves`). Pure relocation —
`vc-x1 chid -h` etc. byte-identical.

- `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/common_args.rs` (`impl OptionFlagBundle`).
- `src/common.rs`: `CommonArgs` + `use clap::Args` dropped (the
  `for_each_repo` / `collect_ids` / `resolve_*` / `format_*`
  helpers stay).
- `chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`: flatten the relocated
  struct; each gains the `//!` module docstring it lacked;
  bodies otherwise unchanged.
- `main.rs`: `suppress_banner` reads `a.common.no_label` for all
  four (back to pre-cycle).

`0.49.0-1.2` (the ARCHITECTURE.md slim + chores-10 reorg, own
section below) follows; the two are kept separate at close-out —
no squash, no extra `0.49.0-1` commit.

An earlier `0.49.0-1.1` decomposed `CommonArgs` into per-flag
leaves; backed out on review, amended in place to the relocation
above — see `### Why not decompose into leaves`.

### Why not decompose into leaves

The first take decomposed `CommonArgs` into per-flag
`options_flags/` leaves (`revision` / `commit_limit` /
`repo_label`) flattened into a `common_bundle` — mirroring
`init` + `provision_bundle`. Backed out:

- **No reuse.** `init`'s leaves are reused (`--dry-run` →
  `clone`, etc.) — a per-flag leaf is the unit of sharing. The
  four here share the *whole set*; none of `revision` / `limit`
  / `label` / etc. is reused elsewhere, so per-flag leaves buy
  nothing the shared struct doesn't already give.
- **`value`/`id` friction.** Two single-field-`value` leaves
  ([README](../src/options_flags/README.md)) collide on the clap
  arg *id* (derived from the field name) when co-flattened —
  `clap_builder` panics. The fix (`#[arg(id = …)]` on each) is
  the "not obvious when `id` vs `value`" sharp edge —
  self-inflicted by decomposing leaves that didn't need to be.

Rule of thumb:

- Leaf — when *that flag / small group* is genuinely shared at
  that granularity (`--dry-run`, `--squash`).
- Inline-fields bundle — when a *whole arg set* is shared by N
  commands but its parts aren't reused.
- Extract a leaf later, when a second consumer is real:
  `CommonArgs` then `#[command(flatten)]`s the extracted type
  (one definition, no duplication).

### Cycle re-scope (0.49.0-1)

0.49.0-0 opened a "finish Migration A" cycle for these four
subcommands; at 0.49.0-1 review it was expanded to the full
**"CommonArgs sweep"** (`chores-06` / `chores-07`'s long plan)
and re-ordered B-first so the Migration A ports land once. (The
0.49.0-0 commit/section keep the original framing; this records
the change.) The three parts:

- **Migration B** (0.49.0-1, sub-steps -1.1 relocation, -1.2
  docs reorg):
  - relocate `CommonArgs` → `options_flags/common_args.rs`
    (inline-fields bundle, no per-flag leaves);
  - move all four subcommands onto it;
  - remove `src/common.rs`'s `CommonArgs`.
- **`--scope`** (0.49.0-2) — `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope`
  (`code|bot|code,bot|<path>`):
  - wires up `scope.rs`'s `parse_scope` + `Scope::Single` (both
    `#[allow(dead_code)]`, built in 0.42.0 for this) and
    `common.rs`'s `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`;
  - `-R` dropped, not aliased; arbitrary multi-repo
    (`-R . -R .claude`) not preserved — per the 0.42.0 capture
    (`chores-06` 0.41.0-4); `--scope=<path>` covers single-repo,
    default `default_scope()`;
  - `for_each_repo` takes an already-resolved `Vec<PathBuf>`
    (comma-expansion + `["."]` default move to the boundary);
  - `init`'s `--scope` (the separate `ScopeKind` `code,bot|por`)
    left alone — the "por/dual parity" todo.
- **Migration A** (0.49.0-3..-6) — `XxxParams` + `fn x(&Context,
  &XxxParams)` ports against the post-B/-scope `CommonArgs`:
  - introduces the shared clap-free `CommonParams` (resolved
    `DotSpec` + `Header` + repos);
  - all four take a fallible `TryFrom<&XxxArgs>` (scope→repo
    resolution can error);
  - `show` also parses `--files` → `FileLimit` at the boundary.

Revised ladder (supersedes the 0.49.0-0 one):

- 0.49.0-1 Migration B — relocate `CommonArgs` to
  `options_flags/common_args.rs`; this re-scope. Landed as two
  kept-separate sub-steps (no `0.49.0-1` close-out commit):
  - 0.49.0-1.1 the relocation + all four importers
  - 0.49.0-1.2 docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; start chores-10
- 0.49.0-2 `-R`/`--repo` → `-s`/`--scope` (wire `parse_scope` /
  `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`; slim `for_each_repo`) —
  may split into `-2.N`
- 0.49.0-3 chid Migration A + introduce `CommonParams`
- 0.49.0-4 desc Migration A
- 0.49.0-5 list Migration A
- 0.49.0-6 show Migration A (`TryFrom`, `FileLimit` parse)
- 0.49.0 close-out — drop suffix, todo→Done (Migration A
  12/12 + CommonArgs sweep), README + ARCHITECTURE.md

## docs: slim ARCHITECTURE.md; chores-10 (0.49.0-1.2)

Acting on review feedback that `ARCHITECTURE.md` had drifted
into "how" / transient territory — per-subcommand migration
status, version-by-version tables, sub-step ladders. Pull that
out so `ARCHITECTURE.md` is "what + some why, generic" and the
transient tracking lives here.

- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — slimmed to "what + some why, generic":
  - kept: Overview, the two-layer section + rationale + `Naming`,
    a generic module map (kinds of module — no per-subcommand
    table, no version annotations), the Subcommand-model recipe,
    See-also;
  - the `## Migration A` / `## Migration B` sections → short
    "what + why" + a pointer here for live status (the
    per-subcommand status table, the version-by-version "done"
    list, the "State today" bullets are gone).
- `notes/chores-10.md` — new (this file): the
  `## Migration A / Migration B — live tracking` tables (out of
  `ARCHITECTURE.md`) + the `0.49.0-*` sections moved out of
  `chores-09.md`.
- `notes/chores-09.md` — the `0.49.0-*` sections removed; it now
  ends at `0.48.4` (all done/closed).
- `notes/README.md` — notes that `chores-09` is closed and
  `chores-10` is the active file with the tracking section.
- `notes/todo.md` — "Design:" link repointed to `chores-10.md`;
  the `0.49.0-1` sub-step ladder shows `-1.1` (the relocation)
  and `-1.2` (this), kept separate — see the `0.49.0-1.1`
  section.

# References

[1]: https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/commit/10788bd158c4 "10788bd158c4574fe5a10fab41ea32e4becc86d3"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Architecture

How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI layer
and the (emerging) clap-free subcommand layer. This is the
"what + some why" — generic and slow-moving. The *how* and the
per-cycle / per-subcommand status live in `notes/chores-*.md`
and `notes/todo.md`.

This is a living document; the app is under continuous
development and the repo will keep changing.

## Overview

`vc-x1` is a single binary that supports subcommands,
`vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace**
with an app repo plus a `.claude` bot-session repo. The commits
cross-reference each other via `ochid:` trailers at the bottom
of each commit message. See [`README.md`](README.md) for the
user-facing picture and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for the bot
workflow.

## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params

Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library embedding)
can call the same core without dragging clap along. The trigger
was `args.account.account`-style nested-leaf access leaking into
subcommand bodies; accessor shortcuts were rejected as hiding
the mismatch rather than fixing it.

- **CLI layer (clap-aware)**: builds `XxxParams` from the
  user-facing `XxxArgs` `#[derive(Args)]` clap struct.
  Options/flags used in more than one subcommand live as
  flattened leaves/bundles in `src/options_flags/`.
- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)**: entrypoint shape
  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams) -> Result<…>` —
  isolates the subcommand from clap and from the user.
  - `Context` — platform state shared by every subcommand.
  - `XxxParams` — that subcommand's inputs, derived from
    config / command-line args.
- **Boundary conversion**: `XxxArgs → XxxParams` via
  `impl From<&XxxArgs>` or `impl TryFrom<&XxxArgs>`, depending
  on whether the conversion can fail.

Two parameters per subcommand; the signature documents what the
subcommand depends on. `Context` is the same across all
subcommands; `XxxParams` carry the per-subcommand inputs.

Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
[Migration A](#migration-a--args--context--params)):

1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
   `XxxParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a TUI
   matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each subcommand returns
   a structured result; CLI formats it, a library writes
   nothing to stdout itself.
4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests
   a recording sink.
5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.

None of this is sacred — the whole structure is an experiment.
The current scope is deliberately narrow: a small `Context`
(two params, not one god blob); no trait-based DI yet; one
crate, not a workspace split; `src/options_flags/` leaves left
in place — only their consumers change shape. Widen any of it
when the evidence (a second front-end, a second consumer crate,
a real platform-surface divergence) calls for it — not before.

### Naming

Earlier drafts of this design used `Workspace` / `XOptions`;
the implementation (0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XxxParams`:

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the dual-repo
  project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XxxParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Module map

The *kinds* of module and what they're for — not an exhaustive
catalog (read the `//!` docstrings; `notes/chores-*.md` for
migration status):

**CLI layer:**

- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge: `Cli` (`Parser`), `Commands`
  (`Subcommand`) + the dispatch arms, `cli_with_banner()` (the
  per-subcommand `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner), `CompleteEnv` wiring,
  and `bm_track` (the permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic on every command's entry/exit).
- `src/options_flags/` — reusable `#[derive(Args)]` *leaves*
  (one flag, or a small fixed pair) and *bundles*
  (`#[command(flatten)]` of N leaves) shared across
  subcommands, so a flag is defined (parser, help, completer)
  exactly once. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
- `src/common.rs` — helpers the read-only commit-query
  subcommands (`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`) share:
  `for_each_repo`, `collect_ids`, `resolve_revset`, the
  `DotSpec` / `..`-notation parsing, `Header` /
  `resolve_header`, the `format_*` printers, and
  `find_workspace_root` / `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`.

**Subcommand-layer scaffolding:**

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`, the shared platform handle
  (today: the loaded `UserConfig` + the `--log` path; built
  once at startup via `Context::load(log)`).
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for explicit
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

**Subcommand modules** — `src/<x>.rs` holds the subcommand's
`XxxArgs` (`#[derive(Args)]`, composing `options_flags/` leaves
where one exists) and the entrypoint `pub fn x(...)`. Modules:
`chid`, `desc` (+ `desc_helpers`), `list`, `show`,
`validate_desc`, `fix_desc`, `clone`, `init` (+ `init/params`),
`symlink`, `sync`, `finalize`, `push` (a resumable state
machine). Which ones have ported to `(ctx, params)` (Migration
A) and which compose `options_flags/` leaves (Migration B):
`notes/chores-*.md` + `notes/todo.md`.

**Support:**

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures and tempdir resolution. Subcommand
  modules also carry their own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and
  `integration_tests` for `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XxxArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XxxArgs)` to `Commands` in `src/main.rs`
   with a `///` summary (and `#[command(long_about = …)]` for
   the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.

## Migration A — args → Context + Params

The ongoing port of each subcommand's
`pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` to
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`, adding an
`XxxParams` flat struct + a `From` (or `TryFrom`, if the
conversion is fallible) at the binary edge. `init` (0.44.0) is
the worked example. Out of scope for the ports until a real
consumer surfaces: typed errors,
returned-outcomes-vs-`println!`, `ProgressSink`, `Context`
fields beyond `UserConfig` + `--log`. Per-subcommand status:
`notes/chores-*.md` (the "Migration A / Migration B — live
tracking" section).

## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification; `notes/chores-*.md` for
per-subcommand status.

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
  per-subcommand usage.
- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  — leaf/bundle patterns for Migration B.
- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list, including
  both migrations.
- `notes/chores-*.md` — per-cycle design captures and the
  Migration A/B live-tracking tables (currently
  [`chores-10.md`](notes/chores-10.md); `chores-09.md` and
  earlier are closed).

# Architecture

How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI layer
and the (emerging) clap-free subcommand layer. This is the
"what + some why" — generic and slow-moving. The *how* and the
per-cycle / per-subcommand status live in `notes/chores-*.md`
and `notes/todo.md`.

This is a living document; the app is under continuous
development and the repo will keep changing.

## Overview

`vc-x1` is a single binary that supports subcommands,
`vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace**
with an app repo plus a `.claude` bot-session repo. The commits
cross-reference each other via `ochid:` trailers at the bottom
of each commit message. See [`README.md`](README.md) for the
user-facing picture and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for the bot
workflow.

## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params

Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library embedding)
can call the same core without dragging clap along. The trigger
was `args.account.account`-style nested-leaf access leaking into
subcommand bodies; accessor shortcuts were rejected as hiding
the mismatch rather than fixing it.

- **CLI layer (clap-aware)**: builds `XxxParams` from the
  user-facing `XxxArgs` `#[derive(Args)]` clap struct.
  Options/flags used in more than one subcommand live as
  flattened leaves/bundles in `src/options_flags/`.
- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)**: entrypoint shape
  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams) -> Result<…>` —
  isolates the subcommand from clap and from the user.
  - `Context` — platform state shared by every subcommand.
  - `XxxParams` — that subcommand's inputs, derived from
    config / command-line args.
- **Boundary conversion**: `XxxArgs → XxxParams` via
  `impl From<&XxxArgs>` or `impl TryFrom<&XxxArgs>`, depending
  on whether the conversion can fail.

Two parameters per subcommand; the signature documents what the
subcommand depends on. `Context` is the same across all
subcommands; `XxxParams` carry the per-subcommand inputs.

Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
[the Context+Params port](#args--context--params)):

1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
   `XxxParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a TUI
   matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each subcommand returns
   a structured result; CLI formats it, a library writes
   nothing to stdout itself.
4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests
   a recording sink.
5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.

None of this is sacred — the whole structure is an experiment.
The current scope is deliberately narrow: a small `Context`
(two params, not one god blob); no trait-based DI yet; one
crate, not a workspace split; `src/options_flags/` leaves left
in place — only their consumers change shape. Widen any of it
when the evidence (a second front-end, a second consumer crate,
a real platform-surface divergence) calls for it — not before.

### Naming

Earlier drafts of this design used `Workspace` / `XOptions`;
the implementation (0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XxxParams`:

- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the dual-repo
  project root (`find_workspace_root`).
- `XOptions` → `XxxParams` — avoids visual collision with
  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.

## Module map

The *kinds* of module and what they're for — not an exhaustive
catalog (read the `//!` docstrings; `notes/chores-*.md` for
migration status):

**CLI layer:**

- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge: `Cli` (`Parser`), `Commands`
  (`Subcommand`) + the dispatch arms, `cli_with_banner()` (the
  per-subcommand `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner), `CompleteEnv` wiring,
  and `bm_track` (the permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
  diagnostic on every command's entry/exit).
- `src/options_flags/` — reusable `#[derive(Args)]` *leaves*
  (one flag, or a small fixed pair) and *bundles*
  (`#[command(flatten)]` of N leaves) shared across
  subcommands, so a flag is defined (parser, help, completer)
  exactly once. See
  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
- `src/common.rs` — helpers the read-only commit-query
  subcommands (`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`) share:
  `for_each_repo`, `collect_ids`, `resolve_revset`, the
  `DotSpec` / `..`-notation parsing, `Header` /
  `resolve_header`, the `format_*` printers, and
  `find_workspace_root` / `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos`.

**Subcommand-layer scaffolding:**

- `src/context.rs` — `Context`, the shared platform handle
  (today: the loaded `UserConfig` + the `--log` path; built
  once at startup via `Context::load(log)`).
- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
- `src/scope.rs` — `Scope` (`Roles(Vec<Side>)` for the
  dual-repo `code`/`bot` roles, `Single(PathBuf)` for explicit
  single-repo mode) and `parse_scope`.

**Subcommand modules** — `src/<x>.rs` holds the subcommand's
`XxxArgs` (`#[derive(Args)]`, composing `options_flags/` leaves
where one exists) and the entrypoint `pub fn x(...)`. Modules:
`chid`, `desc` (+ `desc_helpers`), `list`, `show`,
`validate_desc`, `fix_desc`, `clone`, `init` (+ `init/params`),
`symlink`, `sync`, `finalize`, `push` (a resumable state
machine). Which ones have ported to `(ctx, params)` (Migration
A) and which compose `options_flags/` leaves (Migration B):
`notes/chores-*.md` + `notes/todo.md`.

**Support:**

- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures and tempdir resolution. Subcommand
  modules also carry their own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and
  `integration_tests` for `sync` / `push`).

## Subcommand model

Adding a subcommand `x`:

1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XxxArgs`
   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
2. Add a variant `X(x::XxxArgs)` to `Commands` in `src/main.rs`
   with a `///` summary (and `#[command(long_about = …)]` for
   the long form).
3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
   prose).
5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
   and stays at this layer.

## Migration A — args → Context + Params

The ongoing port of each subcommand's
`pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` to
`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`, adding an
`XxxParams` flat struct + a `From` (or `TryFrom`, if the
conversion is fallible) at the binary edge. `init` (0.44.0) is
the worked example. Out of scope for the ports until a real
consumer surfaces: typed errors,
returned-outcomes-vs-`println!`, `ProgressSink`, `Context`
fields beyond `UserConfig` + `--log`. Per-subcommand status:
`notes/chores-*.md` (the "Migration A / Migration B — live
tracking" section).

## Migration B — per-subcommand flags → `src/options_flags/`

Independently of Migration A, per-subcommand inline `#[arg]`
fields are being lifted into reusable leaves/bundles under
`src/options_flags/` so a flag is defined (parser, help,
completer) exactly once. See
[`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
for the leaf / bundle / Pattern-A mechanics and the
Flag-vs-Option classification; `notes/chores-*.md` for
per-subcommand status.

## See also

- [`README.md`](README.md) — user-facing overview and
  per-subcommand usage.
- [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) — bot workflow, versioning,
  commit/push conventions, code conventions.
- [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md)
  — leaf/bundle patterns for Migration B.
- [`notes/todo.md`](notes/todo.md) — live task list, including
  both migrations.
- `notes/chores-*.md` — per-cycle design captures and the
  Migration A/B live-tracking tables (currently
  [`chores-10.md`](notes/chores-10.md); `chores-09.md` and
  earlier are closed).
